subject: In case you missed: He got 8 Interview & 2 offer doing this
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here,

Special Highlight

One thing I don’t talk about enough is the community behind the
Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9n87dxflhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/48hwkv9vhehmw8z6p8sx/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
).

​

It’s not a course people binge and disappear from.

​

It’s 5,200+ international students and professionals in the U.S.
who are all in the same phase.

​
Applying. Interviewing. Getting rejected. Trying again.

​

Inside, people:
- Share real timelines, not LinkedIn highlight reels
- Get honest resume feedback, not generic advice
- Show up to focus sessions when motivation is low
- Join office hours when they feel stuck or unsure

​

If you’re at a point where you want structure, clarity, and the
right environment around you, you can join our community.

-->Join The Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9n87dxflhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/48hwkv9vhehmw8z6p8sx/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
)
Join The Accelerator ( https://link.yudij.com/newsletteremail
)Just putting it out there for the ones who need it.

​
Every week, I hear stories from students who feel invisible in
their job search.
They’ve done everything right. Built the resume, applied
everywhere, waited and still, silence.

That’s where Yogeshwar (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9n87dxflhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/e0hph7h7mo9qlgf8/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj11eXpwWTR1N3dPTQ==
) was when I met him.
A robotics master’s student who came to the U.S. in 2024,
hopeful but stuck.

For months, nothing worked.
No callbacks. No interviews. Just waiting.

And he said:

“I applied to maybe 100 - 200 jobs… nothing worked. Not even one
interview call.”
But instead of giving up, he did something most people wouldn’t.
He stopped waiting for opportunities and started creating them.

Within months, he landed eight interviews and two job offers.​
Not from job boards.
But from birthday parties, conferences, and simple human
connection.

The 3 moments that changed his story

1. The birthday party conversation
----------------------------------

"Everyone was having fun, but I personally went to each and every
person and just spoke.
‘What are you applying for? What are you doing? Do you know
anyone who’s hiring?'"
That one conversation turned into a referral.
That referral turned into the internship he’s doing today.

Sometimes, opportunity doesn’t come from LinkedIn.
It comes from courage.

2. The conference hack
----------------------

“I went to a robotics summit in Boston.
I emailed the organizers saying I’m a student who wants to
promote your event.
They gave me a free pass worth $560.”
Once there, he realized the lines to meet recruiters were
endless.
So he did something most students wouldn’t think to do:

“I made a small visiting card. My name, my LinkedIn QR code, and
one line about me.
I gave it to every recruiter I met. One of them kept it in his
wallet, and two days later, I got a call.”

Visitor Card
That’s not luck.
That’s intentional creativity.

3. The cold message that opened doors
-------------------------------------

“Hi, I’m capable of building robots and mechanical design to
motion control and autonomy.
I’m ready to relocate and can share my resume and references.
Your referral would mean a lot to me and can define my career
too.”
It wasn’t fancy.
It was real.​
And from that, he got 5 interviews and two offers.
Just by reaching out like a human.

​
The mindset behind it all
-------------------------

When I asked him what made the difference, he said something I
wish every student could hear:

“Only if you be a little different than what others do, you’ll
get noticed.
Otherwise, it’s very difficult in this market.”
He didn’t do more.
He did what mattered.

In our Job Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9n87dxflhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/48hwkv9vhehmw8z6p8sx/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), I tell students the same thing:
It’s not about sending 200 applications.
It’s about building 20 meaningful connections.

​
What Yogeshwar reminded me
--------------------------

“When one door closes, a window opens. Don’t focus on the closed
door, look for the window.
Keep working hard. Trust in God’s plan. It’s not your time today,
but soon enough, it will be.”
He didn’t stare at what didn’t work.
He leaned into what could.

That’s what turns effort into momentum.
And waiting into preparation.

If you’re in that quiet season where nothing seems to move - keep
building.
Keep showing up.
Your window might be closer than you think.

​
Watch the full episode here → He Got 8 Interviews & 2 Job Offers
from This One Networking Hack (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9n87dxflhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/e0hph7h7mo9qlgf8/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj11eXpwWTR1N3dPTQ==
)​

-Yudi J

The HireReady Newsletter Vol. 79:

Stop Waiting, Start Dictating: How to Manufacture Urgency and Speed Up Your Hire

Tired of the endless waiting game after a great interview? The standard advice to "be patient" and "follow up politely" is exactly why you are still waiting. It's time to stop acting like a hopeful guest at a party and start behaving like the sought-after talent they can't afford to lose. This newsletter edition is all about ethically forcing the decision-makers' hands and creating a sense of urgency that gets you the offer, faster.

🛠️ The job search hacks 🛠️

🎯 Weaponize Your Match Rate to Create Scarcity 🎯

Instead of just being a "good fit," you can present yourself as a "perfect match," backed by the very data they use to filter candidates. This shifts the dynamic from you hoping you're what they want to you showing them objective proof that you are, creating an immediate fear of missing out on the ideal candidate.

When you can say, "My qualifications are an X% match for this role's requirements," you've moved beyond subjective opinion. You’re signaling that you understand their process on a deeper level and that you represent a rare, low-risk hire.

Next Steps:

- Identify a top-choice job where you know you're a strong candidate.
- Use the Jobscan Resume Scanner to tailor your resume to the job description until you achieve a high match rate (aim for 80% or higher).
- Save a PDF of the high-scoring report.
- In your follow-up email after the interview, attach your resume and casually mention: "As a testament to my strong alignment with this role, I’ve attached my resume which scores a 92% match against the job description. I’m confident I have the precise skills you’re looking for."

Free Tool: Prove Your Resume is a Perfect Match
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VVPX576xlQCgW87cS-97xY70cV1-ywc5JYk-4N3X6VHv3gP0xW7lCdLW6lZ3nHN40JBHXxD2MNW8QCbBl2qvjGKW78rTc13Pv39cW52kv8h5JB4VRW29bC_G2w6X54W59Dwkb63TJmcMBpdQwc4CP-W8t8VDr6s75RsW2BfDKl1KLWk5W6XyX6H7T_qDQW4cYTxS2Ng-nzW5rKN_S2MsfNCW3_1Bjb3-G95dW7g84QN5NmLBDW7w0sT84-Q6MfW3M4lfF7Hhyh3W3_ytKd43xgRlMX4TzCpjd3WW2-l-My54G-vZW8VF_TZ4phqNBW6N_rqF3cKzZdW66kGJQ4Mb969W752zBb47kVbkW7_d4g067GWDkf3Pv_mv04 )

🧭 Pre-Frame Your "Competitive Landscape" 🧭

Most people wait until they have a competing offer to create urgency. That’s reactive. The power move is to proactively signal that you are a candidate in high demand before an offer is even on the table.

During the interview, when they ask about your job search, you can frame your situation honestly but strategically. Instead of saying, "I'm talking to a few other places," which sounds generic, try something more specific like, "I'm in the final interview stages with two other SaaS companies, but this role is particularly exciting to me because of X." This communicates that the window of opportunity to hire you is closing, encouraging them to move faster if they're serious. (Do not lie. This tip is about reframing your answers to create demand.)

Next Steps:

- Before your next interview, get clear on your timeline and the status of your other applications.
- When the interviewer asks, "Where are you in your job search?" or "Do you have any questions for us?," use this as your opening.
- Frame your answer around a timeline. For example: "I have a final-round interview with another company next week, which is why I'm particularly interested in understanding your timeline for this role."
- Always bring it back to your genuine interest in their company to show you're not just playing the field.

Article: Prepare Your Interview Talking Point
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VVPX576xlQCgW87cS-97xY70cV1-ywc5JYk-4N3X6VHP3gP0xW7Y8-PT6lZ3lRW62KVFY2p4R8kW4YZCqP3xhTBtW1fP5tl27r73QW7gz6K51ZbrRSVlWkgF2XYxzWW4dKfRz3y8wslW7Yv4k43Gd87cW7w521-4wP5nFN6j55Q9FTjjLW5rNYwy88X9DnN6nVVqBrK7QxW9bm5lH9hF-HPMBpn2XXpysRW46CNsp466pDnN6rjfrQ2Hh6pVqCmN968tSNSW1TzVT86tNLYzW14cL0z31rkrDN2d5Wc75H8sJV1FffP5SBs2dW8K4GrN2CnjGpW5K5MTC6sjW5sW22X3wj9fHjc-W9cyFW-1mLvjGW2G3g7N3xSq4BW2pZcqP5WK_Ncf70fFP404 )

📅 The "First 30 Days" Follow-Up 📅

Your post-interview "thank you" note is the most underrated tool for manufacturing urgency. While everyone else is sending a generic, "Thanks for your time," you're going to send a micro-proposal that shows you're already thinking like a top-performing employee. This makes you seem less like a candidate and more like a colleague they haven't hired yet.

Instead of a simple thank-you email, draft a brief, one-page document outlining what your first 30 days in the role would look like. Reference specific challenges or opportunities discussed in the interview. For example, "In our conversation, we discussed the challenge of streamlining the new client onboarding process. In my first 30 days, I would focus on A, B, and C to address this." This demonstrates immediate value and creates a powerful sense of loss if they choose someone else.

Next Steps:

- Immediately after your interview, jot down 2-3 key problems or priorities that were discussed.
- Draft a concise, 1-2 paragraph "First 30 Days" plan that addresses one of those points with clear, actionable ideas.
- Send your thank-you email within 24 hours, and attach this plan as a PDF.
- In the email, write something like, "I was so energized by our conversation about [the challenge] that I put together a few initial thoughts on how I'd approach it in my first month. I've attached them for your consideration."

Guide: Elevate Your Follow-Up Game
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VVPX576xlQCgW87cS-97xY70cV1-ywc5JYk-4N3X6VHP3gP0xW7Y8-PT6lZ3mfW8VtnV77sm1TTW8FQT3R59sV1fW24rNt085rwD9W7_VmWx5r5XsJW4G4p8w9lYX9TW8nL5JT7tRGQqW3sR8rX61SRPdW6rNXYw6lbpltW2N6-DM8F-WWBW46yVmQ8ZQsnFW25yPdM4hMw3fW8FbnRb3p82sCW41phKy5vsgfVW7Qw97k228n6tW2cD2vF6yQCrnN4VjXSFW8zPTW8KQ6wk2_DLpXW1x57_c1GpPNxN6Q75RCxXd5NVQJVB36W-8cJMP3q5YhDN7nW8QNTl13fTB1VW62hjr02vF6ZdW6Y7JGw68cx7VW47Rpmj2Prb-pW6nnD0r79_J7RdmvGNg04 )

🔗 The Proactive LinkedIn "Warm-Up" 🔗

Why wait for an interview to make a first impression? You can create a sense of familiarity and urgency before you even apply by "warming up" your target company on LinkedIn. The goal is to be a known and respected entity by the time your application lands, making it feel less like a cold submission and more like the next logical step in a budding professional relationship.

Weeks before you apply, optimize your LinkedIn profile with keywords and phrases from your target company's job descriptions. Then, start strategically engaging. Follow the company, like and comment thoughtfully on their posts, and connect with people on the team you'd be working with. By the time you apply and your name comes up, the hiring manager will have already seen you in their orbit, creating a subtle sense of "this person is already one of us" that can accelerate their interest.

Next Steps:

- Identify 5-10 target companies you'd love to work for.
- Use Jobscan’s LinkedIn Optimization tool to ensure your profile is aligned with the roles at those companies.
- For the next two weeks, dedicate 15 minutes a day to engaging with content from those companies and their employees. Leave insightful comments, not just "great post!"
- When you're ready to apply, you can even mention your admiration for a recent company project or post in your cover letter.

Tool: Optimize Your Profile for Attraction
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VVPX576xlQCgW87cS-97xY70cV1-ywc5JYk-4N3X6VHv3gP0xW7lCdLW6lZ3pLW6-nSRg6KFrR2W6RBkFP8D7VVnW8sztcp7j-XKsW2Hn5YC8W7MYHW9dq8263K7n6TW9jxL8x1h-r0zVkN0Bz3hfzJfW4gyk6t6L0qkNW39VT552Dwx9_W2jXt5p9hhLjxW879rDM48HHg5W3NQg0w3f2CFWW9hQmhQ4ZcvH9W7N4Z4_1ysrp_W24QMCy5Q8x8vW3jmjJw5yW5pSW8B19Lw36Hjw8W5wxrsK6M0DDDW3R45q482H5cvW3j469-4ZNk8TW1tDZxK4jMXsJW6znDtQ5rHMxmW6mVlbk7ySmg2VG5b6h8cPB9Lf4cSfpq04 )

📈Announce Your "Value Spike"📈

A key to creating urgency is signaling that your market value has just increased. Instead of just listing your skills, you can create a narrative that you have recently acquired a new, highly relevant skill set that you are eager to deploy.

Have you just finished a new certification? Completed a major project? Mastered a new software? Weave this into your interview conversation or follow-up. For example: "I just completed my certification in [in-demand skill], and I'm incredibly excited to apply these new techniques to a challenge like [company's problem]." This tells them that your skills are current and that other companies are likely to take notice of your newly upgraded qualifications.

Next Steps:

- Identify a recently completed project, certification, or course that is highly relevant to the job.
- Quantify the result if you can (e.g., "the project I just wrapped up increased lead generation by 15%").
- Practice weaving this "value spike" into your interview answers, especially when asked about your skills or recent work.
- Add the new certification or skill prominently to your resume and LinkedIn profile to reinforce the message.

Article: How to Showcase Your Newest Skills
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VVPX576xlQCgW87cS-97xY70cV1-ywc5JYk-4N3X6VJ43gP0xW8wLKSR6lZ3lLW27bgpC8t679YW6fCvVr8plTT8Vkr5tt4PN1hpW2DCN1R1jbW8SW7Gmfzn5ZLQhSVh8JtK7lCfCFW4DNYL088L55NW4vvHjb5wgYyqW5CNjFj2djG9tW7pK59B24BbxdW2r2R3L7FzgPJW53CwKC28rkcHW2tHWgK99jHhhW2X5-Kp48Xkw7W6cc-bN8PZP9TW5nBs8G7d4_jQW2NXgc65k923pW7X_wk320r7NkW4sqwvb6dkc2WW8qcLX96CJRfpW3Br7vR2q1GbDW6FNdWD12vbBpW7gxjVk8jqT50W4MSlXV6H5LWFW1sT65G495_bbN2TpN8m9VV6hW68B9Zw6Wyf2YW7jdf6f5pW38Lf7kqXVY04 )

The job search doesn't have to be a passive waiting game. You have more power than you think to influence the pace and outcome. Stop waiting for the phone to ring and start giving them a reason to pick it up. Your next move? Choose one hack from this newsletter and put it into action today. Go on, make them hurry.

To your successful search,

James Hu,

Jobscan Founder and CEO

subject: Resend Agent Skills, new Community Program, and New Features
email_text:
Hey, it's Zeno Rocha.

Can you believe January is almost over? We've accelerated into the new year, shipping new features, bug bashing, and doubling down on stability and reliability.

Here are some of the highlights...





EMPOWER YOUR AI AGENTS

https://resend.com/blog/introducing-email-skills

Developers and creators are building increasingly more with AI agents. We released three new skills to give your AI the expertise needed to land in the inbox, add email to your app, and style your email templates.

→ Read the announcement https://resend.com/blog/introducing-email-skills





ANNOUNCING RESEND INSIDERS

https://resend.com/insiders

We're launching a brand new community program, designed for creators to share their content, help us improve the product, and get exclusive perks.

→ Apply to Insiders https://resend.com/insiders





CHANGE MULTIPLE ITEMS AT ONCE

https://resend.com/changelog/bulk-actions

Bulk actions are now available throughout the Dashboard and offer bulk actions for 40, 80, or 120 items at a time.

→ Read the changelog https://resend.com/changelog/bulk-actions





OWN YOUR DATA


https://youtu.be/-RzkHd9s8HM?si=O7HpssJrd-xZTnmA

If you're not already storing your own data, we've created and released an open-source project, Webhooks Ingester https://resend.com/docs/webhooks/ingester, which you can set up in a few clicks to store all your own data in your own database using Resend webhooks.

→ Read about Webhooks Ingester https://resend.com/changelog/webhooks-ingester





NEW SUPPRESSED STATUS

https://resend.com/changelog/email-suppression-visibility

A suppression happens when Resend intentionally prevents an email from being sent to a recipient. This occurs when deliveries to that address have bounced or been marked as spam. We introduced a new suppressed status to give you clearer visibility into your email performance and help you respond with confidence.

→ Read the changelog https://resend.com/changelog/email-suppression-visibility





NATURAL WARMUP WINS

https://resend.com/blog/do-you-need-a-warmup-service

Warming up a domain—the process of gradually establishing trust with inbox providers—is important for consistently landing in the inbox. Is it better to warm up your own domain, or should you pay for a warmup service?

→ Read the post https://resend.com/blog/do-you-need-a-warmup-service





JOIN US IN UTAH

https://luma.com/ia00vywi

Join us next week, Thursday, February 5th, in Lehi, Utah. Kent C Dodds and Dave Kiss are presenting talks on AI & dev tooling. Meet the team and get a chance to win a Mac mini.

→ Register for Free https://luma.com/ia00vywi




Happy sending,
Zeno Rocha

subject: What if the best job leads aren’t online?
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here,

Special Highlight

One thing I don’t talk about enough is the community behind the
Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/v8uq2vdr74hrhvmqqz4sghv3xwmllh9hro8nx/p8hpge2es9h4rmoqpkfq/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
).

​

It’s not a course people binge and disappear from.

​

It’s 5,200+ international students and professionals in the U.S.
who are all in the same phase.

​
Applying. Interviewing. Getting rejected. Trying again.

​

Inside, people:
- Share real timelines, not LinkedIn highlight reels
- Get honest resume feedback, not generic advice
- Show up to focus sessions when motivation is low
- Join office hours when they feel stuck or unsure

​

If you’re at a point where you want structure, clarity, and the
right environment around you, you can join our community.

-->Join The Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/v8uq2vdr74hrhvmqqz4sghv3xwmllh9hro8nx/p8hpge2es9h4rmoqpkfq/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
)
Join The Accelerator ( https://link.yudij.com/newsletteremail
)Just putting it out there for the ones who need it.

​
Every week, I hear stories from students who feel invisible in
their job search.
They’ve done everything right. Built the resume, applied
everywhere, waited and still, silence.

That’s where Yogeshwar (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/v8uq2vdr74hrhvmqqz4sghv3xwmllh9hro8nx/48hvhehmwv8x79cx/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj11eXpwWTR1N3dPTQ==
) was when I met him.
A robotics master’s student who came to the U.S. in 2024,
hopeful but stuck.

For months, nothing worked.
No callbacks. No interviews. Just waiting.

And he said:

“I applied to maybe 100 - 200 jobs… nothing worked. Not even one
interview call.”
But instead of giving up, he did something most people wouldn’t.
He stopped waiting for opportunities and started creating them.

Within months, he landed eight interviews and two job offers.​
Not from job boards.
But from birthday parties, conferences, and simple human
connection.

The 3 moments that changed his story

1. The birthday party conversation
----------------------------------

"Everyone was having fun, but I personally went to each and every
person and just spoke.
‘What are you applying for? What are you doing? Do you know
anyone who’s hiring?'"
That one conversation turned into a referral.
That referral turned into the internship he’s doing today.

Sometimes, opportunity doesn’t come from LinkedIn.
It comes from courage.

2. The conference hack
----------------------

“I went to a robotics summit in Boston.
I emailed the organizers saying I’m a student who wants to
promote your event.
They gave me a free pass worth $560.”
Once there, he realized the lines to meet recruiters were
endless.
So he did something most students wouldn’t think to do:

“I made a small visiting card. My name, my LinkedIn QR code, and
one line about me.
I gave it to every recruiter I met. One of them kept it in his
wallet, and two days later, I got a call.”

Visitor Card
That’s not luck.
That’s intentional creativity.

3. The cold message that opened doors
-------------------------------------

“Hi, I’m capable of building robots and mechanical design to
motion control and autonomy.
I’m ready to relocate and can share my resume and references.
Your referral would mean a lot to me and can define my career
too.”
It wasn’t fancy.
It was real.​
And from that, he got 5 interviews and two offers.
Just by reaching out like a human.

​
The mindset behind it all
-------------------------

When I asked him what made the difference, he said something I
wish every student could hear:

“Only if you be a little different than what others do, you’ll
get noticed.
Otherwise, it’s very difficult in this market.”
He didn’t do more.
He did what mattered.

In our Job Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/v8uq2vdr74hrhvmqqz4sghv3xwmllh9hro8nx/p8hpge2es9h4rmoqpkfq/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), I tell students the same thing:
It’s not about sending 200 applications.
It’s about building 20 meaningful connections.

​
What Yogeshwar reminded me
--------------------------

“When one door closes, a window opens. Don’t focus on the closed
door, look for the window.
Keep working hard. Trust in God’s plan. It’s not your time today,
but soon enough, it will be.”
He didn’t stare at what didn’t work.
He leaned into what could.

That’s what turns effort into momentum.
And waiting into preparation.

If you’re in that quiet season where nothing seems to move - keep
building.
Keep showing up.
Your window might be closer than you think.

​
Watch the full episode here → He Got 8 Interviews & 2 Job Offers
from This One Networking Hack (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/v8uq2vdr74hrhvmqqz4sghv3xwmllh9hro8nx/48hvhehmwv8x79cx/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj11eXpwWTR1N3dPTQ==
)​

-Yudi J

 talent.




Company Spotlight: General Assembly



In this week's ‘Why Work Here’ series, we chat with Daniele Grassi 
<https://www.linkedin.com/in/dgrassii/>, CTO & Co-Founder, to discover what 
makesGeneral Assembly <https://generalassemb.ly/> a great place to work. 



General Assembly is a global leader in practical AI skills training, preparing 
businesses and individuals for the workforce of the future.


Key Insights:



For job seekers - Be at the center of the AI transformation by helping 
companies and governments worldwide upskill their workforce during one of the 
biggest shifts in how we work.



For recruiters - Prioritize hiring people who thrive on challenges and are 
passionate about learning and impact, especially as you scale globally across 
diverse time zones and markets.



For founders: When market disruption hits, listen to inbound demand from 
unexpected customers—it can reveal how to expand your total addressable market 
from a niche to everyone. <https://wellfound.com/company/halo-dot-science>


They're hiring! See 2 open roles 
<https://wellfound.com/company/general-assembly>. 



Watch the full interview on YouTube 
<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ELB20KRMtk4> or Spotify. 
<https://open.spotify.com/episode/3hWa9werEqTqKRxoONCGxI?si=c62d8cc9d14b462b> 






 <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ELB20KRMtk4>

Startups Hiring Now  <https://wellfound.com/company/oklo>



Loop

Delivery intelligence platform for modern food brands.

See 49 open roles. <https://wellfound.com/company/loop-ai-xyz>



Pleo

Centralise your business spending for complete financial efficiency and 
balance the books effortlessly with Pleo.

See 37 open roles <https://wellfound.com/company/pleo>



GlossGenius

The Brains Behind Beauty

See 30 open roles. <https://wellfound.com/company/glossgenius>



Dorsia

Tech-powered platform built to transform the hospitality industry

See 16 open roles. <https://wellfound.com/company/dorsia_>




Job Search Tip: Don't wait for a referral. Apply within 24-48 hours.



Applications flood in immediately when roles go live. While you're chasing 
referrals, someone else is already in the recruiter screen.



Lachlan Ma <https://www.linkedin.com/in/lachlan-ma/> from Affirm 
<https://wellfound.com/company/affirm> (ex-TikTok) sees it constantly: 
candidates spend a week networking while the hiring manager falls in love with 
someone who applied on day one.



Companies hire on a rolling basis. The first great candidate often gets the 
offer.



Set a deadline: chase referrals for 24-48 hours max, then apply anyway. You 
can sort job boards by "most recent" to catch roles while the applicant pool is 
still manageable. Plus, you can always add a referral after your application is 
already in.


 Watch the full ‘Ask a Recruiter’ episode with Lachlan Ma from Affirm on 
Wellfoundhere. <https://youtu.be/jysn52y1vmM>






 <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PYkclCQuKY4>





What We're Reading



Trending perk: Cash out early (and often)

Clay 
<https://www.nytimes.com/2026/01/28/business/dealbook/clay-start-up-tender-offers.html>
 is part of a growing trend of startups offering workers options to sell stock 
before going public. The company just made itssecond offer 
<https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260128514638/en/Clay-Announces-Second-Employee-Tender-Offer-in-Nine-Months-at-a-%245B-Valuation>
 in 9 months.



VCs heart Cali.

California startups 
<https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/california-record-startup-funding-ai-chips-2025/>
 raked in a record-breaking 63% of U.S. funding last year—5X more than 
runner-up New York—thanks to behemoths like OpenAI and Anthropic.



AI heats Ireland

Dublin’s Technical University 
<https://www.cnbc.com/2026/01/27/data-centers-ai-district-heating-aws-amazon-ireland.html>
 uses waste heat from AWS data center to warm its campus.



Brain-cpu without the implant

Chinese startup Gestala 
<https://www.wired.com/story/this-chinese-startup-wants-to-build-a-new-brain-computer-interface-no-implant-required-gestalta/>
 is developing a noninvasive brain-computer interface using ultrasound 
technology that requires no implanted device.

subject: A/c xx9905: Your Lifetime Free Credit Card is ready!
NetAcad Student Newsletter | January 2026
email_text:
Hello Nandini,
 

The new experience at NetAcad.com is live! We're excited to see that many classes are already underway. This milestone marks a significant step in our mission to power an inclusive future for all. In our ongoing spirit of innovation, we will continue to add more teaching and learning features, courses, and resources.


To our partners and educators: thank you for your continued support, patience, and trust in Cisco Networking Academy. We highly value your collaboration and look forward to the positive impact we will continue to make together in our communities around the world.


Scroll down for tips on the new NetAcad.com platform, new courses, orientation learning collections, and more. We are also thrilled to unveil a whole new page dedicated to careers promoting current job opportunities with new employment partners like Indeed, the #1 job search platform (currently U.S. only).


And with Cybersecurity Awareness Month fast-approaching, you can get ready by checking out the first in our 10-part virtual series (see the Events section)! We also have a campaign 
Sourcebook forthcoming, so be on the lookout for this awesome guide that will help you and your instructors promote opportunities and attract even more students. Finally, don't forget to remind your instructors to encourage their students to check out the new Find yourself in the future and Women Rock-IT special events coming soon.
First report released
The AI-Enabled ICT Workforce Consortium - led by Cisco with 8 leading tech companies plus key advisors - releases first report. Looks at effects of AI on top 50 ICT jobs, with actionable training recommendations to enable an AI-powered workforce.
Update to legal agreements - August 26
Stay tuned for updates to The Cisco Networking Academy membership guide, Terms and Conditions, privacy documents, and Packet Tracer Distribution Terms on August 26.
End-of-life: Continuing Education (CE) credits
Cisco Networking Academy is exiting the Cisco Continuing Education (CE) Credits program. Learn more <http://nsmail.netacad.com/MDU5LVZGWi04MzQAAAGfpWq6mCSB4qqbiA29wjFx4sfUaA7PdUhO1TUiINtuZ_hJCohShdXoz2hD60pin2Kr7w9oqIM=>
Journey to Convergence
Visit Convergence Hub
Keep track of everything to do with Convergence of SkillsForAll.com and NetAcad.com on the Convergence Hub.
FINAL CHECKLIST
Convergence this month!
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet
1. Downtime: 72 hours
Both NetAcad.com and SkillsForAll.com will be down for 72 hours. Start and end times depend on your location.

Take note <http://nsmail.netacad.com/MDU5LVZGWi04MzQAAAGfpWq6mGJ8Jou89ftInY48kv5EphSq5SzC5CiiXZA-pPIEE1Hb_Ko_OTHVZ1VQ0OB5RrpOmlQ=>
2. Maintain downtime access
to Cisco Packet Tracer during planned downtime, especially for the 72 hours both sites will be down for convergence.

Prepare <http://nsmail.netacad.com/MDU5LVZGWi04MzQAAAGfpWq6mOHJ7r8IVczsU8YDUgJxADfyMAougHEAy-Bkjxq5yB5jRaXeybnxUdLleiu1LByiZes=>
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet
3. Quick Start Guide
Download the new NetAcad.com Quick Start Guide for Instructors.
Download <http://nsmail.netacad.com/MDU5LVZGWi04MzQAAAGfpWq6mErIjhtwoi6ATFMHHSXsl0PZOUwLt1V8HeBmh0A6ppIXrkkFi4XE3usf-5o726v0exw=>
4. Understand simplified roles
See how the number of roles are simplifying in the new NetAcad.com. 
View <http://nsmail.netacad.com/MDU5LVZGWi04MzQAAAGfpWq6mHlv-HeRbJs_Ddj8Yh_U_P22Tp8CX9VFm7Ll_y5SYQTIy9EURs8StitFiVwAwWK92rc=>
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet
5. Start course customizations now

IPD Course Customization recordings cover how to migrate course customizations to the new NetAcad.com. And you don't need to wait for convergence! Start building content now then add to courses from August 26. 
Replay IPD <http://nsmail.netacad.com/MDU5LVZGWi04MzQAAAGfpWq6mPWgp2oKtqa5Ckal8S7ZKoLdL-T7Uwnkdr8jML6r41sOlsqLZcIhQdsmVZkGdrHMpTk=>
6. Are you ready for the new NetAcad.com?
Three August IPD sessions continue preparing educators for convergence: 
1) Top 5 Instructor Tasks
2) Academy Management
3) Are you Ready – convergence overview. 
Register IPD <http://nsmail.netacad.com/MDU5LVZGWi04MzQAAAGfpWq6mPWgp2oKtqa5Ckal8S7ZKoLdL-T7Uwnkdr8jML6r41sOlsqLZcIhQdsmVZkGdrHMpTk=>
Course Updates
Course translations
Arabic and Ukrainian Networking course translations are now available as instructor-led.
CCNP end-of-life
End-of-life date has been set for Cisco Networking Academy CCNP Enterprise: Core Networking (ENCOR) and CCNP Enterprise: Advanced Routing and Services (ENARSI).
Good News
International Day of the World's Indigenous People
August 8 marks International Day of the World's Indigenous People. Learn how our Cisco Networking Academy community elevates through education with these great success stories. 

Julio Lezcano, Panama <http://nsmail.netacad.com/MDU5LVZGWi04MzQAAAGfpWq6mAT_iL-eeF7-90m05y6veB-KYpO9qTssEpuAgZpBPp-sJ_TWTRhK37KzkMOe1-De07A=>  I  Dr Gabriella Arellano, United States <http://nsmail.netacad.com/MDU5LVZGWi04MzQAAAGfpWq6mImKbU4U0IJNqEm9jxFOx2vNmU2cUgcxBa-Jv0k4wD7mkm8HAl9PlutQR4nnC5GbXXs=>  I  Tully, Australia <http://nsmail.netacad.com/MDU5LVZGWi04MzQAAAGfpWq6mDucmB5jAxxb2U8GyvuGekTAT1Fwh05NVcFtkrLxs8ZT7xBxEC0DacXt8r2pve0yy2I=>
Find yourself in the future
Grow student numbers by promoting these quarterly virtual broadcasts as students navigate their own career path. Students learn latest technology trends, presented by Cisco experts. Coming soon: educator sourcebook.

subject: In case you missed: Most international students are aiming wrong
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here.

Before I start, I want to share something.

Special Mention

​Manifest Law (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nl5x9ilhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/dpheh0heddvqweam/aHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5pZmVzdGxhdy5jb20vcGFydG5lci95dWRp
)​

Many of you have been asking about visas, job transitions, and
long-term options in the US. Instead of guessing or relying on
WhatsApp advice, I strongly recommend speaking to a qualified
immigration lawyer.

I work closely with the team at Manifest Law, and I regularly
bring their lawyers onto my weekly live sessions. I trust their
advice and how they approach student and employment-based
immigration cases.

They’ve also agreed to offer a free legal consultation for
members of my job hunting accelerator community. (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nl5x9ilhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/wnhvg2w2aghqmmnrvxi7/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
)​

If you have questions around:

* F1, OPT, STEM OPT
* H1B, cap-gap, or employer sponsorship
* Job change, layoffs, or status planning
* Long-term options and timelines

This is a genuine resource I recommend, especially if you want
clarity before making important decisions.

-->Schedule a FREE Consultation With Manifest (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nl5x9ilhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/dpheh0heddvqweam/aHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5pZmVzdGxhdy5jb20vcGFydG5lci95dWRp
)
Schedule a FREE Consultation With Manifest (
https://manifestlaw.com/partner/yudi )
​
Most international students are targeting the wrong roles

Here’s the #1 pattern I keep seeing with international students
job hunting in the U.S.

They are optimizing for job titles, not hiring problems.

That single mistake quietly kills a lot of strong profiles.

I see it every week:

* "I’m only applying for Product Manager roles"
* "I’m targeting SWE roles only"
* "I want Data Scientist roles, nothing else"

These students are smart.
They’re hardworking.
They’re doing a lot of things “right”.

And still, they’re stuck.

Let’s break down why.

How companies actually hire (not how you think they do)
-------------------------------------------------------

Companies don’t start with job titles.

They start with problems.

Here’s what the internal conversation usually sounds like:

* Teams are missing deadlines
* Customers are stuck after onboarding
* Systems are failing at scale
* Too many stakeholders, no ownership
* Compliance and risk are becoming expensive
* Data exists, but decisions are slow or wrong

Only after that does HR step in and assign a label.

PM.
TPM.
Analyst.
Engineer.
Ops.

The label is secondary. The problem is primary.

Most job seekers get this backwards.

Why targeting one “popular” role backfires
------------------------------------------

When you restrict yourself to one narrow title, three things
happen.

1. You enter the most crowded funnel
------------------------------------

Popular roles attract everyone.
Career switchers. New grads. Laid-off seniors. International
students.

Even strong candidates get filtered out early.

2. Your resume becomes distorted
--------------------------------

You start forcing bullets to sound like a PM or SWE.
Instead of telling the truth about what you’re actually good at.

Recruiters sense this immediately.

3. You misread rejection
------------------------

You assume the problem is skill or intelligence.
In reality, it’s positioning.

Same candidate. Wrong frame.

The role-expansion strategy that actually works
-----------------------------------------------

High-performing candidates don’t ask:
“What role do I want?”

They ask:
“What problems can I solve right now?”

Here’s how that translates into real outcomes.

Same skills. Adjacent roles. Better results.
--------------------------------------------

If you’re targeting Product Manager roles
-----------------------------------------

Also consider:

* Technical Program Manager
* Program Manager
* Product Operations
* Implementation Manager

These roles value execution, alignment, and delivery.
Competition is lower. Sponsorship history is often stronger.

If you’re targeting Software Engineer roles
-------------------------------------------

Also consider:

* Solutions Engineer
* Platform or Support Engineer
* Automation-focused QA
* Cloud or Systems Operations

Still technical. Often closer to revenue.
Many companies sponsor these roles more consistently.

If you’re targeting Data Scientist or Data Analyst roles
--------------------------------------------------------

Also consider:

* Business Analyst
* Analytics roles in healthcare or finance
* Data + compliance hybrid roles

Less hype. More stability. Fewer applicants.

Same background.
Same degree.
Different framing.

Why this matters more for international students
------------------------------------------------

For international students, job hunting is not just career
strategy.

It’s immigration strategy.

Your priority is not the “perfect title”.

Your priority is:

* Staying employed
* Building U.S. experience
* Working at companies that sponsor
* Creating internal mobility

Titles can change later.
Momentum is what keeps you in the game.

The hidden benefit of broad role targeting
------------------------------------------

When students expand their role lens, I consistently see:

* Higher response rates
* Clearer resumes
* Better interviews
* Less panic-driven decision making

They stop asking:
“Am I qualified enough?”

And start showing:
“Here’s how I solve your problem.”

That shift alone changes outcomes.

Why environment matters more than advice
----------------------------------------

One thing I’ve noticed inside my Job Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nl5x9ilhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/wnhvg2w2aghqmmnrvxi7/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
):

Students don’t just improve because of tactics.
They improve because of exposure.

When you’re in a community of 5,000+ job seekers across roles:

* You see alternative paths earlier
* You stop obsessing over titles
* You make decisions with more clarity

Job hunting alone narrows your thinking.
The right environment expands it.

The question you should ask before your next application
--------------------------------------------------------

Before applying to another role, ask yourself:

Am I being rejected because I’m underqualified?
Or because I’m forcing myself into a role that doesn’t fit?

Most of the time, it’s the second.

If you want a structured way to think about role selection,
positioning, and execution in today’s market, you can learn more
about the Job Hunting Accelerator here (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nl5x9ilhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/wnhvg2w2aghqmmnrvxi7/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
).

– Yudi

https://604b57c3.unsubscribe.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nl5x9ilhvze4k55hnh7lwrq
) | Update your profile (

subject: Updates to how privacy settings work on Play
email_text:
Hello Nandini,

We've updated settings that manage personalisation and activity history on  
Play. You'll have dedicated controls for personalisation on Play and for  
saving activity in your Google Account's Play History, and these settings  
will be easily accessible in the Google Play menu.

In the past, personalisation and history on Play were managed by Web & App  
Activity (WAA) settings in your Google Account. Going forwards, WAA will no  
longer control Play personalisation and Play History. Instead, you'll be  
able to manage those settings directly on Play, and you'll see this change  
in your account in the next few days. Learn more about Play personalisation  
and Play History  
<http://support.google.com/googleplay?p=personalize_play_migration>

When the transition happens for your account, your Play settings will be  
based on the most recent choice that you made in WAA, so your experience  
will stay the same. If you have WAA turned on during the transition, which  
is happening over the next few days, then your Play personalisation and  
Play History settings will be on, and if WAA is off, then Play  
personalisation and Play History will be off. As before, you’ll be able to  
choose how long your activity is saved in your Play History and if you want  
to delete it from Play History manually or automatically.

After the transition is complete, WAA and Play settings will be separate,  
and changes to one will no longer affect the other. You can view and change  
your Play privacy settings in Personalisation in Play  
<https://myactivity.google.com/product/google_play/personalization> at any  
time. Learn more about the transition and how it affects your settings  
<http://support.google.com/googleplay?p=personalize_play_migration>

Thanks for using Google Play!

Should you have any questions, please visit our Help Centre  
<https://notifications.google.com/g/p/ANiao5oRRq0eA50bEW-9D6N8upfeW0HxV9wA8lODs7ab7yXcEGdX4w9LZlb2nqrA_AOfq5sTY9uOrLIo_xwFMd0jwS-ZJjgn5mC31z45ySqgAxrUqqKmZffjVnKgDTHP0n98QE9pfdosCDug9oLl6zGrTWp1YmCRbF08dSqMA7Xh5VNswNToSKdgkyO_7XMQQBZfbSS8OD-Pq1ZDTswBPvMSZhz0fTytvSaU8B9Cuqv7IWUnQpnDg9wizn3gEdkfy8s19Tz4>

subject: Most international students are aiming wrong
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here.

Most international students are targeting the wrong roles

Here’s the #1 pattern I keep seeing with international students
job hunting in the U.S.

They are optimizing for job titles, not hiring problems.

That single mistake quietly kills a lot of strong profiles.

I see it every week:

* "I’m only applying for Product Manager roles"
* "I’m targeting SWE roles only"
* "I want Data Scientist roles, nothing else"

These students are smart.
They’re hardworking.
They’re doing a lot of things “right”.

And still, they’re stuck.

Let’s break down why.

How companies actually hire (not how you think they do)
-------------------------------------------------------

Companies don’t start with job titles.

They start with problems.

Here’s what the internal conversation usually sounds like:

* Teams are missing deadlines
* Customers are stuck after onboarding
* Systems are failing at scale
* Too many stakeholders, no ownership
* Compliance and risk are becoming expensive
* Data exists, but decisions are slow or wrong

Only after that does HR step in and assign a label.

PM.
TPM.
Analyst.
Engineer.
Ops.

The label is secondary. The problem is primary.

Most job seekers get this backwards.

Why targeting one “popular” role backfires
------------------------------------------

When you restrict yourself to one narrow title, three things
happen.

1. You enter the most crowded funnel
------------------------------------

Popular roles attract everyone.
Career switchers. New grads. Laid-off seniors. International
students.

Even strong candidates get filtered out early.

2. Your resume becomes distorted
--------------------------------

You start forcing bullets to sound like a PM or SWE.
Instead of telling the truth about what you’re actually good at.

Recruiters sense this immediately.

3. You misread rejection
------------------------

You assume the problem is skill or intelligence.
In reality, it’s positioning.

Same candidate. Wrong frame.

The role-expansion strategy that actually works
-----------------------------------------------

High-performing candidates don’t ask:
“What role do I want?”

They ask:
“What problems can I solve right now?”

Here’s how that translates into real outcomes.

Same skills. Adjacent roles. Better results.
--------------------------------------------

If you’re targeting Product Manager roles
-----------------------------------------

Also consider:

* Technical Program Manager
* Program Manager
* Product Operations
* Implementation Manager

These roles value execution, alignment, and delivery.
Competition is lower. Sponsorship history is often stronger.

If you’re targeting Software Engineer roles
-------------------------------------------

Also consider:

* Solutions Engineer
* Platform or Support Engineer
* Automation-focused QA
* Cloud or Systems Operations

Still technical. Often closer to revenue.
Many companies sponsor these roles more consistently.

If you’re targeting Data Scientist or Data Analyst roles
--------------------------------------------------------

Also consider:

* Business Analyst
* Analytics roles in healthcare or finance
* Data + compliance hybrid roles

Less hype. More stability. Fewer applicants.

Same background.
Same degree.
Different framing.

Why this matters more for international students
------------------------------------------------

For international students, job hunting is not just career
strategy.

It’s immigration strategy.

Your priority is not the “perfect title”.

Your priority is:

* Staying employed
* Building U.S. experience
* Working at companies that sponsor
* Creating internal mobility

Titles can change later.
Momentum is what keeps you in the game.

The hidden benefit of broad role targeting
------------------------------------------

When students expand their role lens, I consistently see:

* Higher response rates
* Clearer resumes
* Better interviews
* Less panic-driven decision making

They stop asking:
“Am I qualified enough?”

And start showing:
“Here’s how I solve your problem.”

That shift alone changes outcomes.

Why environment matters more than advice
----------------------------------------

One thing I’ve noticed inside my Job Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/92ump8kn26inh64oqmrt9hzoxm933bwh26453/vqhqk3p3hrhom8vq32sg/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
):

Students don’t just improve because of tactics.
They improve because of exposure.

When you’re in a community of 5,000+ job seekers across roles:

* You see alternative paths earlier
* You stop obsessing over titles
* You make decisions with more clarity

Job hunting alone narrows your thinking.
The right environment expands it.

The question you should ask before your next application
--------------------------------------------------------

Before applying to another role, ask yourself:

Am I being rejected because I’m underqualified?
Or because I’m forcing myself into a role that doesn’t fit?

Most of the time, it’s the second.

If you want a structured way to think about role selection,
positioning, and execution in today’s market, you can learn more
about the Job Hunting Accelerator here (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/92ump8kn26inh64oqmrt9hzoxm933bwh26453/vqhqk3p3hrhom8vq32sg/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
).

– Yudi

Special Mention

​Manifest Law (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/92ump8kn26inh64oqmrt9hzoxm933bwh26453/dpheh0hedro2wocm/aHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5pZmVzdGxhdy5jb20vcGFydG5lci95dWRp
)​

Many of you have been asking about visas, job transitions, and
long-term options in the US. Instead of guessing or relying on
WhatsApp advice, I strongly recommend speaking to a qualified
immigration lawyer.

I work closely with the team at Manifest Law, and I regularly
bring their lawyers onto my weekly live sessions. I trust their
advice and how they approach student and employment-based
immigration cases.

They’ve also agreed to offer a free legal consultation for
members of my job hunting accelerator community. (

* F1, OPT, STEM OPT

* Job change, layoffs, or status planning
* Long-term options and timelines

This is a genuine resource I recommend, especially if you want
clarity before making important decisions.

Hey there –
 
Below is a short overview of what’s new in Clerk over the past couple of weeks. We've been busy, and we're excited to share a few
updates with you!
 
Track product stickiness with the new user retention report
Track how sticky your product is with automatic user retention reporting. Clerk now includes a retention report in the Dashboard
that helps you understand how frequently users return after signing up, visualize retention trends over time, and compare
performance against benchmarks.
 



 
This makes it easy to support Web3-native user flows without custom wallet auth infrastructure. Read more →
[https://go.clerk.com/U5RTZh8]
 



 
More control with Role Sets for Organizations
Role Sets let you define which roles are available inside each organization. This gives you more control over how permissions are
structured across different customers, plans, or product tiers.
 



 
Instead of exposing every role, you can now tailor role availability per organization. Read more → [https://go.clerk.com/U4vrOeZ]
 



Smarter default organization name and logo
Clerk can now automatically suggest both a name and logo for a user’s first organization during onboarding.
 



 
Suggestions are inferred from signals like the user’s email domain or profile information, helping users get set up faster with
fewer decisions. You can enable this behavior or keep organization creation fully manual, depending on your setup. Read more →
[https://go.clerk.com/l0eHeDs]



 
Local verification with JWT OAuth access tokens
OAuth access tokens now default to JWT format for new applications. JWT tokens allow local verification, better compatibility with
third-party services, and simpler debugging.
 



 
If your use case requires immediate revocation, opaque tokens are still supported. Read more → [https://go.clerk.com/DqFjIsc]
 



 
Secret key access controls
Secret key management is now restricted to Admins. Members can still view non-sensitive metadata, but only Admins can create,
reveal, or delete keys. This improves security while keeping teams informed. Read more → [https://go.clerk.com/k1KqFuq]
 



 
Hide incomplete analytics periods
You can now hide incomplete time periods on the Overview analytics page. This keeps reports clean and avoids confusion when a
period has not fully completed. Read more → [https://go.clerk.com/TFLLhwd]
 



 



 
Other features, fixes, and improvements
 * Sign-in with Solana [https://go.clerk.com/xgFVYxO]

 * Member role can no longer manage secret keys within the Clerk Dashboard [https://go.clerk.com/Yz61TZe]

 



 
Founders, Food, and First Principles
Earlier this week, Colin and the team camped out at Woolly Pig near the YC offices to treat founders to lunch and talk shop. It
was a great chance to connect with current Clerk users and let early-stage builders pick Colin’s brain about the initial days of
finding product-market fit. We spent the time nerding out on everything from the state of Auth and MCP to locally built drones!
 



 
Lastly, if you’re working on something new, head over to your Clerk dashboard [https://go.clerk.com/sbbfxqI] and start building
today.
 
Thanks for reading!
 
– Steve Tenuto

subject: Easy transition to your study abroad destination – Student Essential Services just for you!
email_text:
From dream to Degree, with you at every step!


ICICI and IDP IELTS brings financial solutions for your international journey.
Know more
Dear Nandini,



Congratulations on your decision to pursue higher education abroad!

We understand that relocating to a new country can feel overwhelming  especially when dealing with the many logistics involved. That’s why our Student Essential Services (SES) are designed to support you at every step of your journey.

From the moment you're accepted into your dream university to settling into your new life, we’re here to ensure your transition is smooth and stress-free.
Our SES includes:
- Education Loans – Get the funds you need to finance your dream.
	- Money Transfers – Quick, secure, and hassle-free transactions
	- Student Banking – Set up your finances with ease.
	- Forex Prepaid Card – Perfect travel companion for students abroad



Let us handle the complexities so you can focus on your studies and make the most of your international experience.

.

A lot has changed in my life lately.

If you watched my recent YouTube video (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/k0u59w0dvnf6h5zqmrltlho9x0577a8hd5738/g3hnh5hmg662mwhr/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj0yc0Nzd1RLdW44VQ==
), you already know this.

I’ve moved to North Carolina. New place, new routines, and slowly
starting to feel settled. There’s something grounding about
unpacking boxes and building a rhythm again.

I’ve been feeling deeply grateful. For this phase of life. For
the work I get to do. And for the trust so many of you place in
me when you share your stories, your doubts, and your resumes.

If you’re in North Carolina too, I’d genuinely love to meet you
sometime. This journey is much easier when it doesn’t feel
anonymous.

Now, let me tell you what’s been on my mind.

Every month inside my Job Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/k0u59w0dvnf6h5zqmrltlho9x0577a8hd5738/p8hpge2es9h4rgge40uq/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), we do resume reviews.

I see resumes from international students who have done a lot
right - good degrees, internships, part-time work, projects,
leadership roles.

And still… no callbacks.

Because their resume doesn’t survive the first few moments it’s
looked at.

And most people don’t even realize that’s where things are
breaking.

The first moment: A quick glance
--------------------------------

This is where someone decides, very quickly, whether to keep
reading or move on.

Here’s a common example I see during reviews.

The resume opens with something like:
"Skills: Communication, Leadership, Problem Solving, Teamwork."

Nothing is technically wrong.
But nothing pulls you in either.

Compare that to opening with something specific:
"Led a 4-person team to launch an internal dashboard used by 3
departments."

Same person.
Same experience.
Very different reaction.

In the first case, the reader has to work to understand you.
In the second, they immediately see relevance.

This stage isn’t about impressing.
It’s about being easy to read.

Clean layout. One column. Enough white space.
Three or four strong bullets instead of ten average ones.

If someone has to squint, scroll, or decode - they won’t.

The second moment: the scan for fit
-----------------------------------

If your resume survives the first glance, now the reader is
asking a different question:

"Is this person right for this role?"

This is where I often see resumes lose momentum.

For example, a job description asks for "stakeholder
communication," but the resume says, “Worked with different
teams.”

Or the role asks for "data analysis," but the resume says,
“Handled reports.”

The experience might be there - but the signal is weak.

Another example I see often:
Someone has 3 years of relevant experience, but it’s spread
across roles and internships in a way that makes it hard to spot
quickly.

The reader shouldn’t have to add things up in their head.
If you’re qualified, it should be obvious within seconds.

The final moment: real reading
------------------------------

Only a small percentage of resumes reach this stage.

Now someone is actually reading line by line, trying to decide if
they want to talk to you.

This is where how you did something matters.

“I improved process efficiency” is fine.
I reduced processing time by 30% by redesigning the workflow”
tells a story.

“I worked on a data project” is vague.
“I built a Tableau dashboard used by leadership to track weekly
KPIs” shows thinking.

Here’s the irony I see every month.

People spend hours polishing this part - rewriting bullets,
changing words, perfecting phrasing.

But they never fixed the earlier stages.

So their resume never even reaches the point where those details
matter.

That’s the frustrating part.

I’m sharing all this because I know how personal job searching
feels, especially if you’re navigating the US market as an
international student.

You’re not just submitting applications.
You’re carrying timelines, visa pressure, family expectations,
and silence from companies that never explain their decisions.

If your resume isn’t working yet, it’s not a verdict on your
ability.
It’s feedback on how your story is being presented.

That’s why we do monthly resume reviews (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/k0u59w0dvnf6h5zqmrltlho9x0577a8hd5738/p8hpge2es9h4rgge40uq/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
) inside the Accelerator. Not to judge, not to rush but to slow
things down and see what’s actually happening, together.

And beyond resumes, there’s something powerful about being around
people who are in the same phase, asking the same questions, and
making progress one step at a time.

As I settle into this new chapter of my life, I keep coming back
to this thought.

Careers don’t change overnight.
They change when clarity replaces guessing.

If you’re still here, still learning, still refining - you’re
doing better than you think.

And again, if you’re anywhere near North Carolina, I’d genuinely
love to meet:)

— Yudi

P.S. Inside theJob Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/k0u59w0dvnf6h5zqmrltlho9x0577a8hd5738/p8hpge2es9h4rgge40uq/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), we work on resumes, strategy, and decision making every month
with a community that understands this journey deeply. No
pressure 0 just support, structure, and honest feedback when you
need it.

Whenever you’re ready.

The HireReady Newsletter Vol. 78:

Your Job Description is a Starting Point, Not a Box

Forget everything you’ve been told about sticking to your current role's job description. The most successful people I know treat their job description not as a set of rules, but as a rough draft. They see their role as a living document they can, and should, rewrite from the inside. This issue is all about how to start editing.

🛠️ The job search hacks 🛠️

🔍 Reverse-Engineer Your Next Promotion with the Resume Scanner 🔍

Most people think of resume scanners as a tool to get a job, not to grow in one. That’s exactly why this works. Instead of just trying to match the job you have, you’re going to use the Jobscan Resume Scanner to identify the skills and keywords for the job you want within your own company. You're creating a data-driven roadmap for your professional development and proving you're already operating at the next level.

Next Steps:

- Find a job description for the role you want to be promoted into. This could be a senior version of your current role or a different position entirely within your company.
- Update your current resume to reflect all the projects and accomplishments you've achieved in your current role.
- Use the Jobscan Resume Scanner to compare your updated resume against that target job description.
- Analyze the skill gaps identified in the report. This is your personal development plan for the next 6-12 months.

Free Tool: Create Your Career Plan
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWsn9Q1rnrG-W12yZSM6LRm6cVhPn1w5JF-gBN74PqtC3gP0xW7lCdLW6lZ3pyW8_tMxM7yQF7xW8tk4JW69wsMsW6YyVLZ5BYN9DW691hF-8Xr6F7W8BKSNy5jtsx5W2R5NpZ4xPBz8VwW9151bHRkBVwTBF62fHNBqW8nP_1Q34-GkDW34xQR77hyn9NMBSsqDb6FRHW1VXWm28Xcl46W2lT1Fd7k9NqRW5v0Lf5799rsGVKCT3B4cpMGKW7dq9ly9cxP-lW8KdZHx2BTgKSW7zqHbs77MJFhW7xyrDJ5NqfWwW5h_P018C50C-W57WHHw4bqXMxW8k7BF12ydm9jW2RsDk97LrpDJW67fSBF6r0Jbmf2pRppT04 )

👂 Conduct a "Listening Tour" to Uncover Hidden Needs 👂

Your job description was written by people who had a specific problem they needed to solve. But I guarantee there are dozens of other problems, frustrations, and opportunities your team and your boss are dealing with that never made it into that document. Your mission is to become an internal detective. By proactively seeking out and solving these "unwritten" problems, you make yourself indispensable and naturally expand your role into more valuable territory.

Next Steps:

- Schedule brief, informal 15-minute chats with key colleagues in and outside of your immediate team.
- Ask open-ended questions like, "What's the most time-consuming part of your week?" or "If you could wave a magic wand and fix one process, what would it be?"
- Listen for recurring themes and pain points, especially those that align with your skills but fall outside your official duties.
- Pick one small, unaddressed problem you can help solve and take the initiative to work on it.

💡 Master the "Adjacent Skill" That No One Else Wants 💡

Every team has critical tasks that are technically no one's responsibility. These are often messy, complex, or just plain boring. While everyone else avoids them, you can strategically claim one as your own. By becoming the go-to expert on an "adjacent skill" (something related to but not explicitly in your job description) you build unique value. This could be anything from becoming the team's expert on a new software to mastering the art of creating compelling presentations.

Next Steps:

- Identify a recurring team need or a new company-wide initiative that is not yet "owned" by anyone.
- Evaluate which of these needs aligns with your interests and could be a valuable skill in the future.
- Dedicate a couple of hours each week to learning and practicing this skill.
- Start quietly offering your help to colleagues in this area, gradually establishing yourself as the resident expert.

Report: High-Income Skills
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWsn9Q1rnrG-W12yZSM6LRm6cVhPn1w5JF-gBN74PqtW3gP0xW7Y8-PT6lZ3kDW3bhq5g3zf0X6W8cs3165M8Z-KW3Pv7m045yTkHW1JbFZb7C3k_MVZL7ql3yqZg0W6zRx6Q1-g6xNW6lXhJX597Y3jW8zbRfn4mlwjKW2wsfrV1sK8Z6W2Th3wn2-cF28W9jJG5s7X2ylJN6V9nJ7CkWNWW85H9Vg6XbpQjW9g3LVj3prR3tW8JH20R3GL1BQW3QgfGY8rT9z0W6wy50q1TRC3QW5wFT_T8HZDnhW5597nb86HXf7W5k9RkY8PtCc-W7PtXHL7mlp_CW2J104G6X_T6GW6pn76V8_Hm54W3PY0SC7bn1dVW934R-81FVSbPW4by1Cr55Nw3sf1WcQl004 )

📝 Write Your "Accomplishments Journal," Not a To-Do List 📝

Your company tracks your responsibilities, but it's up to you to track your impact. Most people focus on what they need to do each day. I want you to focus on what you've done. By keeping a running log of your accomplishments, complete with metrics and outcomes, you shift your mindset from being a task-doer to a value-creator. This isn't just for your annual review; it's the raw material for rewriting your job description to reflect the high-impact work you're actually doing.

Next Steps:

- Create a simple document or spreadsheet titled "Accomplishments."
- At the end of each week, write down 1-3 things you accomplished, no matter how small.
- For each accomplishment, try to quantify the impact. Instead of "helped with the new marketing campaign," write "contributed to a marketing campaign that increased leads by 12%."
- Review this journal before any meeting with your manager to anchor the conversation in the value you've created.

Guide: How to Write Your Accomplishments
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWsn9Q1rnrG-W12yZSM6LRm6cVhPn1w5JF-gBN74PqtW3gP0xW7Y8-PT6lZ3mlW8GHnZN3TQb7WN7x0RSwqx6ktW39N_r85p-WcCN5PVDFL-cbHCN4PZZ9_sx628W3tBXl474BHsrN4km0JbczyRVW8dc6FQ6YzYHLW2JjxSB51LJzXW9cHFY870rp_NW5QL4Jb5xnvnGW6BFF2n3QfXQwW5yD89h6yKFbzW8CvQRQ1z7kjYW68l4JB76Yh_QW2NvgPZ2Djv9jW8KFPm15wh3k5W1NG8jV3ygcfjW5rV7CY7GH7F0W5p3xS75WCDhfW70jmPl8jrggpW42PB2V4lx_gmW4TgSR93HkBvqW6zqRTD6Ty5YqW5L14bZ4d0TblW57C6L63YZKXWf7w3jKH04 )

🚀 Propose a "Pilot Project" Based on Your Discoveries 🚀

Once you've identified hidden needs and started tracking your impact, it's time to formalize your role expansion. Instead of asking for a new title or a promotion outright, propose a small, low-risk "pilot project." Frame it as an experiment to solve one of the key problems you uncovered during your listening tour. This allows you to demonstrate your capabilities and the value of a redefined role without waiting for permission.

Next Steps:

- Distill your findings into a single, compelling problem statement.
- Outline a 30- or 60-day pilot project that addresses this problem, clearly defining the goal, the required resources, and how you'll measure success.
- Present this to your manager not as a request for more work, but as a proactive initiative to benefit the team.
- Execute the pilot flawlessly, tracking your results to build a case for making this new responsibility a permanent part of your role.

Stop seeing your job as a fixed position and start seeing it as a platform for creation. You have far more power than you think to shape your career, and it begins the day after you're hired. Now, go find a problem no one else sees and start solving it.

To your successful search,

James Hu,

Jobscan Founder and CEO

Catch the most important news, facts, and stories from Amazon, subscribe to Amazon News. How Amazon leaders use AI: Life hacks from top executives Artificial intelligence is transforming how people work and live, and Amazon's leaders are no exception. From using Rufus to automate shopping and track price drops, to leveraging Alexa+ to bring handwritten family recipes to life in the kitchen, executives across Amazon are finding creative ways to integrate AI into their daily routines. These aren’t futuristic experiments—they’re practical tools that help busy professionals manage family calendars, discover new books, code with their kids, and stay connected across time zones. Here’s how five Amazon leaders are using AI to simplify daily life and tackle real-world challenges. Read more Amazon One Medical’s Health AI assistant delivers personalized care guidance Amazon One Medical has launched a Health AI assistant that provides 24/7 personalized guidance directly in the One Medical app. This tool goes beyond generic health information by accessing your medical records, lab results, and medication history to deliver tailored advice. The assistant can explain your lab results in plain language, help you understand their meaning, and can guide you toward the right level of care. It also handles tasks like booking appointments and renewing medications through Amazon Pharmacy. When clinical expertise is needed, safeguards help ensure seamless connection to your care team via messaging, video calls, or same-day appointments. All interactions maintain HIPAA-compliant privacy, and conversations aren’t added to your medical record unless you choose. Read more 5 things to know about Peter DeSantis, Amazon's new leader for AGI, chips, and quantum computing Peter DeSantis, a 27-year Amazon veteran, now leads a newly formed organization uniting AI models, custom chips, and quantum computing. The role puts him at the center of some of Amazon’s most ambitious long-term technology investments. Amazon named a top 3 most admired company for the 10th year Fortune named Amazon a top 3 most admired company for the 10th consecutive year, based on evaluations from top executives and analysts across nine criteria, including innovation and social responsibility. The recognition reflects 2025 investments that delivered meaningful impact—from expanding rural delivery networks with more than $4 billion in investment, to supporting 700,000 employees through education and training programs, delivering 12.8 million meals to fight food insecurity, and building practical AI tools like Rufus to simplify everyday life. Read more Other stories you might like: 5 ways Amazon’s newest Dash Cart can save you time and money Amazon launches the Digital Arabic Library, connecting Arabic speakers with more than 38,000 titles ‘Legally Blonde’ prequel ‘Elle’ to premiere on July 1 on Prime Video Amazon's new portable Just Walk Out lanes bring checkout-free shopping to pop-up venues How to watch the 2026 Oscar nominees on Prime Video

OpenAI pivots to ‘practical adoption,’ micro apps catch on, Moonshot surges, 
Quadric delivers on-device AI, and 5 trending companies hiring this week.




 <https://wellfound.com>

Quick Hits



‘Agentic commerce’: Chinese super apps 
<https://www.cnbc.com/2026/01/21/china-tech-ai-agentic-commerce-super-apps-alibaba-taobao-qwen-tencent-wechat-doubbao-weixin.html>
 soar.



Asian ascent: India’s app downloads reach 26B 
<https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/21/indias-app-downloads-rebounded-to-25-5-billion-in-2025-fueled-by-ai-assistants-and-microdrama-boom/>
.



Audio Acrobat: PDF-to-podcast 
<https://www.theverge.com/news/864811/adobe-acrobat-studio-generate-podcast-presentation>
 arrives.



Desert dreams: OpenAI courts Middle East 
<https://www.cnbc.com/2026/01/21/openai-seek-investments-from-middle-east-for-multibillion-dollar-round.html>
.



Future chips: Gates invests in ‘metamaterials 
<https://www.photonics.com/Articles/Neurophos-Raises-72M-in-Seed-Round/a69540>.’



Kid code: Google trio launches learning app 
<https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/22/former-google-trio-is-building-an-interactive-ai-powered-learning-app-for-kids/>
.



Orbiting rivals: Bezos launches TeraWave 
<https://www.cnbc.com/2026/01/21/bezos-blue-origin-satellite-internet-spacex-amazon.html>
.



Pinning hopes: Apple ponders AI wearable 
<https://www.theverge.com/news/865212/apple-ai-pin-wearable-airtag-rumor>.



Social settling: Snap resolves addiction lawsuit 
<https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c62ndl2ydzxo>.



User surge: Threads 
<https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/18/threads-edges-out-x-in-daily-mobile-users-new-data-shows/>
 leapfrogs X.




Company Spotlight: Junior <https://www.linkedin.com/in/trey-holterman/>



In this week's ‘Why Work Here’ series, we chat with Nicholas Moschopoulos 
<https://www.linkedin.com/in/nimo-1/>, CTO & Co-Founder, to discover what makes 
Junior <https://wellfound.com/company/junior-8> a great place to work. 



Junior provides AI workflow automation and report generation for deal teams 
and their advisors.


Key Insights:



For job seekers - Engineers own products end-to-end, from design to sales 
calls, giving you direct exposure to customers and business operations beyond 
just writing code.



For recruiters - Look for candidates who want genuine startup exposure—not 
those who'll be satisfied with just a Jira board of bug fixes.



For founders: Being bootstrapped isn't a limitation; it's a strategic 
advantage that gives early employees more equity and keeps decision-making 
focused on customers rather than investors. 
<https://wellfound.com/company/halo-dot-science>


They're hiring! See 12 open roles <https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/junior>. 



Watch the full interview on YouTube <https://youtu.be/xXOs1ML9T74> or Spotify 
<https://open.spotify.com/episode/0OdlpfssFRriEGBSFXI5ZA?si=LauhNk-SQu2nrVeMvOSp1g>
.






 <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xXOs1ML9T74>

Startups Hiring Now  <https://wellfound.com/company/oklo>



SingleStore

The cloud-native database built with speed and scale to power real-time 
applications.

See 22 open roles. <https://wellfound.com/company/singlestore>



Ursa Major

Innovation built to Fly Faster: propulsion and defense systems for any 
mission, any domain.

See 62 open roles. <https://wellfound.com/company/ursa-major-technologies-1>



T-Rex Solutions

We solve our clients’ critical challenges by leveraging our innovative 
technical expertise.

See 52 open roles. <https://wellfound.com/company/t-rex-solutions>



AnySignal

Mission-Critical Space and National Security Infrastructure.

See 23 open roles. <https://wellfound.com/company/anysignal>




Job Search Tip: Don’t spray and pray. Stay in the 10–20 range.



Many job seekers respond to rejection by increasing volume and spending less 
time on each application.



Sam Wright <https://www.linkedin.com/in/samwri321/> from Huntr, 
<https://huntr.co/> analyzed thousands of job searches and found that 
candidates who send 100+ applications tend to take longer to get hired. 



 The 75th percentile of successful job seekers averages around nine 
applications per week.



That range matters because it leaves time to tailor: read the role carefully, 
understand the company, and apply where you’re already competitive rather than 
stretching everywhere.



Watch the full ‘Ask a Recruiter’ episode with Sam Wright from Huntr on 
Wellfoundhere. <https://youtu.be/ALWhVtrXbIM> 




 <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WDf9JbPXWT0>





What We're Reading



OpenAI focuses on ‘practical adoption’

OpenAI shifts its focus to practical day-to-day adoption 
<https://www.cnbc.com/2026/01/19/openai-to-focus-on-practical-adoption-in-2026-says-finance-chief-sarah-friar.html>
—especially in health, science, and enterprise.



‘Micro apps’ widen dev net

Armed with determination and AI, non-developers are building 
<https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/16/the-rise-of-micro-apps-non-developers-are-writing-apps-instead-of-buying-them/>
 apps from scratch. Upstarts like Replit enable the trend with tools fornatural 
language app creation 
<https://www.cnbc.com/2026/01/15/ai-startup-replit-launches-feature-to-vibe-code-mobile-apps.html>
.



Moonshot surges

Alibaba-backed Kimi chatbot creator Moonshot AI 
<https://www.cnbc.com/2026/01/19/alibaba-backed-startup-moonshot-ai-alibabi-backed-startup-chinese-ai-ipo.html>
 orbits a $5B valuation. Chatbots ride a wave of popularity in China, where 
ChatGPT is banned.



Quadric delivers on-device AI

SF- and India-based Quadric 
<https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/22/quadric-rides-the-shift-from-cloud-ai-to-on-device-inference-and-its-paying-off/>
 is building a royalty-driven on-device AI business serving enterprises and 
governments.

subject: Something I’ve been noticing lately
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here.

A lot has changed in my life lately.

If you watched my recent YouTube video (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nrmxgalhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/z2hghnhe5er93zap/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj0yc0Nzd1RLdW44VQ==
), you already know this.

I’ve moved to North Carolina. New place, new routines, and slowly
starting to feel settled. There’s something grounding about
unpacking boxes and building a rhythm again.

I’ve been feeling deeply grateful. For this phase of life. For
the work I get to do. And for the trust so many of you place in
me when you share your stories, your doubts, and your resumes.

If you’re in North Carolina too, I’d genuinely love to meet you
sometime. This journey is much easier when it doesn’t feel
anonymous.

Now, let me tell you what’s been on my mind.

Every month inside my Job Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nrmxgalhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/vqhqk3p3hrhomo32p5cg/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), we do resume reviews.

I see resumes from international students who have done a lot
right - good degrees, internships, part-time work, projects,
leadership roles.

And still… no callbacks.

Because their resume doesn’t survive the first few moments it’s
looked at.

And most people don’t even realize that’s where things are
breaking.

The first moment: A quick glance
--------------------------------

This is where someone decides, very quickly, whether to keep
reading or move on.

Here’s a common example I see during reviews.

The resume opens with something like:
"Skills: Communication, Leadership, Problem Solving, Teamwork."

Nothing is technically wrong.
But nothing pulls you in either.

Compare that to opening with something specific:
"Led a 4-person team to launch an internal dashboard used by 3
departments."

Same person.
Same experience.
Very different reaction.

In the first case, the reader has to work to understand you.
In the second, they immediately see relevance.

This stage isn’t about impressing.
It’s about being easy to read.

Clean layout. One column. Enough white space.
Three or four strong bullets instead of ten average ones.

If someone has to squint, scroll, or decode - they won’t.

The second moment: the scan for fit
-----------------------------------

If your resume survives the first glance, now the reader is
asking a different question:

"Is this person right for this role?"

This is where I often see resumes lose momentum.

For example, a job description asks for "stakeholder
communication," but the resume says, “Worked with different
teams.”

Or the role asks for "data analysis," but the resume says,
“Handled reports.”

The experience might be there - but the signal is weak.

Another example I see often:
Someone has 3 years of relevant experience, but it’s spread
across roles and internships in a way that makes it hard to spot
quickly.

The reader shouldn’t have to add things up in their head.
If you’re qualified, it should be obvious within seconds.

The final moment: real reading
------------------------------

Only a small percentage of resumes reach this stage.

Now someone is actually reading line by line, trying to decide if
they want to talk to you.

This is where how you did something matters.

“I improved process efficiency” is fine.
I reduced processing time by 30% by redesigning the workflow”
tells a story.

“I worked on a data project” is vague.
“I built a Tableau dashboard used by leadership to track weekly
KPIs” shows thinking.

Here’s the irony I see every month.

People spend hours polishing this part - rewriting bullets,
changing words, perfecting phrasing.

But they never fixed the earlier stages.

So their resume never even reaches the point where those details
matter.

That’s the frustrating part.

I’m sharing all this because I know how personal job searching
feels, especially if you’re navigating the US market as an
international student.

You’re not just submitting applications.
You’re carrying timelines, visa pressure, family expectations,
and silence from companies that never explain their decisions.

If your resume isn’t working yet, it’s not a verdict on your
ability.
It’s feedback on how your story is being presented.

That’s why we do monthly resume reviews (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nrmxgalhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/vqhqk3p3hrhomo32p5cg/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
) inside the Accelerator. Not to judge, not to rush but to slow
things down and see what’s actually happening, together.

And beyond resumes, there’s something powerful about being around
people who are in the same phase, asking the same questions, and
making progress one step at a time.

As I settle into this new chapter of my life, I keep coming back
to this thought.

Careers don’t change overnight.
They change when clarity replaces guessing.

If you’re still here, still learning, still refining - you’re
doing better than you think.

And again, if you’re anywhere near North Carolina, I’d genuinely
love to meet:)

— Yudi

P.S. Inside theJob Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nrmxgalhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/vqhqk3p3hrhomo32p5cg/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), we work on resumes, strategy, and decision making every month
with a community that understands this journey deeply. No
pressure 0 just support, structure, and honest feedback when you
need it.

Whenever you’re ready.
