subject: Speaking Test Venue Details
email_text: Dear Nandini Singh,        Passport: U3534205        Thank you for selecting IDP Education India Pvt. Ltd. for your upcoming IELTS on Computer test. We are pleased to confirm your seat, and the test will be conducted on a single day. To ensure a smooth process, kindly review the important details for each session below:             Speaking Test   Listening, Reading, and Writing Test         Date: Sunday, January 25, 2026   Time: 9:40AM   Venue: IDP CD IELTS Test Centre 3rd Floor, Shree Hara, 49-48-14, Santhipuram, Beside IOC Petrol pump, Highway, Visakhapatnam - 530016, Andhra Pradesh       Date: Sunday,25 Jan 2026   Time: 12:15 PM   Venue: IDP CD IELTS Test Centre 3rd Floor, Shree Hara, 49-48-14, Santhipuram, Beside IOC Petrol pump, Highway, Visakhapatnam - 530016, Andhra Pradesh             You must bring your current, valid passport. Certified copies will not be accepted.   Arrival at least 20 minutes before your scheduled test time is mandatory.   Please Note: Speaking test schedule cannot be changed.           Registration starts at 12:15 PM and closes at 12:45 PM   The test commences at 1:00 PM SHARP. Late entry will not be permitted, so please ensure punctuality.   You must bring your current, valid passport. Certified copies will not be accepted.                 ##DVS##       Note  : Your Speaking Test will be conducted over a high-definition video call with a certified IELTS examiner at an official IELTS test centre. A reference document about Video call speaking test is attached to help you prepare better.       For Test Day Rules and Regulations, please refer to Frequently Asked Questions at  www.ieltsidpindia.com .         Additional Information:     An electronic test report form (eTRF) will be issued for all test formats, accessible in your candidate login section after 2 working days from the result declaration date.   Electronic and personal items such as wallet, handbags, laptops, wristwatches, smartwatch, and mobile phones are strictly prohibited in the test room. IDP Education India Pvt. Ltd. takes no liability for any such items.   Entry may be denied for individuals experiencing cough, fever, or breathing difficulties due to suspected ailment.   Parents and guardians are not allowed inside the test venue.     Ensure confidentiality of your personal details like name, passport number, date of birth, and contact information, refraining from sharing them with anyone outside the test venues.           Wishing you the best of luck in your test. Should you have any inquiries, please contact us at  ielts.india@idp.com  or call  9228053999 .           Best wishes,               IDP Education India Pvt. Ltd.           Please note this is an auto-generated email and replies to this email will not be received.

subject: The enterprise in 2030
email_text:
AI in Action

By IBM


By IBM

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/enterprise-2030-ibm-kxyje?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BCmyRqQo%2BQhO8yCT21XReHw%3D%3D&midToken=AQEIM4HzHiyK2w&midSig=0LCgOyagtcUI41&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-ghmp60~mkoac020~81-null-null&eid=ghmp60-mkoac020-81&otpToken=MWIwYzE5ZTAxNTJmY2FjN2IyMjQwNGVkNDAxN2U3YjU4YWM2ZDA0NjkwYWQ4NjYxNzljNDA3NmI0YjU5NWZmYWY3ZDFkZmI1NDNjNmM4ZDA1Mzg3ZjRkOTYyYmViNjdiYzhkNDA0M2ZjNmVlZmMxZDI4YjJiYSwxLDE%3D
  
AI isn’t just enhancing the business model. By 2030, it will be the business model. IBM Institute for Business Value research reveals that while most executives expect AI to drive significant revenue, few can clearly identify where that value will come from. This creates a critical leadership gap. The advantage will go to organizations that move beyond generic tools and deliberately design and orchestrate AI agents around their data, workflows, and culture. Read more for insights from The Enterprise 2030 report, showing how AI‑first leaders are turning orchestration into a durable competitive edge. Plus, learn more about our newest AI advancements. ⤵ Competitive pressure will make big bets non-negotiable. 🚀 Speed will define winners in 2030. Over half of executives say competitive advantage will hinge on how fast organizations act, and not how perfect their decisions are. They also state that AI‑first companies embracing experimentation expect dramatically higher productivity, faster cycle times, and stronger delivery than their peers. “A startup can now operate at the same scale as a large enterprise, but move at a much faster speed. That means smaller companies can really disrupt the markets they’re going after.” - Aaron Levie, CEO and Co-founder, Box Today’s productivity gains = tomorrow’s industry transformation. 🤝 AI's productivity gains are just the beginning. While leaders expect AI to deliver major efficiency gains by 2030, the real advantage will go to organizations that reinvest those gains to reinvent business models—using a flywheel of productivity, innovation, and growth to reshape entire industries. The best AI will be one-of-a-kind. Your kind. 🤖 Competitive advantage won't come from having the biggest AI models, but from making them uniquely yours. Leaders that integrate multi‑model AI portfolios with proprietary data—and embed AI directly into business strategy, not just workflows—will differentiate, redefine leadership roles, and outpace peers by 2030. “AI’s future isn’t about bigger models. It’s about smarter integration with people and processes.” - Jinesh Dalal, Head and Vice President, Technology Development, C-Metric AI won’t do all your thinking for you. 🧠 Enterprise work is being reshaped by AI faster than skills can keep up. Most executives expect current roles and skills to become obsolete by 2030, driving the rise of AI “orchestrators” who manage agentic systems across functions. While AI promises major productivity gains, leaders say success will hinge on “re-skilling” the workforce around problem-solving, innovation, and adaptable thinking, not just technical expertise. “We have to push our creativity to see how many things we can do without human intervention. That is a mandate.” - Jacobo Diaz Garcia, CFO and Head of Digital Banking, Bankinter Quantum will cause the next seismic shift. ⚛️ As business leaders focus on staying ahead of the AI curve, they're at risk of missing a seismic shift in quantum computing. The advantage will go to enterprises that act now by building quantum skills, ecosystem alliances, and hybrid quantum‑AI infrastructure to turn emerging quantum capabilities into real competitive value. “Building a robust, proactive plan for quantum resilience is going to take some investment—and I deliberately use the word investment, because it’s not a cost.” - Kristie Chon Flynn, Data Protection Officer, Google 👉 Check out the full report New from IBM Introducing IBM Enterprise Advantage Meet IBM Enterprise Advantage—the service to build and operate your enterprise AI platform for scale and faster value. It brings structure, context and execution to enterprise AI, helping you move from isolated use cases to outcomes at scale with existing technology while integrating what you need. subject: The enterprise in 2030
email_text:
AI in Action

By IBM


By IBM

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/enterprise-2030-ibm-kxyje?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BmJuISBXaRoGv7M2FCf%2FtAg%3D%3D&midToken=AQEIM4HzHiyK2w&midSig=2qvbMkQgBkUI41&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-ghmp60~mkoaakjd~rl-null-null&eid=ghmp60-mkoaakjd-rl
  
AI isn’t just enhancing the business model. By 2030, it will be the business model. IBM Institute for Business Value research reveals that while most executives expect AI to drive significant revenue, few can clearly identify where that value will come from. This creates a critical leadership gap. The advantage will go to organizations that move beyond generic tools and deliberately design and orchestrate AI agents around their data, workflows, and culture. Read more for insights from The Enterprise 2030 report, showing how AI‑first leaders are turning orchestration into a durable competitive edge. Plus, learn more about our newest AI advancements. ⤵ Competitive pressure will make big bets non-negotiable. 🚀 Speed will define winners in 2030. Over half of executives say competitive advantage will hinge on how fast organizations act, and not how perfect their decisions are. They also state that AI‑first companies embracing experimentation expect dramatically higher productivity, faster cycle times, and stronger delivery than their peers. “A startup can now operate at the same scale as a large enterprise, but move at a much faster speed. That means smaller companies can really disrupt the markets they’re going after.” - Aaron Levie, CEO and Co-founder, Box Today’s productivity gains = tomorrow’s industry transformation. 🤝 AI's productivity gains are just the beginning. While leaders expect AI to deliver major efficiency gains by 2030, the real advantage will go to organizations that reinvest those gains to reinvent business models—using a flywheel of productivity, innovation, and growth to reshape entire industries. The best AI will be one-of-a-kind. Your kind. 🤖 Competitive advantage won't come from having the biggest AI models, but from making them uniquely yours. Leaders that integrate multi‑model AI portfolios with proprietary data—and embed AI directly into business strategy, not just workflows—will differentiate, redefine leadership roles, and outpace peers by 2030. “AI’s future isn’t about bigger models. It’s about smarter integration with people and processes.” - Jinesh Dalal, Head and Vice President, Technology Development, C-Metric AI won’t do all your thinking for you. 🧠 Enterprise work is being reshaped by AI faster than skills can keep up. Most executives expect current roles and skills to become obsolete by 2030, driving the rise of AI “orchestrators” who manage agentic systems across functions. While AI promises major productivity gains, leaders say success will hinge on “re-skilling” the workforce around problem-solving, innovation, and adaptable thinking, not just technical expertise. “We have to push our creativity to see how many things we can do without human intervention. That is a mandate.” - Jacobo Diaz Garcia, CFO and Head of Digital Banking, Bankinter Quantum will cause the next seismic shift. ⚛️ As business leaders focus on staying ahead of the AI curve, they're at risk of missing a seismic shift in quantum computing. The advantage will go to enterprises that act now by building quantum skills, ecosystem alliances, and hybrid quantum‑AI infrastructure to turn emerging quantum capabilities into real competitive value. “Building a robust, proactive plan for quantum resilience is going to take some investment—and I deliberately use the word investment, because it’s not a cost.” - Kristie Chon Flynn, Data Protection Officer, Google 👉 Check out the full report New from IBM Introducing IBM Enterprise Advantage Meet IBM Enterprise Advantage—the service to build and operate your enterprise AI platform for scale and faster value. It brings structure, context and execution to enterprise AI, helping you move from isolated use cases to outcomes at scale with existing technology while integrating what you need. Transform your: Business processes and compliance IT and technology Workforce Product and digital innovation 👉 Lean more about scaling your AI for faster value creation

subject: In case you missed: I Didn’t Expect This Answer From Him
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here.

I recently recorded a podcast with Girish (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4ewg2rhlh82pvrkki4hl6wzv/z2hghnhe5oq60ztp/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_ZmVhdHVyZT15b3V0dS5iZSZ2PWRKM2JfTC1vOGlR
).

Going in, I thought the conversation would be technical.
Robotics. Automation. Control systems.

Instead, the moment that stayed with me was much quieter.

At one point, he told me he cried during his job hunt.

More than once.

And this is someone who, within four months of landing in the US,
had three internship offers in robotics. In a market where most
international students couldn’t even get interview calls.

That contrast stayed with me.

When Girish came to the US in 2024, hiring for international
students was already broken.
Applications disappearing into portals.
Final round rejections because of visa constraints.

He didn’t come with some magical advantage.
One year of work experience.
No big-name US internships.

What he did come with was clarity.

Before he even landed, he knew exactly what he wanted to work on.
Control systems.
Automation.
Robotics at the intersection of hardware and software.

So while others were applying to anything that sounded “tech,”
Girish filtered aggressively.
If a role didn’t fit, he didn’t apply.
If it did, he went deep.

That decision alone saved him months of confusion.

He Didn’t “Fix” His Resume. He Changed the Story.
-------------------------------------------------

One thing I noticed during our conversation was how intentionally
he spoke about his work.

He didn’t say, “I worked on robotics.”
He explained what broke, what changed, and what improved.

Instead of listing tools, he talked about outcomes.
Instead of sounding impressive, he sounded clear.

He also built a portfolio.
Because a one-page resume couldn’t explain systems that involved
hardware, software, control logic, and real-world constraints.

That mattered in interviews.

The Job Hunt Was Not a Solo Sport
---------------------------------

Another thing that stood out was how little he romanticized doing
this alone.

Girish leaned heavily on a small group of classmates.
They shared job postings.
They shared recruiter contacts.
They shared feedback after rejections.

No secrecy.
No insecurity.

One of his internships came through referral and outreach.
The others came from strong cold applications that were actually
aligned with the role.

This wasn’t hustle culture.
It was coordinated effort.

Where Purdue Actually Showed Up
-------------------------------

During the podcast, I asked Girish a direct question.
Did Purdue actually make a difference, or was it all personal
effort?

He didn’t overthink the answer.

In robotics, many companies hire from a very narrow set of
schools.
Stanford. CMU. A few others.
Purdue already sits in that conversation.

But what mattered more than the brand was how the program (MS in
Robotics Engineering (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4ewg2rhlh82pvrkki4hl6wzv/p8heh9h4r9n68vuq/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9wdXJkdWUtQUlS
)) was designed.

His program gave him real freedom.
He could shape his coursework around control systems, robotics,
AI, and IoT instead of being locked into a fixed template.
That meant every semester moved him closer to the kind of roles
he was actually applying for.

Some of his courses were industry-connected projects.
Not simulations.
Not case studies.
Real problems with real companies, timelines, and expectations.

He described those courses as “almost like unpaid internships,”
because they gave him something concrete to talk about in
interviews.
Not theory.
Not intention.
Actual work.

When interviews went deep, he didn’t have to explain what he
wanted to do.
He could explain what he had already done.

If you’re someone exploring robotics, automation, or
systems-focused roles, it’s worth understanding how programs like
this are structured.

You can take a simple look at the Purdue's Autonomy, IoT, and
Robotics Professional Master's Program here and see if it aligns
with what you want to build next → program link (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4ewg2rhlh82pvrkki4hl6wzv/p8heh9h4r9n68vuq/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9wdXJkdWUtQUlS
)​

If you want to hear the full conversation, you can watch the
podcast here → podcast link (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4ewg2rhlh82pvrkki4hl6wzv/z2hghnhe5oq60ztp/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_ZmVhdHVyZT15b3V0dS5iZSZ2PWRKM2JfTC1vOGlR
)​

-Yudi J

Special Highlight

One thing I don’t talk about enough is the community behind the
Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4ewg2rhlh82pvrkki4hl6wzv/l2h9relebmhlqo82q5f6/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
).

​

It’s not a course people binge and disappear from.

​

It’s 4,000+ international students and professionals in the U.S.
who are all in the same phase.

​
Applying. Interviewing. Getting rejected. Trying again.

​

Inside, people:
- Share real timelines, not LinkedIn highlight reels
- Get honest resume feedback, not generic advice
- Show up to focus sessions when motivation is low
- Join office hours when they feel stuck or unsure

​

If you’re at a point where you want structure, clarity, and the
right environment around you, you can join our community.

)

Hello Nandini,

Thinking about IELTS but unsure how to begin, what to study, or how scoring works?

Don’t worry! That’s exactly why this FREE IELTS Masterclass exists. These sessions offer expert tips to help you prepare faster and score your desired IELTS band.


Why should you attend this:
• A clear understanding of the IELTS format & scoring
• Smart strategies for Listening, Reading, Writing & Speaking
• Common mistakes students make and 
how to avoid them
• Expert-led prep tips to help you save time & improve scores
• Live Q&A to get your doubts answered
• Free prep resources to continue practising after the session




Register to secure your spot in the Masterclass. Don’t miss this opportunity to learn from the best and bring yourself closer to your study, work, or migration goals.

.

subject: I Didn’t Expect This Answer From Him
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here.

I recently recorded a podcast with Girish (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/xmuwgx7mnks6hpkk3glf5h2q34zllfnhdxogk/3ohphkh3xd24plir/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_ZmVhdHVyZT15b3V0dS5iZSZ2PWRKM2JfTC1vOGlR
).

Going in, I thought the conversation would be technical.
Robotics. Automation. Control systems.

Instead, the moment that stayed with me was much quieter.

At one point, he told me he cried during his job hunt.

More than once.

And this is someone who, within four months of landing in the US,
had three internship offers in robotics. In a market where most
international students couldn’t even get interview calls.

That contrast stayed with me.

When Girish came to the US in 2024, hiring for international
students was already broken.
Applications disappearing into portals.
Final round rejections because of visa constraints.

He didn’t come with some magical advantage.
One year of work experience.
No big-name US internships.

What he did come with was clarity.

Before he even landed, he knew exactly what he wanted to work on.
Control systems.
Automation.
Robotics at the intersection of hardware and software.

So while others were applying to anything that sounded “tech,”
Girish filtered aggressively.
If a role didn’t fit, he didn’t apply.
If it did, he went deep.

That decision alone saved him months of confusion.

He Didn’t “Fix” His Resume. He Changed the Story.
-------------------------------------------------

One thing I noticed during our conversation was how intentionally
he spoke about his work.

He didn’t say, “I worked on robotics.”
He explained what broke, what changed, and what improved.

Instead of listing tools, he talked about outcomes.
Instead of sounding impressive, he sounded clear.

He also built a portfolio.
Because a one-page resume couldn’t explain systems that involved
hardware, software, control logic, and real-world constraints.

That mattered in interviews.

The Job Hunt Was Not a Solo Sport
---------------------------------

Another thing that stood out was how little he romanticized doing
this alone.

Girish leaned heavily on a small group of classmates.
They shared job postings.
They shared recruiter contacts.
They shared feedback after rejections.

No secrecy.
No insecurity.

One of his internships came through referral and outreach.
The others came from strong cold applications that were actually
aligned with the role.

This wasn’t hustle culture.
It was coordinated effort.

Where Purdue Actually Showed Up
-------------------------------

During the podcast, I asked Girish a direct question.
Did Purdue actually make a difference, or was it all personal
effort?

He didn’t overthink the answer.

In robotics, many companies hire from a very narrow set of
schools.
Stanford. CMU. A few others.
Purdue already sits in that conversation.

But what mattered more than the brand was how the program (MS in
Robotics Engineering (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/xmuwgx7mnks6hpkk3glf5h2q34zllfnhdxogk/n2hohvhv2ox679i6/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9wdXJkdWUtQUlS
)) was designed.

His program gave him real freedom.
He could shape his coursework around control systems, robotics,
AI, and IoT instead of being locked into a fixed template.
That meant every semester moved him closer to the kind of roles
he was actually applying for.

Some of his courses were industry-connected projects.
Not simulations.
Not case studies.
Real problems with real companies, timelines, and expectations.

He described those courses as “almost like unpaid internships,”
because they gave him something concrete to talk about in
interviews.
Not theory.
Not intention.
Actual work.

When interviews went deep, he didn’t have to explain what he
wanted to do.
He could explain what he had already done.

If you’re someone exploring robotics, automation, or
systems-focused roles, it’s worth understanding how programs like
this are structured.

You can take a simple look at the Purdue's Autonomy, IoT, and
Robotics Professional Master's Program here and see if it aligns
with what you want to build next → program link (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/xmuwgx7mnks6hpkk3glf5h2q34zllfnhdxogk/n2hohvhv2ox679i6/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9wdXJkdWUtQUlS
)​

If you want to hear the full conversation, you can watch the
podcast here → podcast link (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/xmuwgx7mnks6hpkk3glf5h2q34zllfnhdxogk/3ohphkh3xd24plir/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_ZmVhdHVyZT15b3V0dS5iZSZ2PWRKM2JfTC1vOGlR
)​

-Yudi J

Special Highlight

One thing I don’t talk about enough is the community behind the
Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/xmuwgx7mnks6hpkk3glf5h2q34zllfnhdxogk/48hvhehmw2p6x4ix/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
).

​

It’s not a course people binge and disappear from.

​

It’s 4,000+ international students and professionals in the U.S.
who are all in the same phase.

​
Applying. Interviewing. Getting rejected. Trying again.

​

Inside, people:
- Share real timelines, not LinkedIn highlight reels
- Get honest resume feedback, not generic advice
- Show up to focus sessions when motivation is low
- Join office hours when they feel stuck or unsure

​

If you’re at a point where you want structure, clarity, and the
right environment around you, you can join our community.

-->Join The Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/xmuwgx7mnks6hpkk3glf5h2q34zllfnhdxogk/48hvhehmw2p6x4ix/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
)
Join The Accelerator ( https://link.yudij.com/newsletteremail
)Just putting it out there for the ones who need it.

​

subject: In case you missed: Steps that turned interviews into 3 offers
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here.

Before I talk about something exciting, I want to share this with
you.

​MPOWER Financing (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4enkrpalh82pvrkki4hl6wzv/owhkhqhwxnzq5xcv/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9tcG93ZXI=
)​

Most students think loans only cover tuition.
​

But the real stress starts with all the extra costs like housing,
health insurance, and even your first grocery run in the U.S.

​

That’s where MPOWER Financing will make your life easier.

​

No cosigner, no collateral, no U.S. credit and 100% online
process.

Plus, they back it up with visa mock interviews and a career
program once you land.

​

If you’re still figuring out your finances this Fall, they’re
worth a look.

​

They will help you before you land and after you land because
their success is in your success.

-->Check Your Eligibility for Free (60 seconds) (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4enkrpalh82pvrkki4hl6wzv/owhkhqhwxnzq5xcv/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9tcG93ZXI=
)
Check Your Eligibility for Free (60 seconds) (
https://link.yudij.com/mpower )
​
If applications are about getting seen, interviews are about
reducing uncertainty.

This is where most job seekers lose momentum.

They get a few calls.
They prep randomly.
They “feel” ready.
And then wonder why things stall.

​Nirmayee (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4enkrpalh82pvrkki4hl6wzv/z2hghnhe57gl0ntp/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dD02MDlzJnY9T0xaSUp3Q3Q3LTg=
) didn’t approach interviews like that.

She treated them as a separate phase with its own systems.

Here’s what that looked like in practice.

Step 1: Interview prep was structured, not reactive
---------------------------------------------------

She didn’t wait for interview invites to start preparing.

By the time calls came in, she already had:

* a clear story of her background
* 8–10 prepared examples she could reuse
* a basic structure for behavioral and role-based questions

Once interviews started, prep became role-specific.

Instead of “preparing everything,” she asked:

* What does this role care about?
* What signals does this company look for?
* Which of my experiences map best here?

Each interview had a purpose.
Each prep session had an outcome.

This matters because interviewers aren’t testing how much you
know.
They’re testing how clearly you think under pressure.

Step 2: She built a story bank (and reused it intelligently)
------------------------------------------------------------

One underrated move she made was creating a simple story bank.

Not scripts.
Stories.

Each story covered:

* the situation
* her role in it
* the decision she made
* the result or learning

The same story could answer multiple questions:

* conflict
* leadership
* failure
* ambiguity
* execution

Most candidates try to invent answers live.
That increases anxiety and inconsistency.

Reusing stories increases clarity and confidence.

This is something we practice deliberately inside the Job Hunting
Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4enkrpalh82pvrkki4hl6wzv/x0hph6hekzgqx0s5/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
). Not memorizing answers, but learning how to flex stories
across questions.

Step 3: She used AI as a prep partner, not a shortcut
-----------------------------------------------------

AI didn’t replace her prep.
It sharpened it.

She used AI to:

* refine explanations when answers felt rambling
* test alternate ways of framing the same story
* simulate interviewer follow-up questions
* summarize feedback after mock interviews

What she didn’t do:

* memorize AI-generated answers
* use generic responses that sounded polished but empty

The rule she followed was simple:
AI helps you think faster, not think for you.

Used this way, AI reduced prep time without flattening her
personality.

Step 4: Portfolio and project walkthroughs were intentional
-----------------------------------------------------------

When asked about her work, she didn’t “talk through” projects
randomly.

She presented them.

Her structure was consistent:

* problem context
* constraints
* decisions made
* outcome or learning

No unnecessary slides.
No over-design.
Just clarity.

Nirmayee's Portfolio : Skill Section

Portfolio Screenshot : Certifications and Project breakdown
This approach works beyond product roles.

Data candidates.
Engineers.
Designers.

If you can explain your work clearly, you control the interview.

Step 5: She managed imposter syndrome through preparation, not
pep talks
-----------------------------------------------------------------

Imposter syndrome didn’t disappear.
It just got quieter.

Why?

Because preparation created evidence.

* repeated mock interviews
* clear articulation of impact
* familiarity with her own stories

Confidence didn’t come first.
It followed repetition.

This is why job searching alone is harder than it needs to be.

Practicing in isolation magnifies doubt.
Practicing with others normalizes it.

That’s also why community-based prep matters so much once
interviews start.

Step 6: Offer stage thinking (before offers arrived)
----------------------------------------------------

Even before offers came in, she had clarity on:

* what kind of role she wanted to grow into
* what trade-offs she was willing to make
* what didn’t align long-term

This prevented panic decisions.

Many students get one offer and stop thinking.
She had multiple because she stayed composed through the process.

Offers are leverage.
But only if you’re clear.

Why this matters
----------------

Getting interviews is one skill.
Converting them is another.

Nirmayee’s results weren’t about being exceptional.
They were about being systematic when most people improvise.

That’s the difference between “hoping something works” and
“knowing what to adjust.”

If you haven’t watched the full podcast yet, you can listen to my
conversation with Nirmayee here (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4enkrpalh82pvrkki4hl6wzv/z2hghnhe57gl0ntp/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dD02MDlzJnY9T0xaSUp3Q3Q3LTg=
).

It adds context to many of the decisions she made.

-Yudi J

Special Highlight

One thing I don’t talk about enough is the community behind the
Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4enkrpalh82pvrkki4hl6wzv/x0hph6hekzgqx0s5/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
).

​

It’s not a course people binge and disappear from.

​

It’s 4,000+ international students and professionals in the U.S.
who are all in the same phase.

​
Applying. Interviewing. Getting rejected. Trying again.

​

Inside, people:
- Share real timelines, not LinkedIn highlight reels
- Get honest resume feedback, not generic advice
- Show up to focus sessions when motivation is low
- Join office hours when they feel stuck or unsure

​

If you’re at a point where you want structure, clarity, and the
right environment around you, you can join our community.

-->Join The Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4enkrpalh82pvrkki4hl6wzv/x0hph6hekzgqx0s5/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
)
Join The Accelerator ( https://link.yudij.com/newsletteremail
)Just putting it out there for the ones who need it.

​

​Unsubscribe (
https://604b57c3.unsubscribe.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4enkrpalh82pvrkki4hl6wzv
) | Update your profile (
https://preferences.convertkit-mail2.com/qdu3pe0r67c7h4enkrpalh82pvrkki4hl6wzv
) | 600 1st Ave, Ste 330 PMB 92768, Seattle, WA 98104-2246

The HireReady Newsletter Vol. 77: Your Current Job is Your Secret Career Weapon

Stop thinking about your job as a dead end. Most career advice tells you to look for opportunities elsewhere, but the most powerful career moves are the ones you make before you even start looking. Your current job isn't just a place to earn a paycheck; it's a laboratory where you can experiment, build, and prepare for your next big thing, all while someone else foots the bill. Let's get to work.

🛠️ The job search hacks 🛠️

🔍 X-Ray Your Job Description to Find Your Next Promotion 🔍

Everyone thinks of a resume scanner as something you use when you're applying for a new job. Another way to use a resume scanner is to see the future. Instead of comparing your resume to a job you want, compare your current job description to the description of the job you want next. The results will give you a detailed, personalized roadmap of the exact skills and keywords you need to acquire in your current role to be a perfect candidate for your next one. You’re not just scanning your resume; you're scanning your career path.

Next Steps:

- Find a job description for a role you want in the future.
- Go to the Jobscan Resume Scanner and paste in that future job description.
- Instead of your resume, paste in your current job description.
- Analyze the results to identify the key skills and experiences you're missing. Now you have a targeted list of things to work on in your "career laboratory."

Scan Your Career Path
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWXbJ528d5g6W8sWZ0H42l122W70RWGB5Jn6JlN52hRhd3gP0xW7lCdLW6lZ3q4W1M2Vvw4Sdb50W4Y3D3n42vpjgW3js9zj970RRjW1Nl__M35QK_JW30FPlH2-bmfKW5Mb0Yq6f1Y_GW8NHNZy6jX9S9W8wRdDY1B_4w7W336y-z1xCxNVVt6lVH4_tL4JV5cVfn5T6GlRW6hmRrX7f0XS3V45Q6528f666V8Fgvc4QxttrN2rhkPGBl9XMW2DZxC-7DymB1W2Cq96X7gg3wxW4FC5vf4dfZ5DW6KXrVh3jF26wVJQ8HK99C3XkN6PF1x6cZ4rzVF35407Q6Rs0W5nqgLs9jykwdW8_CQsv5DvvPvf8djBkg04 )

🚀 Build Your "Escape Velocity" Resume Inside Your Current Company 🚀

Don't wait until you're job searching to think about your resume accomplishments. The savviest professionals are constantly "harvesting" accomplishments from their daily work. Start thinking like a resume writer every single day. Did you solve a minor but annoying problem? That's a bullet point. Did you help a coworker on a project? That's a collaboration skill. Your goal is to proactively seek out and document small wins that align with your next career move. You're not just doing your job; you're building a portfolio of evidence that you're ready for the next one.

Next Steps:

- Open a document and title it "Accomplishment Log."
- At the end of each week, write down at least one thing you did that had a positive outcome, no matter how small.
- Next to each accomplishment, try to quantify it. How much time did it save? How much did it improve a process?
- When it's time to update your resume, you'll have a rich, detailed list of achievements to choose from, not just a vague memory of your responsibilities.

Guide: Accomplishments to Add to Your Resume
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWXbJ528d5g6W8sWZ0H42l122W70RWGB5Jn6JlN52hRhx3gP0xW7Y8-PT6lZ3p-V5KV8X203vHyW4VFWj34P02KYW8NrRdR6r6SZBW1v3Qb-4ZnTHSVyrGWH97WzjQW36kkM69hZ3kYW8MfZcT6m5LlfVwMG6h6BzQyvN2Mz69hng84yMfkQ7nt89fVW3XP7Y61S5vBLW1K8y_Y86ZJqyW5n96f75fg3zGW8b_1B81HZnmjV_ksK97Cn5Z-W5GYRCH25YZCXW7MlBF68xzZV4W3QT7q_47xZskW4l3dqJ2t1nWfW777jPh2_V9gNW9jBwhJ58zHj-W43CNjy3VcRTmW6tQ8261mXyVmW7g_-zd7_dD_0W2RbT6J6pMyGfW9hsw1_7tJym0f1xNVkq04 )

📘 Turn Your Performance Review into a Masterclass for Your Next Job 📘

Performance reviews feel like a judgment. They're not. They're a golden opportunity to get your boss to help you plan your exit.

Instead of passively receiving feedback, go into your next review with a list of skills you want to develop that you know are valuable for your next job. Frame them as ways you can contribute more to your current role. You'll get company-sponsored training and projects that build your resume for your next move, and your boss will think you're the most engaged employee they have.

Next Steps:

- Before your next performance review, research the key skills required for your target job.
- Prepare a short list of 2-3 of these skills and brainstorm how they could apply to your current role.
- During your review, say something like, "I'm really interested in growing my skills in [Skill X] and [Skill Y]. I think it could help our team with [Project A]."
- Work with your manager to create a development plan that includes these skills, effectively getting your current job to pay for your upskilling.

Guide: Key Skills to Add to Your Resume
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWXbJ528d5g6W8sWZ0H42l122W70RWGB5Jn6JlN52hRhd3gP0xW7lCdLW6lZ3lyW3rZw6B5jBC45W16vxlb16bDKXW4JRkbk7-zlqQW73J8Xr1JkX7PW59nX6w1gh868N23-7h6pBQZHN75XRMFPFM13W2nvDjX80gxqdW1gwkNW2xK8btW6YknjR1rw824W3t1Q877zXG38W454-Xm118h1FW7Dzr4h4JdFHkW5kMpyv8wdjgdM5fJRBG5ZgWW8x_zwH7F9FNkN857S6hl3nPcW8yBK9Z5RRgbdN1NYyL_NCc9BVvzMDP3T_9qqW6vcr4l8LJcSdV-J4249dB1ljW4LdZlb7wl0Z1W7DkTsp2MSp0nf1X54GK04 )

💬 Conduct "Informational Interviews" with Your Coworkers 💬

Everyone tells you to do informational interviews with people at other companies. That's fine, but it's often awkward and transactional. The real gold is inside your own company. Your colleagues in other departments have a wealth of knowledge and experience that you can learn from.

Take a coworker from a department you're interested in out for coffee and ask them about their projects, their challenges, and their successes. You'll not only build internal allies but also gather powerful stories and metrics that can inform your own career path and areas where you can grow and gain experience.

Next Steps:

- Identify a colleague in a department that interests you.
- Invite them for a 20-minute coffee chat (in-person or virtual).
- Ask open-ended questions like, "What's the most interesting project you're working on right now?" or "What's the biggest challenge your team is facing?"
- Listen for stories of impact and accomplishment. How are they measuring success? What problems are they solving? This is invaluable research as you look for growth opportunities that will help your next job search.

Guide: Learn to Tell Your Story
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWXbJ528d5g6W8sWZ0H42l122W70RWGB5Jn6JlN52hRhx3gP0xW7Y8-PT6lZ3pfN5885qVjxT-BW3nGyhr7m65ZdN8kkyfTRGgBgW5dNfwn96r4nGW1kbCbZ3K7YNlW4rnQmg6c_9JgVB1GRV7rbH_QW4rR_916F9S3kW96zF0X91f66bVvyZvC774gRYW8HwH-h867r80W5FNTG-23pPBrW29_bFf3b3TfsW4dmjST7QbfxXW4Z-pB51ZYNvdVgwXtq7nKwLvW3wwdpM2YtNSmW1xGNVS2JXfTmW7qM1q_4lc494W8XRxbJ7ws-HCW5s80vc575nHsW5pch1d7Z5lwQW3tj5H65VH0XqW4lXSBT95TkGTW1fd1Cs8vg41QN50Wqp9WpfbMf2S3Tyl04 )

⭐ Become the "Go-To" Person for a Future-Proof Skill ⭐

Look at the job descriptions for roles you want five years from now. What skills appear over and over again? Pick one of those skills and decide to become the unofficial expert. Volunteer for any project that involves it, read everything you can about it, and offer to help others with it. Soon, you'll be the go-to person. This not only makes you more valuable in your current role but also builds a powerful personal brand and a key talking point for your future job search. You're not just an employee; you're an in-house consultant for a high-demand skill.

Next Steps:

- Research job descriptions for your dream roles and identify a recurring, in-demand skill.
- Find a way to start learning that skill, whether through online courses, internal training, or self-study.
- Look for opportunities to apply that skill at your current job, even in small ways.
- Proactively offer your help and knowledge to colleagues. Before you know it, you'll be known as the expert.

Guide: 75 Free Certification Ideas
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWXbJ528d5g6W8sWZ0H42l122W70RWGB5Jn6JlN52hRhx3gP0xW7Y8-PT6lZ3lwW1BtXVY11K1J2W8BS9b11bzPWQW7vtfp7259btsW7hfXY-1yWNJhW90dnN64sYfRJN8Fr_kKWGJMbW8yQhj25vpjNcW85XPqk1Ycww1W4pPPVT1ms-0XW1Cb33h3HbjfzW52jZMx8yk1BLW3xscLh87lgDKW40q15k55dk9BW1pSD7j6bFch1W1ghX7W3JvCtbW4dVkpS545hY-W2CYVBR1Sb_R3N17t8gddhpt7W8MW5kz6f_X-VW3xp2kh92wDGGW1sTCSZ5xwmQqVQ5RDn84sYvgVG_w8n1km63kW17qF3-8zltB-W27zT2m3lqJMtW8MDFV689HnP9f7R-Qxd04 )

Your next career move isn't something you wait for; it's something you build, piece by piece, right where you are now. Stop waiting for the perfect opportunity to fall into your lap and start building it yourself. This week, pick just one of these hacks and try it. You'll be amazed at how much power you have to shape your future, right now.

To your successful search,

James Hu,

Jobscan Founder and CEO

Founder tip: "We don't want a bunch of Walter Cronkites. We don't want people 
reporting the news. Come with a solution."
 <https://www.linkedin.com/in/trey-holterman/>



Trey Holterman <https://www.linkedin.com/in/trey-holterman/>, CEO and 
Co-Founder ofTennr <https://www.tennr.com/>, doesn’t want Walter Cronkites on 
his team. He wants people who see what’s broken and either fix it themselves or 
pull in whoever can.

Calling out issues matters. But stopping there just adds noise. The people who 
make early teams work show up with options, not just observations.

Early teams drown when everyone’s pointing at fires. They survive when people 
grab tools and start building fixes.

Job Search Tip: How can you tell if engineering actually works well with the 
rest of the company?



Matt Glickman <https://www.linkedin.com/in/mattglickman/>, Senior Technical 
Recruiter atAcuityMD <https://www.acuitymd.com/>, says to ask every leader the 
same question during your interview loop: How do you collaborate across 
departments? 



Then listen for whether they’re describing the same company, or not.



If the engineering manager and the product lead give you roughly the same 
answer, that's alignment. If their stories don't match, that's your red flag.



Misaligned answers don't mean someone's lying. They mean leaders are living in 
separate realities. You can't fix that as the new hire.



Watch the full Ask a Recruiter episode, “Interview Red Flags & Green Flags for 
Engineers, Data Scientists & PMs,”here. <https://youtu.be/NeT79AopBxo>

And then wonder why things stall.

​Nirmayee (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/38u04kwm2otkh2x4gzqurh4qd8onns7h5l9pg/25h2hoh3l8lqnzb3/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dD02MDlzJnY9T0xaSUp3Q3Q3LTg=
) didn’t approach interviews like that.

She treated them as a separate phase with its own systems.

Here’s what that looked like in practice.

Step 1: Interview prep was structured, not reactive
---------------------------------------------------

She didn’t wait for interview invites to start preparing.

By the time calls came in, she already had:

* a clear story of her background
* 8–10 prepared examples she could reuse
* a basic structure for behavioral and role-based questions

Once interviews started, prep became role-specific.

Instead of “preparing everything,” she asked:

* What does this role care about?
* What signals does this company look for?
* Which of my experiences map best here?

Each interview had a purpose.
Each prep session had an outcome.

This matters because interviewers aren’t testing how much you
know.
They’re testing how clearly you think under pressure.

Step 2: She built a story bank (and reused it intelligently)
------------------------------------------------------------

One underrated move she made was creating a simple story bank.

Not scripts.
Stories.

Each story covered:

* the situation
* her role in it
* the decision she made
* the result or learning

The same story could answer multiple questions:

* conflict
* leadership
* failure
* ambiguity
* execution

Most candidates try to invent answers live.
That increases anxiety and inconsistency.

Reusing stories increases clarity and confidence.

This is something we practice deliberately inside the Job Hunting
Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/38u04kwm2otkh2x4gzqurh4qd8onns7h5l9pg/g3hnh5hmglg0x4ur/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
). Not memorizing answers, but learning how to flex stories
across questions.

Step 3: She used AI as a prep partner, not a shortcut
-----------------------------------------------------

AI didn’t replace her prep.
It sharpened it.

She used AI to:

* refine explanations when answers felt rambling
* test alternate ways of framing the same story
* simulate interviewer follow-up questions
* summarize feedback after mock interviews

What she didn’t do:

* memorize AI-generated answers
* use generic responses that sounded polished but empty

The rule she followed was simple:
AI helps you think faster, not think for you.

Used this way, AI reduced prep time without flattening her
personality.

Step 4: Portfolio and project walkthroughs were intentional
-----------------------------------------------------------

When asked about her work, she didn’t “talk through” projects
randomly.

She presented them.

Her structure was consistent:

* problem context
* constraints
* decisions made
* outcome or learning

No unnecessary slides.
No over-design.
Just clarity.

Nirmayee's Portfolio : Skill Section

Portfolio Screenshot : Certifications and Project breakdown
This approach works beyond product roles.

Data candidates.
Engineers.
Designers.

If you can explain your work clearly, you control the interview.

Step 5: She managed imposter syndrome through preparation, not
pep talks
-----------------------------------------------------------------

Imposter syndrome didn’t disappear.
It just got quieter.

Why?

Because preparation created evidence.

* repeated mock interviews
* clear articulation of impact
* familiarity with her own stories

Confidence didn’t come first.
It followed repetition.

This is why job searching alone is harder than it needs to be.

Practicing in isolation magnifies doubt.
Practicing with others normalizes it.

That’s also why community-based prep matters so much once
interviews start.

Step 6: Offer stage thinking (before offers arrived)
----------------------------------------------------

Even before offers came in, she had clarity on:

* what kind of role she wanted to grow into
* what trade-offs she was willing to make
* what didn’t align long-term

This prevented panic decisions.

Many students get one offer and stop thinking.
She had multiple because she stayed composed through the process.

Offers are leverage.
But only if you’re clear.

Why this matters
----------------

Getting interviews is one skill.
Converting them is another.

Nirmayee’s results weren’t about being exceptional.
They were about being systematic when most people improvise.

That’s the difference between “hoping something works” and
“knowing what to adjust.”

If you haven’t watched the full podcast yet, you can listen to my
conversation with Nirmayee here (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/38u04kwm2otkh2x4gzqurh4qd8onns7h5l9pg/25h2hoh3l8lqnzb3/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dD02MDlzJnY9T0xaSUp3Q3Q3LTg=
).

It adds context to many of the decisions she made.

-Yudi J

Special Highlight

One thing I don’t talk about enough is the community behind the
Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/38u04kwm2otkh2x4gzqurh4qd8onns7h5l9pg/g3hnh5hmglg0x4ur/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
).

​

It’s not a course people binge and disappear from.

​

It’s 4,000+ international students and professionals in the U.S.
who are all in the same phase.

​
Applying. Interviewing. Getting rejected. Trying again.

​

Inside, people:
- Share real timelines, not LinkedIn highlight reels
- Get honest resume feedback, not generic advice
- Show up to focus sessions when motivation is low
- Join office hours when they feel stuck or unsure

​

If you’re at a point where you want structure, clarity, and the
right environment around you, you can join our community.

subject: Developer security news - Arcjet for Python
email_text:
Arcjet is now available for Python. Add bot detection, rate limiting, and signup protection directly in FastAPI and Flask.

arcjet-logo-1 (https://hs.arcjet.com/e3t/Ctc/JA+113/d5pJYZ04/VWSNs83_V_HDW7wPQ0g2jK-xDW63xJzN5JkSBcN7KkK1s3prCCW6N1vHY6lZ3nFW8xBtJB1kJQ1KV4h_nP4C0hSwW4Skztl719CG5V-NK-L9cvYYNW6J1xYD3zb44gW71ZJTF8TrfcRW1RNFH72X75bPVRWZLb2bWjKgN4ztj9Y56YVkW6svRZJ2XS2_FW7c9M-z2s4R_2W3c9W-D54ZWwFW5cttq53hCThlW90vNK61FzNh9W79CxdV4qx-pXW1V78-Z5G3xgNW8KV4Bz6r7HHgW13c6D_1W1vkFW6jVb3X4Bl8n2W5SM4xn6ZJgZvN6kNSMvWswFDW5N_SBS2D-XRQf2RlL2M04 )

Arcjet developer security newsletter

The view from the bridge

What we're thinking about.

Security belongs in your Python code

We spend a lot of time talking with Python teams about how they protect their applications. Frameworks like FastAPI and Flask make it easy to build and ship APIs, but security often still lives outside the application, with separate configuration, different mental models, and no context about your business.

Teams are focused on practical problems like stopping fake signups, throttling abusive traffic, and protecting sensitive endpoints. They want to handle those problems where they already work, inside their application code.

This week, we’re launching the Arcjet Python SDK (https://hs.arcjet.com/e3t/Ctc/JA+113/d5pJYZ04/VWSNs83_V_HDW7wPQ0g2jK-xDW63xJzN5JkSBcN7KkK1M3prCCW7lCdLW6lZ3mpW5W5FYl3vn-c6VRKMKl6dHZ61W2qS6-w7mVbTmW4WF2PB5hYzKlW2Gf72V7HyZxwN64ZVMPP0yZLW5Gv-Yp2zG4fLW4nWwgC2Pbq3jVl3NJb6LTxpQW965pGj93-N1dW4F7jk97drLm1W7F-mp48fddQFW4rbd5s4Jc0prW4LgDq12tHcmYVYQ52P6lz46XW34z37v5FxqzKW5Gb5M44vzMd8W1LVtbX72f7lgW45x0mH5m9hJqN7Km5M4bFtz2W4ggkGm403_8SW2qnLTm6MTd6dW63SzH-8w8n9CW2zs0gl2pPfbCf208Y9l04 ) to make that possible. It lets you add rate limiting, bot detection, signup protection, email validation, and Shield directly in code. The SDK supports both FastAPI and Flask and is available today in beta.

Our goal is simple. Security should feel like just another feature. Now that's possible in Python as well as JS/TS.

- David Mytton, Founder, Arcjet

If you’re working in the US, chances are you’re sending money to

​

For the longest time, I assumed losing some money during
transfers was just part of the process. A small fee here, a
slightly worse exchange rate there.

​

Over the last 11 years, I’ve tried multiple platforms. Most of
them quietly take a cut one way or another.

​

Recently, almost a year back I started using Crobo Money and it
genuinely surprised me. No transfer fees, exchange rates very
close to Google’s rate, and a refund if anything goes wrong.

​

​I shared a short video breaking this down with real numbers (

). If you’re already sending money home, this might save you more

​
Today's newsletter is about execution.

Nirmayee didn’t get one lucky offer.
She ended up with 3 offers, across closely related roles, by
running a deliberate system in a tough market.

The roles she was targeting sat in the product / business /
analytics intersection.
Not wildly different tracks.
Just different enough to widen opportunity without diluting
focus.

That detail matters more than people realize.

Watch our full conversation here -> Watch here (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/27upg3vko0ioh8l3k2za3hgz5m744hghn3row/58hvh7hg3v29e6t6/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dD0ycyZ2PU9MWklKd0N0Ny04
).

Let’s break down what she actually did, step by step.

Step 1: She didn’t chase “any job”. She picked a direction early.
-----------------------------------------------------------------

Most job seekers say:
“I’m applying broadly to keep my options open.”

What that usually means:

* one generic resume
* one LinkedIn headline
* confused recruiter signals

Nirmayee did something smarter.

She defined one primary role and one adjacent role.

Example structure:

* Primary: Product / Business-facing role
* Adjacent: Analytics or execution-heavy role that used the same
skills

Why this works:

* same core experience
* overlapping keywords
* minimal resume changes
* higher interview conversion

She was not switching between software, finance, consulting, and
marketing.
She stayed in one lane with two exits.

Write down:

* Role A (main target)
* Role B (backup that still builds toward Role A long-term)

If Role B doesn’t help your long-term story, it’s noise.

Step 2: Her resume was built for scanning, not reading
------------------------------------------------------

Recruiters don’t read resumes.
They scan.

Nirmayee optimized for:

* first 10 seconds
* top half of page 1
* clear impact signals

Her resume:

* did NOT list responsibilities
* did NOT explain everything she did
* focused on outcomes, decisions, and ownership

She also updated her resume continuously.

After:

* recruiter screens
* interview rounds
* feedback from peers

The resume evolved.

This is exactly why, inside my Job Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/27upg3vko0ioh8l3k2za3hgz5m744hghn3row/25h2hoh3lvwo5qs3/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), we do resume reviews multiple times. Not because people are
bad writers, but because resumes need iteration based on market
response.

Step 3: Cold applications were intentional, not emotional
---------------------------------------------------------

Nirmayee applied with rules.

Her cold application strategy looked like this:

* apply early (ideally within first 3 to 5 days)
* avoid mass “Easy Apply” roles with 1,000+ applicants
* prioritize companies where alumni or similar profiles existed

She also accepted one uncomfortable truth:
​Most applications won’t convert.

So instead of obsessing over rejections, she focused on response
rate.

That’s why tracking mattered.

Step 4: She tracked her job search like a project
-------------------------------------------------

No guessing.
No “I think I applied a lot”.

She used a tracker to log:

* company
* role
* application date
* referral or cold
* response
* interview stage

Nirmayee's job tracker/Notion Board
​

Why this matters:

* patterns show up fast
* you see which roles convert
* you stop blaming yourself blindly

Most students don’t fail because they’re bad.
They fail because they don’t observe their own data.

Inside the accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/27upg3vko0ioh8l3k2za3hgz5m744hghn3row/25h2hoh3lvwo5qs3/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), this is one of the first systems we set up because clarity
reduces anxiety immediately.

Step 5: Referrals were layered, not rushed
------------------------------------------

She didn’t DM strangers asking for referrals.

Her approach:

* short, specific outreach
* clear role context
* genuine curiosity about the team

Many conversations didn’t lead to referrals immediately.
But they led to:

* internal insights
* resume forwarding later
* interview prep help

Cold applications opened doors.
Referrals increased trust.

Both were used together.

Why this got her 3 offers
-------------------------

Not because she was perfect.
Not because she had insider access.

Because:

* her profile was easy to understand
* her resume spoke recruiter language
* her applications were timed
* her strategy was consistent

That’s how you go from hoping for one offer to choosing between
multiple.

If reading this made you realize you’re doing a lot but not
seeing proportional results, that gap is usually structural.

Inside my Job Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/27upg3vko0ioh8l3k2za3hgz5m744hghn3row/25h2hoh3lvwo5qs3/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), we work on this together - resume reviews, focused prep
sessions, and monthly masterclasses with people who’ve actually
hired in these roles.

You don’t need more motivation. You need a repeatable process.

-Yudi J

Special Mention

​Manifest Law (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/27upg3vko0ioh8l3k2za3hgz5m744hghn3row/qvh8h7hde7p6x3bl/aHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5pZmVzdGxhdy5jb20vcGFydG5lci95dWRp
)​

Many of you have been asking about visas, job transitions, and
long-term options in the US. Instead of guessing or relying on
WhatsApp advice, I strongly recommend speaking to a qualified
immigration lawyer.

I work closely with the team at Manifest Law, and I regularly
bring their lawyers onto my weekly live sessions. I trust their
advice and how they approach student and employment-based
immigration cases.

They’ve also agreed to offer a free legal consultation for
members of my job hunting accelerator community. (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/27upg3vko0ioh8l3k2za3hgz5m744hghn3row/25h2hoh3lvwo5qs3/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
)​

If you have questions around:

* F1, OPT, STEM OPT
* H1B, cap-gap, or employer sponsorship
* Job change, layoffs, or status planning
* Long-term options and timelines

This is a genuine resource I recommend, especially if you want
clarity before making important decisions.

If you’re working in the US, chances are you’re sending money to
India every month.
​

For the longest time, I assumed losing some money during
transfers was just part of the process. A small fee here, a
slightly worse exchange rate there.

​

Over the last 11 years, I’ve tried multiple platforms. Most of
them quietly take a cut one way or another.

​

Recently, almost a year back I started using Crobo Money and it
genuinely surprised me. No transfer fees, exchange rates very
close to Google’s rate, and a refund if anything goes wrong.

​

​I shared a short video breaking this down with real numbers (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nm4wvblhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/8ghqhohok4dwzmuk/aHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dWJlLmNvbS9zaG9ydHMvTThPazRIQzc5WW8=
). If you’re already sending money home, this might save you more
than you think.

​

Use code YUDIJ to get a $25 Amazon gift card after your first
$500 transfer.

-->Give it a try -> Crobo Money (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nm4wvblhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/08hwh9h2q8env6ul/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9jcm9ib2J5eXVkaQ==
)
Give it a try -> Crobo Money ( https://link.yudij.com/crobobyyudi
)
​
Today's newsletter is about execution.

Nirmayee didn’t get one lucky offer.
She ended up with 3 offers, across closely related roles, by
running a deliberate system in a tough market.

The roles she was targeting sat in the product / business /
analytics intersection.
Not wildly different tracks.
Just different enough to widen opportunity without diluting
focus.

That detail matters more than people realize.

Watch our full conversation here -> Watch here (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nm4wvblhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/vqh3hrho850mlwig/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dD0ycyZ2PU9MWklKd0N0Ny04
).

Let’s break down what she actually did, step by step.

Step 1: She didn’t chase “any job”. She picked a direction early.
-----------------------------------------------------------------

Most job seekers say:
“I’m applying broadly to keep my options open.”

What that usually means:

* one generic resume
* one LinkedIn headline
* confused recruiter signals

Nirmayee did something smarter.

She defined one primary role and one adjacent role.

Example structure:

* Primary: Product / Business-facing role
* Adjacent: Analytics or execution-heavy role that used the same
skills

Why this works:

* same core experience
* overlapping keywords
* minimal resume changes
* higher interview conversion

She was not switching between software, finance, consulting, and
marketing.
She stayed in one lane with two exits.

Write down:

* Role A (main target)
* Role B (backup that still builds toward Role A long-term)

If Role B doesn’t help your long-term story, it’s noise.

Step 2: Her resume was built for scanning, not reading
------------------------------------------------------

Recruiters don’t read resumes.
They scan.

Nirmayee optimized for:

* first 10 seconds
* top half of page 1
* clear impact signals

Her resume:

* did NOT list responsibilities
* did NOT explain everything she did
* focused on outcomes, decisions, and ownership

She also updated her resume continuously.

After:

* recruiter screens
* interview rounds
* feedback from peers

The resume evolved.

This is exactly why, inside my Job Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nm4wvblhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/l2hehmhlwg2qedf6/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), we do resume reviews multiple times. Not because people are
bad writers, but because resumes need iteration based on market
response.

Step 3: Cold applications were intentional, not emotional
---------------------------------------------------------

Nirmayee applied with rules.

Her cold application strategy looked like this:

* apply early (ideally within first 3 to 5 days)
* avoid mass “Easy Apply” roles with 1,000+ applicants
* prioritize companies where alumni or similar profiles existed

She also accepted one uncomfortable truth:
​Most applications won’t convert.

So instead of obsessing over rejections, she focused on response
rate.

That’s why tracking mattered.

Step 4: She tracked her job search like a project
-------------------------------------------------

No guessing.
No “I think I applied a lot”.

She used a tracker to log:

* company
* role
* application date
* referral or cold
* response
* interview stage

Nirmayee's job tracker/Notion Board
​

Why this matters:

* patterns show up fast
* you see which roles convert
* you stop blaming yourself blindly

Most students don’t fail because they’re bad.
They fail because they don’t observe their own data.

Inside the accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nm4wvblhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/l2hehmhlwg2qedf6/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), this is one of the first systems we set up because clarity
reduces anxiety immediately.

Step 5: Referrals were layered, not rushed
------------------------------------------

She didn’t DM strangers asking for referrals.

Her approach:

* short, specific outreach
* clear role context
* genuine curiosity about the team

Many conversations didn’t lead to referrals immediately.
But they led to:

* internal insights
* resume forwarding later
* interview prep help

Cold applications opened doors.
Referrals increased trust.

Both were used together.

Why this got her 3 offers
-------------------------

Not because she was perfect.
Not because she had insider access.

Because:

* her profile was easy to understand
* her resume spoke recruiter language
* her applications were timed
* her strategy was consistent

That’s how you go from hoping for one offer to choosing between
multiple.

If reading this made you realize you’re doing a lot but not
seeing proportional results, that gap is usually structural.

Inside my Job Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nm4wvblhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/l2hehmhlwg2qedf6/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), we work on this together - resume reviews, focused prep
sessions, and monthly masterclasses with people who’ve actually
hired in these roles.

You don’t need more motivation. You need a repeatable process.

-Yudi J

Special Mention

​Manifest Law (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9nm4wvblhvze4k55hnh7lwrq/m2h7h5h3wr52vxam/aHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5pZmVzdGxhdy5jb20vcGFydG5lci95dWRp
)​

I work closely with the team at Manifest Law, and I regularly

subject: In case you missed: Before you touch your resume, do this
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here.

Before I start, I want to do a special mention.

​MobSquad (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/8ku7lk8eg4hoh2w5vv5ckhk6pm399h3hznwr9/9qhzhnhdn8gq42a9/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9tb2JzcXVhZA==
)​
-----------------------------------------------------------------

We plan careers in quarters.
But we plan our lives in years.

For many international tech professionals in the U.S., those
timelines no longer align.

The work is exciting, the growth is real. Yet major life
decisions often stay on hold because immigration timelines remain
uncertain.

People hesitate to buy homes, delay family plans, and avoid
travel because their status feels fragile.

This is why more tech professionals are exploring MobSquad (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/8ku7lk8eg4hoh2w5vv5ckhk6pm399h3hznwr9/9qhzhnhdn8gq42a9/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9tb2JzcXVhZA==
).

MobSquad helps skilled tech professionals move their careers to
Canada without losing momentum.

They hire you directly, help you relocate to Canada, help secure
a Canadian work permit in 6 to 12 weeks, support permanent
residency in 1 to 2 years, and enable continued work on North
American projects.

For those who want career growth alongside personal stability,
this is a path worth exploring.

You can speak with their team at no cost to see if it fits your
situation.

-->Book a no-cost consultation with MobSquad (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/8ku7lk8eg4hoh2w5vv5ckhk6pm399h3hznwr9/9qhzhnhdn8gq42a9/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9tb2JzcXVhZA==
)
Book a no-cost consultation with MobSquad (
https://link.yudij.com/mobsquad )
​
Today, let me start with something that might feel uncomfortable
if you’ve been job hunting for a while.

Most resumes don’t fail because people lack experience.
They fail because the experience is trapped inside the person’s
head.

I’ve seen this again and again.

Students tell me,
“I know I’ve done good work… I just don’t know how to write it.”

Early in my own journey, I thought resume writing meant
formatting, templates, fonts, and rewriting the same bullet 20
times. I’d open my resume, stare at it, close it, and promise
myself I’d “fix it properly” on the weekend.

The weekend never helped.

What actually helped was realizing this:
​The hard part isn’t writing. It’s remembering.

As a hiring manager I have reviewed thousands of resumes, here's
what I have to say:

“The bottleneck is never formatting.
It’s getting the raw material out of your head.”
That’s exactly what I see with international students too.

You’ve worked on projects.
You’ve solved problems.
You’ve made trade-offs.
You’ve helped teams move forward.

But when it’s time to write, your mind goes blank.

Not because you did nothing.
Because you’re trying to edit while remembering.

That never works.

A better way to build a resume
------------------------------

The system is simple and honestly a bit boring. Which is why it
works.

Instead of “writing a resume,” you answer following questions.

No editing.
No checking notes.
No overthinking.

Just answer each question in about 30 seconds.

That’s it.

20 minutes of dumping what’s in your head onto a page.

Questions like:

* What problems did you solve?
* What are you most proud of?
* What did you improve, fix, or optimize?
* Where did you influence decisions?
* What did you ship, launch, or land?
* What trade-offs did you make?
* Where did you save time, money, or effort?

As you answer, something interesting happens.

You remember things you forgot mattered.
You see patterns in your work.
You realize you did more than you gave yourself credit for.

This is the part most students skip.
And then they wonder why their resume feels empty.

Where AI actually helps (and where it doesn’t)
----------------------------------------------

Once you have those raw answers, then AI becomes useful. Watch my
podcast (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/8ku7lk8eg4hoh2w5vv5ckhk6pm399h3hznwr9/3ohphkh3v8qoxqtr/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dD0zNXMmdj1ILUJKM08xLVhOYw==
) to know more.

You feed your answers into AI with a clear instruction:
Turn this into resume bullets using a simple structure:
Action + Context + Result + Metric.

Now you’re not asking AI to invent your story.
You’re asking it to organize your story.

Big difference.

From there, you stack-rank the bullets.
Put the strongest ones first.
Add a short summary.

And then you ship it.

Not perfect.
Just clear.

This is where most people get stuck
-----------------------------------

They think,
“Let me refine this a bit more.”
“One more rewrite.”
“One more version.”

Weeks pass.

Applications don’t.

One thing I’ve learned from watching hundreds of students go
through this process is this:

Momentum beats perfection every single time.

The students who land interviews aren’t the ones with flawless
resumes.
They’re the ones who apply early, adjust fast, and keep moving.

Why I care about this?
----------------------

Inside my Job Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/8ku7lk8eg4hoh2w5vv5ckhk6pm399h3hznwr9/n2hohvhve4n823c6/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), we don’t start with templates.

We start with clarity.

We break down your experience, pull the raw material out
properly, and then help you turn it into resumes that actually
match roles.

Not one "master resume."
But focused versions that make sense to recruiters.

These question approach is very close to how we do it.
Structure first. Clarity next. Applications after.

Because the goal isn’t a beautiful resume.
The goal is conversations.

If you’re stuck right now
-------------------------

Try this today.

Set a 20-minute timer.
Answer the questions without editing.
Let it be messy.
Then clean it up.

Stop waiting for confidence.
Confidence comes after action, not before.

And if you want guidance while doing this - someone to
sanity-check, structure, and push you when you stall - that’s
exactly what the accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/8ku7lk8eg4hoh2w5vv5ckhk6pm399h3hznwr9/n2hohvhve4n823c6/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
) is built for.

No hype.
Just a system that works when the market is noisy.

-Yudi J

The HireReady Newsletter Vol. 76:

The Art of Career Misdirection

Forget everything you’ve been told about the "right" way to find a job. The truth is, the most effective job search strategies often feel a little bit like cheating. They aren't about breaking the rules, but about understanding them so well that you can bend them to your advantage. This issue is all about the art of career misdirection: clever, unconventional tactics that will make you stand out by doing the opposite of what everyone else is doing.

🛠️ The job search hacks 🛠️

💼 The "Career-Changer's Crystal Ball": Predict Your Future Niche with Jobscan's Skills Data 💼

Everyone tells you to tailor your resume to the job you want. But what if you're not entirely sure what that job is yet? This is especially true for career changers, who often have a wealth of transferable skills but don't know which niche to target.

Instead of guessing, use Jobscan's Career Change tool (https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VXchl693npvPW29yRst30NX29W1qZjFf5J4-LmN3kz7Dx3gP0xW7lCdLW6lZ3kqW4JF5Nx7Tpb8fW8qwpJ08jpCp9VphLNH1RNGvhW91_LzH4nKYcxW4-Zz5K8jBM1qW3SdLfS4srQ4sW8Dh8Qb6DnntXW1b4Wf71bhPW2MHkxZ39b_GDW6lxxJy1sVDDqVx0TdR2Wdxj6W2tMQhz7lg-msW1TvKfH4kkL2dW3VyKbh4BQKQWW2PFcrL8Pg0HXW4XvMtB3Wxcb2W45dlDP36lg5NW8h-bSq3M5FywV2Jbvm1jKySXV-qvmy4QtHq0W59D30D1XBxQ2W1Fjf_q31hNHqW52G66m5xnDffW7Dv3Fb9k06mxf5fzhkg04 ) as a crystal ball to find the most promising career paths for your existing skillset. The trick is to stop thinking about your skills in isolation and start looking for patterns in how they cluster together in high-paying roles.

Next Steps:

- Go to the Jobscan career change tool (https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VXchl693npvPW29yRst30NX29W1qZjFf5J4-LmN3kz7Dx3gP0xW7lCdLW6lZ3kqW4JF5Nx7Tpb8fW8qwpJ08jpCp9VphLNH1RNGvhW91_LzH4nKYcxW4-Zz5K8jBM1qW3SdLfS4srQ4sW8Dh8Qb6DnntXW1b4Wf71bhPW2MHkxZ39b_GDW6lxxJy1sVDDqVx0TdR2Wdxj6W2tMQhz7lg-msW1TvKfH4kkL2dW3VyKbh4BQKQWW2PFcrL8Pg0HXW4XvMtB3Wxcb2W45dlDP36lg5NW8h-bSq3M5FywV2Jbvm1jKySXV-qvmy4QtHq0W59D30D1XBxQ2W1Fjf_q31hNHqW52G66m5xnDffW7Dv3Fb9k06mxf5fzhkg04 ) and upload your resume.
- Identify "bridge" skills - skills you have that frequently appear alongside high-income skills you don't yet have.
- Use this information to identify 2-3 niche roles you're already partially qualified for.
- Focus your upskilling efforts on the few high-income skills you're missing for those specific roles.

Free Tool: Career Change Discovery Report
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VXchl693npvPW29yRst30NX29W1qZjFf5J4-LmN3kz7Dx3gP0xW7lCdLW6lZ3kqW4JF5Nx7Tpb8fW8qwpJ08jpCp9VphLNH1RNGvhW91_LzH4nKYcxW4-Zz5K8jBM1qW3SdLfS4srQ4sW8Dh8Qb6DnntXW1b4Wf71bhPW2MHkxZ39b_GDW6lxxJy1sVDDqVx0TdR2Wdxj6W2tMQhz7lg-msW1TvKfH4kkL2dW3VyKbh4BQKQWW2PFcrL8Pg0HXW4XvMtB3Wxcb2W45dlDP36lg5NW8h-bSq3M5FywV2Jbvm1jKySXV-qvmy4QtHq0W59D30D1XBxQ2W1Fjf_q31hNHqW52G66m5xnDffW7Dv3Fb9k06mxf5fzhkg04 )

🕵️‍♀️ The "Reverse Interview": Qualify Your Future Boss Before They Qualify You 🕵️‍♀️

The biggest mistake job seekers make is acting like they're the only ones being interviewed. This power dynamic is a trap. The reality is, a bad boss can ruin a great job, and you have the power to screen them out before you even get an offer.

The key is to subtly shift the frame of the interview from "Are you good enough for us?" to "Is this environment good enough for me?". You do this by asking thoughtful, slightly challenging questions that reveal the company's culture and your potential manager's leadership style.

Next Steps:

- Before your next interview, prepare three questions that probe for potential red flags. Instead of "What's the company culture like?", ask "Can you tell me about a time a team member disagreed with you and how you handled it?".
- Listen carefully to both what they say and what they don't say. Are their answers vague? Do they get defensive?
- Pay attention to the team dynamics. Do people seem genuinely happy and engaged, or is the atmosphere tense?
- Trust your gut. If something feels off, it probably is. Don't be afraid to walk away from a "great" opportunity if it comes with a toxic boss.

Blog: Learn to Spot the Red Flags
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VXchl693npvPW29yRst30NX29W1qZjFf5J4-LmN3kz7DR3gP0xW7Y8-PT6lZ3q5N43Lk1HnpGx-Vw68006QBG17W1XLdpL4PrywtW2dG9Sx4XTdZBW75LKBb3sSTq3N6jr302b2Yf5W695km47XLYtSW1rw2Yg4Fp_qLW86yR2f8snwx6W7qD4K37fzC_JW4bzDLV3sqpTcW2PQz8g78nZ11Vmb2fF26Ktq8W2TLh541zRnC7W8HmJzN5ZpXjKW5sy3Q-43tl8SW3PggZ-4zjDx-W97Vl5l1hm3SpW7KMZY85l06lMVW3dNJ6QNdfYW8lMczh66t3CdVg6mn-1wBFz-W1SJN8T1vdZw8M1_Rm6W_GCpW4FrVvS2MT31hW3x68SF3VR2WPf3bzmPM04 )

🛠️ The "Weakness Weapon": Turn Your Perceived Flaws into Strengths 🛠️

Every job seeker dreads the "What's your biggest weakness?" question. The standard advice is to give a fake weakness that's actually a strength, like "I'm a perfectionist." This is terrible advice. A much more powerful approach is to be honest about a real weakness, but then reframe it as a strength in a different context. This shows self-awareness, honesty, and the ability to think critically about your own skills.

Next Steps:

- Identify a genuine weakness that isn't a deal-breaker for the role. For example, if you're applying for a creative role, you might admit that you're not the most organized person.
- Prepare a short, compelling story that illustrates how this "weakness" has actually been a strength in a past situation. For example, "While I'm not naturally a 'details' person, this has allowed me to focus on the bigger picture and come up with innovative solutions that others might have missed."
- Show that you're actively working on managing this weakness, for example, by using specific tools or strategies to stay organized.
- Deliver your answer with confidence. The goal is to show that you're not afraid of your imperfections and that you know how to leverage your unique quirks.

Guide: How to Frame Your Skills
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VXchl693npvPW29yRst30NX29W1qZjFf5J4-LmN3kz7DR3gP0xW7Y8-PT6lZ3l4W1Db37p2_P2WZN6NHGC3n206FW7lZ1LZ8YNWGFW1VGNk86Gtp2-VnbHYD2hXT4bW6_ZPWx2sG5JCW3w8PKM1bmg9PW79QSxj4Cc9wdW7y0f-V6fBmMZW3yhTPQ3YmgwMW1mfc7378rgzcW1q3mns8xvYmPN3p-NDFjjfXYW44Jlpd4C1V8FW6YQr876SpR4KW6BdG9l8Ty1w1W3mx4nc6srS4gVcX0Zm10sXVGW5R_hRW18H8khW2sP4h31zNgG7W7h3gw599rqK1Vk5SLR1sx-WjW67X-YB2zcwnlW5Nc6vw8pKSjtW4WHtTZ1-GgvmW3n_qpH1r_KPpf3XV8nj04 )

🤝 The "Anti-Networking" Strategy: Build Real Relationships by Asking for Nothing 🤝

The word "networking" makes most of us cringe. It feels transactional, fake, and exhausting. The "anti-networking" strategy is to flip the script entirely.

Instead of reaching out to people to ask for a job, you reach out to offer something of value with no strings attached. This could be a compliment on a recent project, a helpful article, or an introduction to someone in your network. The goal is to build genuine relationships, not just a list of contacts.

Next Steps:

- Identify 10-15 people in your target industry who you genuinely admire.
- Follow them on social media, read their blog posts, and get a sense of what they're passionate about.
- Find a natural, non-creepy way to offer something of value. For example, if they post about a problem they're having, and you have a potential solution, share it.
- Don't ask for anything in return. The goal is to plant seeds that may or may not grow into job opportunities down the road. The immediate benefit is that you're building a reputation as a generous, helpful person.

Blog: How to Write an Elevator Pitch
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VXchl693npvPW29yRst30NX29W1qZjFf5J4-LmN3kz7Fq3gP0xW95jsWP6lZ3prVddTq325M1JcW6nXVLp5TMbwXW2vnlFV7vdwlhW33G5w55_6spcW67rGjN1W1VbLW301T625cnMsWW36pkBT4HGXPwVF-84J22Pyg0W7fN_yg3pxzvlW253TGq39J7SWW18yWNr3hyTX8W5PKpvZ7F_S6BN5xHpnlFnTnYW6F8F5-7MDGssW6phn7z1vHVQ_W6L-t1f1_xTWWW5g-StC5-l_RcW5t_-6Z1Ls8_GW51cHSS6MjGp8W3P4Lzx7PJ4LCW3YJGt81WKxgzW6pyNDS2ZLm-HVsxqp-1R12GMW6ByDqs32X6YDMxGc0J3JdlMW3Lfv3D1Vh8HqW8jltc54n2r_rW8b-nn54DlXK2W85hcYJ5M4lY_VcvX4p1BTQ3Kf7jBKpY04 )

🤖 The "Resume Decoy": Create a "Boring" Resume to Get Past the Robots 🤖

Everyone is so focused on making their resume "stand out" that they forget the first reader is often a robot. Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) are notoriously bad at parsing creative resumes with fancy formatting, images, or unusual fonts. The "resume decoy" strategy is to have two versions of your resume: a beautifully designed one for human eyes, and a stripped-down, "boring" version specifically for the ATS.

Next Steps:

- Create a plain text version of your resume with no special formatting, columns, or images.
- Use standard, easy-to-read fonts like Arial, Calibri, or Times New Roman.
- Make sure your resume includes the exact keywords from the job description. You can use Jobscan's resume scanner to identify any missing keywords.
- When you apply online, submit the "boring" version of your resume to ensure it gets past the ATS. You can then bring the "pretty" version to the interview.

Guide: How to Convert Your Resume to an ATS-Friendly Format

Dear Customer, 

Fraudsters share fake links on SMS, WhatsApp, or email, posing as bank alerts, e-challans, or official notices.  

The link asks you to download an APK file ( through an app outside the Play Store) , which ,once clicked and installed , can then  
access your  personal and banking details, control your phone, and drain your bank account. 
	
	
	
	

Stay protected from fake APK files by following these 5 habits: 
	
	
	
	

	
	

Download Only from Trusted Sources:  Always install banking apps from official stores like the Google Play Store or Apple App Store. 
	
	
	
	

	
	

Avoid Suspicious Links & Attachments:  Never click on unverified links or download attachments from unknown sources. 
	
	
	
	

	
	

Check App Permissions:  Be cautious of apps that request SMS, call log, or contact access. 
	
	
	
	

	
	

Enable Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA):  Use secure login methods like OTPs, biometrics, or authentication apps. 
	
	
	
	

	
	

Report Fraud Attempts:  Suspected Fake Call, SMS, or WhatsApp? 

Report at  https://t13.mailers.hdfcbank.net/r/?id=t6189cdb7-f5be-4ec8-a97b-ef26a821dad4,1b9964,7c2de https://t13.mailers.hdfcbank.net/r/?id=t6189cdb7-f5be-4ec8-a97b-ef26a821dad4,1b9964,7c2df  (Chakshu portal) or via the Sanchar Saathi app.
