subject: Before you touch your resume, do this
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here.

Before I start, I want to do a special mention.

​MobSquad (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/8ku7lk8eg4hoh2wx2wohkhk6pm399h3hznwr9/08hwh9h2ql04pgal/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9tb2JzcXVhZA==
)​
-----------------------------------------------------------------

We plan careers in quarters.
But we plan our lives in years.

For many international tech professionals in the U.S., those
timelines no longer align.

The work is exciting, the growth is real. Yet major life
decisions often stay on hold because immigration timelines remain
uncertain.

People hesitate to buy homes, delay family plans, and avoid
travel because their status feels fragile.

This is why more tech professionals are exploring MobSquad (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/8ku7lk8eg4hoh2wx2wohkhk6pm399h3hznwr9/08hwh9h2ql04pgal/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9tb2JzcXVhZA==
).

MobSquad helps skilled tech professionals move their careers to
Canada without losing momentum.

They hire you directly, help you relocate to Canada, help secure
a Canadian work permit in 6 to 12 weeks, support permanent
residency in 1 to 2 years, and enable continued work on North
American projects.

For those who want career growth alongside personal stability,
this is a path worth exploring.

You can speak with their team at no cost to see if it fits your
situation.

-->Book a no-cost consultation with MobSquad (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/8ku7lk8eg4hoh2wx2wohkhk6pm399h3hznwr9/08hwh9h2ql04pgal/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9tb2JzcXVhZA==
)
Book a no-cost consultation with MobSquad (
https://link.yudij.com/mobsquad )
​
Today, let me start with something that might feel uncomfortable
if you’ve been job hunting for a while.

Most resumes don’t fail because people lack experience.
They fail because the experience is trapped inside the person’s
head.

I’ve seen this again and again.

Students tell me,
“I know I’ve done good work… I just don’t know how to write it.”

Early in my own journey, I thought resume writing meant
formatting, templates, fonts, and rewriting the same bullet 20
times. I’d open my resume, stare at it, close it, and promise
myself I’d “fix it properly” on the weekend.

The weekend never helped.

What actually helped was realizing this:
​The hard part isn’t writing. It’s remembering.

As a hiring manager I have reviewed thousands of resumes, here's
what I have to say:

“The bottleneck is never formatting.
It’s getting the raw material out of your head.”
That’s exactly what I see with international students too.

You’ve worked on projects.
You’ve solved problems.
You’ve made trade-offs.
You’ve helped teams move forward.

But when it’s time to write, your mind goes blank.

Not because you did nothing.
Because you’re trying to edit while remembering.

That never works.

A better way to build a resume
------------------------------

The system is simple and honestly a bit boring. Which is why it
works.

Instead of “writing a resume,” you answer following questions.

No editing.
No checking notes.
No overthinking.

Just answer each question in about 30 seconds.

That’s it.

20 minutes of dumping what’s in your head onto a page.

Questions like:

* What problems did you solve?
* What are you most proud of?
* What did you improve, fix, or optimize?
* Where did you influence decisions?
* What did you ship, launch, or land?
* What trade-offs did you make?
* Where did you save time, money, or effort?

As you answer, something interesting happens.

You remember things you forgot mattered.
You see patterns in your work.
You realize you did more than you gave yourself credit for.

This is the part most students skip.
And then they wonder why their resume feels empty.

Where AI actually helps (and where it doesn’t)
----------------------------------------------

Once you have those raw answers, then AI becomes useful. Watch my
podcast (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/8ku7lk8eg4hoh2wx2wohkhk6pm399h3hznwr9/vqh3hrho8w26q8hg/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dD0zNXMmdj1ILUJKM08xLVhOYw==
) to know more.

You feed your answers into AI with a clear instruction:
Turn this into resume bullets using a simple structure:
Action + Context + Result + Metric.

Now you’re not asking AI to invent your story.
You’re asking it to organize your story.

Big difference.

From there, you stack-rank the bullets.
Put the strongest ones first.
Add a short summary.

And then you ship it.

Not perfect.
Just clear.

This is where most people get stuck
-----------------------------------

They think,
“Let me refine this a bit more.”
“One more rewrite.”
“One more version.”

Weeks pass.

Applications don’t.

One thing I’ve learned from watching hundreds of students go
through this process is this:

Momentum beats perfection every single time.

The students who land interviews aren’t the ones with flawless
resumes.
They’re the ones who apply early, adjust fast, and keep moving.

Why I care about this?
----------------------

Inside my Job Hunting Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/8ku7lk8eg4hoh2wx2wohkhk6pm399h3hznwr9/l2hehmhlwx5pdqa6/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), we don’t start with templates.

We start with clarity.

We break down your experience, pull the raw material out
properly, and then help you turn it into resumes that actually
match roles.

Not one "master resume."
But focused versions that make sense to recruiters.

These question approach is very close to how we do it.
Structure first. Clarity next. Applications after.

Because the goal isn’t a beautiful resume.
The goal is conversations.

If you’re stuck right now
-------------------------

Try this today.

Set a 20-minute timer.

.



‘Smart bricks’: Legos go digital

Founder Tip: You can't change someone.



This sounds obvious until you're actually hiring. Then, suddenly, you're 
convincing yourself that the smart candidate who seems indifferent will get 
excited once they see the work. Or the talented person who only asks about comp 
will care about the mission later.



They won't.



Shensi Ding <https://www.linkedin.com/in/shensiding/>, Co-Founder of Merge 
<https://wellfound.com/company/mergeapi>, says early-stage hiring is about 
selection, not transformation. You're looking for people who already care, not 
people you can teach to care. 



The mistake most founders make is hiring for potential and then spending six 
months trying to instill drive that was never there to begin with.



Watch the full interview here. <https://youtu.be/J-ehfOjSBBQ>



They're hiring! See 2 open roles here. <https://wellfound.com/company/mergeapi>




 <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5Cf3YmMX_JE>

Startups Hiring Now  <https://wellfound.com/company/oklo>



Current

We're on a mission to change our members' lives by creating better financial 
outcomes

See 21 open roles <https://wellfound.com/company/current>



Industrious

Finding and creating workplace experiences that are optimized for your 
business needs

See 69 open roles <https://wellfound.com/company/industrious>



Anything

Your AI product engineer

See 7 open roles <https://wellfound.com/company/anythingdotcom>



Parallel Learning

We are the first human-centered care solution for students with learning 
differences

See 22 open roles <https://wellfound.com/company/parallel-learning-1>




Job Search Tip: Show you can build with AI, not just talk about it


Chase Johnson <https://www.linkedin.com/in/chasetalent/>, now at Primer 
<https://wellfound.com/company/primer-usa> after scaling Replit 
<https://wellfound.com/company/repl-it>'s team, says recruiters don't want to 
hear you explain how models work. They want proof you built something that 
solved a real problem. 


Chase's take: "If you're not using AI to tinker and solve problems in your 
daily work, you're probably behind."


He's a recruiter, not an engineer. He built an equity calculator because he 
kept needing one.



That's the bar: you saw a problem, you learned enough to fix it, you shipped 
something. The companies hiring right now want people who already think that 
way.


 Watch Chase's full Ask a Recruiter session here. 
<https://youtu.be/_K3-odCWhkE>



Next week: Interview Red & Green Flags for Engineers, Data Scientists & PMs 
withMatt Glickman <https://www.linkedin.com/in/mattglickman/> (Live Q&A) · Jan 
13, 4 PM ET →Sign up <https://wellfound.typeform.com/mattglickman> 




 <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fSTMt3r7sgc>





What We're Reading



Claude’s ‘do more with less’ workflow

Claude’s creator revealed a do-more-with-less workflow 
<https://venturebeat.com/technology/the-creator-of-claude-code-just-revealed-his-workflow-and-developers-are>
 running 5 agents at once. "If you're not reading the Claude Code best 
practices straight from its creator, you're behind as a programmer," one 
developer says.



Seeking: ‘AI forward’ candidates

AI isn’t slowing hiring but is changing who they look for, says the CEO of GPU 
maker AMD. “We’re hiring people who areAI forward 
<https://www.cnbc.com/2026/01/06/amd-lisa-su-ai-jobs-hiring.html>,” she says.



OpenAI unveils ChatGPT Health

230M+ people ask ChatGPT health questions each week, leading OpenAI to roll out
ChatGPT Health 
<https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/07/openai-unveils-chatgpt-health-says-230-million-users-ask-about-health-each-week/>
 to connect medical records.

A hacker dressed as a Power Ranger

subject: In case you missed: He got hired in his first month in the U.S.
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here,

I recently filmed a podcast with Anand (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/k0u59w0dvnf6h5zeo73flho9x0577a8hd5738/6qheh8hl08r074to/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj00UzlUeld6SGhnNA==
), and his job search stood out for one simple reason:

He was prepared before he landed in the U.S.

Not "thinking about job hunting."
Not "starting after classes begin."
Actually prepared.

He landed in the U.S. and had an offer in the same month.

This came after roughly 300 targeted applications, which led to 5
serious interview calls, 2 internships, and eventually one
full-time offer - all while staying on track to graduate
debt-free.
In a brutal market.
As an international student.

That doesn’t happen by accident.

Here’s what he did differently and why it matters if you’re job
hunting right now.

1. He treated job hunting as a pre-landing responsibility
---------------------------------------------------------

Before Anand even boarded his flight, he had already:

* finalized his resume
* optimized LinkedIn for recruiter searches
* shortlisted roles and companies
* understood what skills those roles actually required

So when he landed, he wasn’t “getting started.” He was executing.

Most students lose their first 6-8 weeks just settling in.
Anand used those weeks to apply and interview.

That head start is often the difference between panic and
progress.

This is also why, inside my accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/k0u59w0dvnf6h5zeo73flho9x0577a8hd5738/kkhmh6hn40w4l7bl/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), I push students to finish all setup before landing, not after
the semester overwhelms them.

2. He didn’t market himself like a student
------------------------------------------

Anand already had prior industry experience.
He made sure recruiters saw that immediately.

His resume didn’t read like:
"Master’s student seeking opportunities"

It read like:
"Professional with relevant experience, currently upgrading
skills"

That framing matters.

It changes:

* the kind of interviews you get
* how interviewers speak to you
* what they expect from you

Same background.
Different positioning.
Very different outcomes.

3. He applied early and consistently, not emotionally
-----------------------------------------------------

Anand applied to around 300 application., but more importantly:

* he applied early
* he didn’t over-edit every resume
* he didn’t wait for referrals to start applying

Those 300 applications turned into 5 real callbacks.
From those, he secured 2 internships, one of which played a key
role in his full-time conversion.

He didn’t wait for referrals to start applying.
He kept the pipeline moving.

4. He didn’t spiral after rejections
------------------------------------

This is underrated.

Even with strong alignment, Anand faced rejections.
Instead of rewriting his entire strategy every time, he stayed
steady.

Apply -> Prep->Interview->Iterate.

When your numbers make sense, rejections stop feeling personal.

5. He prepared for interviews with intent, not fear
---------------------------------------------------

When interview calls came in, Anand didn’t prepare "everything."

He:

* studied the job description line by line
* mapped his experience directly to what the role needed
* ignored things that weren’t relevant

That made his prep focused and efficient.

Generic prep creates anxiety.
Targeted prep creates confidence.

This is something we repeatedly see with candidates who convert
interviews faster.

6. He made trade-offs early to win faster
-----------------------------------------

Instead of loading up on multiple on-campus jobs, Anand chose to
protect time for:

* applications
* interview prep
* rest

That decision helped him convert faster and, importantly,
graduate without debt.

Short-term comfort vs long-term stability.
He chose deliberately.

I asked Anand what he’d tell someone job hunting right now. His
advice was simple:

* Start preparing before you feel ready
* Don’t wait to "settle in" before applying
* Apply early, even if your resume isn’t perfect
* Expect rejection and don’t overreact to it
* Focus on one offer, not everyone else’s timeline

He said:

"You don’t need everything to work. You just need one offer to
work."
That mindset keeps people moving when others burn out.

What this means for you
-----------------------

In this market:

* preparation beats panic
* systems beat motivation
* speed beats perfection

If your job search feels reactive or scattered, that’s not a
personal failure.
It’s usually a missing structure problem.

That’s exactly what we work on inside the Job Hunting Accelerator
(
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/k0u59w0dvnf6h5zeo73flho9x0577a8hd5738/kkhmh6hn40w4l7bl/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
) - building a clear, repeatable process so you’re not guessing
every week.

If you want to hear Anand explain his preparation, interviews,
and decisions in his own words, you can watch our full
conversation here:

You can watch my conversation with Anand here → Watch here (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/k0u59w0dvnf6h5zeo73flho9x0577a8hd5738/6qheh8hl08r074to/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj00UzlUeld6SGhnNA==
).

-Yudi J

The latest AI news we announced in December
email_text:
The Monthly AI Recap

By Google


By Google

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/latest-ai-news-we-announced-december-google-huzie?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3ByVNTuVFQQwWg68HL6da3CQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQEIM4HzHiyK2w&midSig=25duYaNQP4zI41&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-ghmp60~mk31vfm8~jx-null-null&eid=ghmp60-mk31vfm8-jx&otpToken=MWIwYzE5ZTAxNTJmY2FjN2IyMjQwNGVkNDAxOWUwYjc4ZGM5ZDQ0NDljYTk4YjYxNzljNDA3NmI0YjU5NWZmYWY3ZDFkZjk1NDFjZmU2ZmM3ZmJjZDI0NWVjNzFiYjRjOTRjZDM5MTczMzc2N2JkMDZlZWI1NCwxLDE%3D
  
Want to keep up with all the AI news coming out of Google? We've got you covered. This new monthly digest breaks down the most important updates and insights, all in one place. Hit the subscribe button to follow along! December is usually a time for reflection, and looking ahead. That’s one reason why we've been focused on taking frontier intelligence out of the lab and putting it into your hands in ways that actually matter for your day-to-day. Whether it’s the lightning speed of Gemini 3 Flash helping you tackle tasks in seconds, the new video verification tools in the Gemini app or the simple relief of having GenTabs tame your open tabs, these updates share a single goal: making technology adapt to you, not the other way around. And as we push these boundaries, we’re staying grounded in responsibility — launching new tools to help you verify AI content so you can explore this new frontier with confidence. We released Gemini 3 Flash, featuring frontier intelligence built for speed. Gemini 3 Flash brings frontier intelligence to virtually every corner of the Google ecosystem, combining the speed of our most advanced models with improved reasoning capabilities to help with everyday tasks, all while keeping costs significantly lower. It's rolling out as the default model in the Gemini app and AI Mode in Search so people everywhere can now experience the incredible reasoning of our frontier model, right in our consumer products. And we’ve scaled this rollout to a global community, including developers building in the API Antigravity, our new agentic development platform, and enterprise customers on Vertex AI.We added new AI verification tools for videos in the Gemini app. We’re bringing video verification capabilities directly to the Gemini app. People can now upload videos — up to 100 MB or 90 seconds — and simply ask if the content was generated or edited using Google AI. Gemini uses imperceptible SynthID watermarks to analyze both audio and visual tracks, pinpointing exactly which segments contain AI-generated elements. We added new AI verification tools for videos in the Gemini app. We’re bringing video verification capabilities directly to the Gemini app. People can now upload videos — up to 100 MB or 90 seconds — and simply ask if the content was generated or edited using Google AI. Gemini uses imperceptible SynthID watermarks to analyze both audio and visual tracks, pinpointing exactly which segments contain AI-generated elements. We announced a new experiment to improve browsing and manage complex online tasks. We’ve all felt the friction of juggling dozens of tabs to research a topic or plan a trip. Enter Disco, a new browsing experience from Google Labs designed to tame that complexity. Disco features GenTabs, an experiment that proactively synthesizes your open tabs and chat history to build custom, interactive web applications — transforming a scattered browser session into a streamlined tool for getting things done. We upgraded Gemini audio models for powerful voice interactions. The updated Gemini 2.5 Flash Native Audio is built to handle complex workflows and natural dialogue — meaning smoother conversations, higher accuracy and better responsiveness to instructions. It’s available now in AI Studio, Vertex AI, Gemini Live and, for the first time, Search Live. Plus, a new live speech translation beta in the Google Translate app brings live translation in 70+ languages directly to your headphones, preserving original intonation and pacing to unlock truly global communication. We released a new Gemini Deep Research agent. We brought a more powerful Gemini Deep Research to developers through the Interactions API. Developers can now embed advanced research capabilities — like navigating complex topics and synthesizing findings — directly into their own applications using a Gemini API key from Google AI Studio. We’ve also open-sourced our new DeepSearchQA benchmark, offering a transparent way to test just how comprehensive and effective research agents can be on web tasks. Plus, we shared how developers are already building mobile-first solutions to address real-world problems, from AI assistants for the visually impaired to tools fostering autonomy for people with cognitive disabilities. We released a new way for shoppers in the U.S. to use our virtual try-on tool. U.S. shoppers now have a more personalized way to find their next favorite outfit with our updated virtual try-on tool. Instead of needing a full-body photo, you can now upload a simple selfie and Nano Banana will generate a realistic, full-body digital version of you. Once you’ve selected your preferred studio-like image and clothing size, you can instantly see how you’d look in billions of products from our Shopping Graph. We expanded Gemini 3 Pro and Nano Banana Pro in Search. We brought our most intelligent model, Gemini 3, to AI Mode in Google Search in nearly 120 countries and territories in English. Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers can visualize complex topics with Gemini 3 Pro by tapping “Thinking with 3 Pro” in the model drop-down in AI Mode. We also brought our generative imagery model, Nano Banana Pro, to AI Mode in more countries in English, starting with Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers. For those in the U.S., we also expanded access to these Pro models (no subscription required), with higher usage limits for Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers. We released the top YouTube trends of 2025 and first-ever personal Recap. YouTube celebrated its 20th birthday by looking back at 2025. MrBeast was the top creator for the sixth year running, while Rosé and Bruno Mars’ track "APT." became the fastest KPop video to hit one billion views. To mark the occasion, YouTube is launching its first-ever Recap so you can see a personalized summary of your year. We added new ways for you to personalize, create and share your Google Photos Recap. Google Photos Recap has returned to help you celebrate your favorite moments from 2025, now with more features to make the experience truly yours. We’ve added new controls that let you hide specific people or photos, ensuring your trip down memory lane is exactly how you want it. Plus, you can now get creative with exclusive templates in CapCut and easily share your finished masterpiece directly to WhatsApp or your favorite social feeds. We released Year in Search 2025. 2025 delivered history-making headlines — from the first American Pope to the global obsession with "KPop Demon Hunters" — but the quietest revolution happened right at our fingertips. Thanks to AI, this was the year we saw a massive shift toward natural, conversational questions with a surge in queries like “How do I…” and “What’s the deal with…” as AI helped technology finally catch up to the way we think.

Catch all the announcements from Amazon at CES by visiting Amazon News. Introducing Alexa.com, a completely new way to interact with Alexa+ Alexa+ is taking a major step forward with Alexa.com, a new web experience that puts the AI assistant right in your browser. Available now for all Alexa+ Early Access customers, this expansion means you can access Alexa's full capabilities whether you're at home, on your phone, or working at your computer. Since launching nine months ago, Alexa+ has grown rapidly with customers having twice as many conversations, making three times more purchases, and requesting recipes five times more often. Alexa.com builds on this momentum by offering seamless integration across all your devices. You can plan meals and automatically add ingredients to your Amazon Fresh cart, upload documents for Alexa to action on and add relevant events to your calendar, control your smart home devices, customize recipes for your dietary restrictions and pick up the cooking instructions on your kitchen Echo device. A navigation sidebar for quick access to recent chats, smart home controls, calendars, shopping lists, and more, making it easier to move between tasks without losing your place. Read more Amazon’s new Fire TV user interface gets you to what you want to watch—even faster Amazon is making it easier than ever to find what you want to watch with a completely redesigned Fire TV experience launching in February. The new user interface is cleaner, faster, and better organized, with some cases showing speed improvements of 20-30%. Now it’s faster and easier to find movies, TV shows, sports, news, and live content across all your subscriptions. The Fire TV team also introduced Amazon's first lifestyle TV, the Amazon Ember Artline, featuring a matte 4K QLED screen designed to display art and photos beautifully while reducing glare. This innovative TV includes an all-new AI-powered room matching feature that recommends artwork based on the style and decor of your room, plus access to over 2,000 pieces of free art. With enhanced Alexa+ integration, you can jump directly to specific movie scenes, create photo slideshows, or get personalized viewing recommendations through natural conversation. Read more Alexa+ expands to Samsung TVs, BMW cars, and more in 2026 Alexa+ is expanding its reach in 2026 through partnerships with Samsung, BMW, Bosch, and other leading brands. Samsung smart TV owners will be the first to experience Alexa+ built into a non-Amazon device, enabling voice-controlled entertainment and smart home management through natural conversations. Customers can discover new shows, control connected devices and services, and play music directly from their televisions. The automotive industry is also getting smarter with BMW's new iX3 electric SUV featuring Alexa Custom Assistant technology. In-vehicle passengers can have actual conversations with their car using natural language, saying things like "Hey BMW, I am feeling a bit chilly could you warm it up in here" without memorizing specific commands. Additional partnerships with Bosch for coffee machines and Oura for health tracking demonstrate how Alexa+ is becoming more integrated into daily routines, whether at home, in the car, or on the go. Read more More from Amazon at CES: CES 2026: Key announcements from Amazon Building Bee at Amazon Ring Unveils a New Generation of Smarter, More Connected Security at CES 2026 Ring and Watch Duty Launch New ‘Fire Watch’ Feature to Help Communities Stay Informed During Wildfires

subject: 5 Formulas Founders Are Using to Grow Their Marketing & Advertising Agencies with AI
email_text:
The Hot Mess

By Aastha Verma ‘Amanat’


By Aastha Verma ‘Amanat’

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/5-formulas-founders-using-grow-marketing-advertising-verma-amanat--eawqf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BdQXHUEtDR1qaWgAmF9GDZw%3D%3D&midToken=AQEIM4HzHiyK2w&midSig=2JYBAw8uPUyY41&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-ghmp60~mk2cv5ij~f-null-null&eid=ghmp60-mk2cv5ij-f&otpToken=MWIwYzE5ZTAxNTJmY2FjN2IyMjQwNGVkNDAxOWUxYmM4NmM2ZDA0MDllYWI4ZTYxNzljNDA3NmI0YjU5NWZmYWY3ZDFkZmI5MTFmOWJjZGE0MDlkZjQyMDFjZDAxZGQ3NmI3NzIxNWYxMjk3NmY0NzQ1MWQ1NSwxLDE%3D
  
AI isn’t a buzzword. It’s already shaping how growth actually happens, from ideation and creativity to personalisation and scaling. The agencies and brands winning today aren’t just using tools. They are embedding AI into systems, strategy, and client value delivery. Here are five formulas top founders and brands are using, backed by real-world case studies that have been published and certified by credible media and industry reports. Formula 1: AI + Human Creativity = Production That Scales Case in point: Untold Fable × L’Oréal (Essie Campaign) For its Essie nail polish brand, L'Oréal partnered with Untold Fable, a content company that used AI tools including OpenAI and Stability AI to produce high-quality visuals without a traditional film crew. This reduced cost, time, and complexity while maintaining creative impact. Read More What this teaches agency founders: Use AI to generate first-pass concepts and assets Keep humans in charge of brand vision, emotional resonance, and final edits This combination accelerates production and gives clients more ideas in less time. Formula 2: Personalisation at Scale = Higher Engagement Example: IBM using Adobe Firefly IBM tested Adobe Firefly to generate over 200 images with more than 1,000 visual variations for personalised marketing. The result was engagement up to 26x higher than their usual campaigns. Read More What founders should learn: Personalisation drives attention and performance AI enables scale without exploding costs Engagement improves when content feels relevant, not generic This is where agencies move from “creative vendors” to growth partners. Formula 3: AI Automation = Better Client ROI Example: LTV.ai LTV.ai raised over $5 million in Series A funding by helping brands like Fable-tics, Sur La Table, and Backcountry send highly personalised post-purchase messages using AI. These messages improved repeat purchases and customer lifetime value without increasing manual effort. Read More Agency insight: AI-powered automation creates sticky services Retention-focused offerings are easier to sell and harder to replace Clients care about outcomes, not dashboards Formula 4: AI as a Co-Creator, Not Just a Tool Example: Coca-Cola’s “Create Real Magic” The Coca-Cola Company launched an AI platform allowing creators to generate artwork using its iconic brand assets. The campaign resulted in over 120,000 pieces of user-generated content and massive global engagement. Read More What agencies can apply: Use AI to invite participation, not just push content Turn audiences into collaborators Build brand affinity, not just impressions This is branding with involvement, not interruption. Formula 5: Predictive AI = Smarter Customer Experiences Example: Starbucks Starbucks uses AI-driven predictive analytics to recommend products and offers based on individual customer behaviour. This has significantly improved engagement and loyalty across digital channels. Read More Agency takeaway: Predictive AI improves timing, relevance, and conversion Smart recommendations outperform broad messaging This can be packaged as a premium service for clients Why This Matters for Agency Founders These formulas are not theoretical. They are real, published examples showing measurable impact in speed, engagement, personalisation, and revenue. The agencies succeeding with AI are not chasing trends. They are the ones who: Build systems before tools Sell strategy, not just execution Combine data with human insight AI will not replace agencies. It will expose agencies without structure. What Founders Should Do Next Audit where time is being wasted Build repeatable frameworks before experimenting with tools Train teams to think strategically, not just produce faster Position AI as infrastructure, not a shortcut Show clients real metrics, not buzzwords AI is not your competitive edge.

subject: He got hired in his first month in the U.S.
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Happy Monday, Yudi here.

I recently filmed a podcast with Anand (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/v8uq2vdr74hrhvmve3eaghv3xwmllh9hro8nx/6qheh8hl0eqprlfo/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj00UzlUeld6SGhnNA==
), and his job search stood out for one simple reason:

He was prepared before he landed in the U.S.

Not "thinking about job hunting."
Not "starting after classes begin."
Actually prepared.

He landed in the U.S. and had an offer in the same month.

This came after roughly 300 targeted applications, which led to 5
serious interview calls, 2 internships, and eventually one
full-time offer - all while staying on track to graduate
debt-free.
In a brutal market.
As an international student.

That doesn’t happen by accident.

Here’s what he did differently and why it matters if you’re job
hunting right now.

1. He treated job hunting as a pre-landing responsibility
---------------------------------------------------------

Before Anand even boarded his flight, he had already:

* finalized his resume
* optimized LinkedIn for recruiter searches
* shortlisted roles and companies
* understood what skills those roles actually required

So when he landed, he wasn’t “getting started.” He was executing.

Most students lose their first 6-8 weeks just settling in.
Anand used those weeks to apply and interview.

That head start is often the difference between panic and
progress.

This is also why, inside my accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/v8uq2vdr74hrhvmve3eaghv3xwmllh9hro8nx/kkhmh6hn4v78w6al/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), I push students to finish all setup before landing, not after
the semester overwhelms them.

2. He didn’t market himself like a student
------------------------------------------

Anand already had prior industry experience.
He made sure recruiters saw that immediately.

His resume didn’t read like:
"Master’s student seeking opportunities"

It read like:
"Professional with relevant experience, currently upgrading
skills"

That framing matters.

It changes:

* the kind of interviews you get
* how interviewers speak to you
* what they expect from you

Same background.
Different positioning.
Very different outcomes.

3. He applied early and consistently, not emotionally
-----------------------------------------------------

Anand applied to around 300 application., but more importantly:

* he applied early
* he didn’t over-edit every resume
* he didn’t wait for referrals to start applying

Those 300 applications turned into 5 real callbacks.
From those, he secured 2 internships, one of which played a key
role in his full-time conversion.

He didn’t wait for referrals to start applying.
He kept the pipeline moving.

4. He didn’t spiral after rejections
------------------------------------

This is underrated.

Even with strong alignment, Anand faced rejections.
Instead of rewriting his entire strategy every time, he stayed
steady.

Apply -> Prep->Interview->Iterate.

When your numbers make sense, rejections stop feeling personal.

5. He prepared for interviews with intent, not fear
---------------------------------------------------

When interview calls came in, Anand didn’t prepare "everything."

He:

* studied the job description line by line
* mapped his experience directly to what the role needed
* ignored things that weren’t relevant

That made his prep focused and efficient.

Generic prep creates anxiety.
Targeted prep creates confidence.

This is something we repeatedly see with candidates who convert
interviews faster.

6. He made trade-offs early to win faster
-----------------------------------------

Instead of loading up on multiple on-campus jobs, Anand chose to
protect time for:

* applications
* interview prep
* rest

That decision helped him convert faster and, importantly,
graduate without debt.

Short-term comfort vs long-term stability.
He chose deliberately.

I asked Anand what he’d tell someone job hunting right now. His
advice was simple:

* Start preparing before you feel ready
* Don’t wait to "settle in" before applying
* Apply early, even if your resume isn’t perfect
* Expect rejection and don’t overreact to it
* Focus on one offer, not everyone else’s timeline

He said:

"You don’t need everything to work. You just need one offer to
work."
That mindset keeps people moving when others burn out.

What this means for you
-----------------------

In this market:

* preparation beats panic
* systems beat motivation
* speed beats perfection

If your job search feels reactive or scattered, that’s not a
personal failure.
It’s usually a missing structure problem.

That’s exactly what we work on inside the Job Hunting Accelerator
(
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/v8uq2vdr74hrhvmve3eaghv3xwmllh9hro8nx/kkhmh6hn4v78w6al/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
) - building a clear, repeatable process so you’re not guessing
every week.

If you want to hear Anand explain his preparation, interviews,
and decisions in his own words, you can watch our full
conversation here:

You can watch my conversation with Anand here → Watch here (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/v8uq2vdr74hrhvmve3eaghv3xwmllh9hro8nx/6qheh8hl0eqprlfo/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj00UzlUeld6SGhnNA==
).

-Yudi J

subject: 10 Gemini prompts to help you keep your New Year’s resolutions
email_text:
The Monthly AI Recap

By Google


By Google

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/10-gemini-prompts-help-you-keep-your-new-years-resolutions-google-tpwne?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3Bg5bHFbJ8QTKSd5KLOoXj6g%3D%3D&midToken=AQEIM4HzHiyK2w&midSig=2AjKhdUXdpyc41&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-ghmp60~mk1fcuih~8z-null-null&eid=ghmp60-mk1fcuih-8z&otpToken=MWIwYzE5ZTAxNTJmY2FjN2IyMjQwNGVkNDAxOWUxYjc4YmNmZDU0ODlhYWI4OTYxNzljNDA3NmI0YjU5NWZmYWY3ZDFkZjgzNDVjNmVkZDc3NGI5ZmY0NmM4OGM1MTkxMTZjMWUzYjY4ZWI3NWIzOTEzOTgxYywxLDE%3D
  
When New Year’s resolutions don’t work out, a lack of planning is often to blame. This year, Gemini can help you fix that. Using the right prompts, scheduled actions and connected apps, you can quickly create detailed guides and structured learning paths that fit your life. Here are 10 prompts you can adapt to help you stick to your resolutions. As you build momentum, you can adjust the prompts, ask Gemini to add tasks to your calendar to stay organized and even create your own Gems, custom versions of Gemini that remember your goals and act as dedicated experts. That way, you’ll never need to start from scratch. 1. Create a custom workout plan. Give Gemini your starting point and your goal to get a step-by-step schedule that builds over time. Use personalization features to reference past chats for an even more tailored plan. You can even ask it to save specific instructions — i.e., Remember I live in NYC and only have access to a treadmill. Prompt: I want to run the New York City marathon in 6 months. I recently finished a half marathon in 2 hours, and I can train 5 days a week. Create a monthly training plan that will help me continue to build my endurance. 2. Declutter your home. Use Gemini to create a structured, daily plan that makes organizational projects feel less overwhelming. Supplement your project using Gemini Live with camera sharing to get real-time feedback. Prompt: I want to organize and declutter my apartment this month. Create a 30-day plan that breaks the process into simple, daily tasks that take no more than 20 minutes each and won’t require me to purchase any new items. 3. Learn a new language. Tell Gemini your goal and available time, and it will structure a curriculum and generate interactive study tools to keep you motivated and test your progress. Prompt: I’d like to learn basic French. Generate a 30-day guided learning plan for these topics and create an interactive multiple-choice quiz on the verb conjugations. Please also generate a set of flashcards for the 50 phrases. 4. Advance your career. Use Gemini to refine your interview skills, analyze a presentation video and generate a professional headshot. Bonus step: Upload a headshot to Gemini and use Nano Banana to add some professional polish for platforms like LinkedIn. Prompt: I have a job interview next week for [role]. Simulate a live, conversational interview with me, focusing on behavioral questions. Second, create a detailed feedback report after I upload my video rehearsal, analyzing my speaking pace, body language and use of filler words. 5. Eat healthier. Ask Gemini to schedule a weekly meal plan tailored to your specific dietary needs and preferences, eliminating the stress of deciding what to cook. Prompt: I want to eat healthier. Every Sunday at 9 a.m., please create a 7-day, high-protein and vegetarian-friendly dinner plan. I have about 45 minutes to cook each night. Include a grocery list for the week. 6. Create a better budget. By asking Gemini to analyze spending data from connected apps, you can get specific, actionable and personalized budgeting rules to implement weekly. Prompt: Analyze the uploaded Google Sheet containing my monthly spending for the last quarter and identify 3 areas of non-essential spending. Suggest practical weekly budgeting rules I can immediately implement to save 15%. 7. Create a custom reading list. Combine expert recs with a manageable pace, keeping you from getting overwhelmed by a backlog of books. Use recurring actions to get a fresh set of recommendations every month. Prompt: I want to read 12 business books in 2026. Schedule a recurring action for the first of every month: Suggest a list of 5 non-fiction titles related to advertising and create a sample monthly reading and review schedule. 8. Replace a bad habit with a good one. ID the triggers for your bad habits and find concrete replacements. Prompt: I want to reduce my phone screen time by 2 hours a week. Outline a 'Habit Replacement Plan' that identifies my typical trigger times and suggests a positive alternative activity for each. 9. Overcome the blank page. Use this prompt to get low-friction creative ideas that help you defeat "blank page" syndrome and keep your new creative habit going. Prompt: Give me 7 unique, one-sentence journaling prompts for the week that encourage reflection on my goals and progress. 10. Plan for long-term success. This future-facing prompt encourages you to think long-term, helping you scale up successful habits and make your resolution a permanent lifestyle change. Prompt: It's July 1st. I've successfully maintained my resolution to [resolution name] for 6 months. Create a 3-part maintenance plan to help me make this a permanent habit and set a bigger goal for the rest of 2026. A good plan can be the difference between a goal and a long-term habit. This year, instead of starting with a vague idea, give Gemini the details to build a roadmap you can actually follow.

Every 365 days, we get the chance to close one chapter and begin another—celebrating what went well, letting go of what didn’t, and setting intentions for what’s next. Whether that means a new job, a lifestyle change, or a move to a new place, I hope the year ahead meets your wishes with a bit of luck and a lot of possibility. subject: In case you missed: Read this before you set goals for the year
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here.

Before anything else, I want to say this.

I’m glad you’re here.

Still curious. Still trying. Still reading on January 3rd.
That already tells me something about you.

This time of year is full of loud promises.
Big declarations. Big goals. Big energy.

But most meaningful change doesn’t start loudly.
It starts quietly, with a small decision to keep going.

I want this note to be that kind of beginning.

If you’re an international student or job seeker in the US (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/p9upzlmrv4i9h2rkwpmsqhpdkx933hrhkp738/48hvhehmkvv6qvtx/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj04Rzl4ZjcxVm9Ncw==
), this year doesn’t just represent “new goals.”

It represents:
new chances
new timelines
new expectations
new pressure

And yet, also new learning.

Every conversation you had last year.
Every rejection that taught you what doesn’t work.
Every small win you didn’t celebrate enough.

None of that disappears because the calendar changed.

You’re not starting from zero.
You’re starting from experience.

One thing I’ve learned from working with students over the years
is this:

The people who do well aren’t the most confident on January 1st.
They’re the ones who stay consistent after January 15th.

They don’t try to overhaul everything.
They don’t chase motivation.
They build a rhythm they can return to even on heavy days.

That’s what I hope for you this year.
Not intensity. Not perfection.

Just steadiness.

If you’re job hunting, here’s a gentle way to think about the
months ahead.

Focus less on “breaking through”
and more on “showing up well.”

Clear roles.
Intentional applications.
Thoughtful conversations.
Enough rest to keep going.

Progress often looks boring while it’s happening.
That doesn’t make it meaningless.

This year doesn’t need a dramatic version of you.

It needs the same you,
a little clearer,
a little more patient,
a little more honest about what you want.

If you keep showing up with that energy, things move.
Sometimes slowly.
But they move.

I’m looking forward to walking this year with you.

Happy New Year.
The quiet, steady kind.

- Yudi J

Founder Tip: Focus on the things you can control.

As we head into 2026, the speculation is already flying. AI is a bubble. 
Fintech is dead. Markets are unpredictable. Everyone has a take.

Ofek Lavian <https://www.linkedin.com/in/ofeklavian/>, Co-founder & CEO of 
Forage <https://www.joinforage.com/>, is not surprised. He did expect an AI 
bubble. What he was more certain of was the doubt that would follow. He has 
seen this cycle before.

 His take is straightforward: speculators are often wrong, bubbles burst, and 
the businesses that survive tend to be the ones built around needs that do not 
depend on sentiment.

Forage works on an evergreen problem. It helps families access government 
benefits, such as EBT and SNAP. People always need to eat, and the system 
remains difficult to navigate.

So as we head into 2026: Lead with conviction. Focus on your mission. Do the 
work.



Watch the full interview here. <https://youtu.be/gIsbduZoJM0>

Job Search Tip: The break is the best time to fix your resume.



Hilary Wagner <https://www.linkedin.com/in/hilarywagner/>, Head of Talent 
Acquisition (Advisory) atRare Compute <https://www.rarecompute.io/>, on what 
recruiters actually need to see. Outreach slows during the holidays, making 
this a good time to revisit your resume and make sure your skills clearly match 
the role you are applying for.

“Candidates are doing themselves a disservice by not being thoughtful about 
that. We want to see the skills on the resume. If you’re that careless, why 
would we want you to work at our company?”

She is direct because this is what the first screen requires. In this market, 
if your resume doesn’t make your qualifications obvious, it usually doesn’t 
move forward.



Watch the full 'Ask a Recruiter' session here <https://youtu.be/jCLwdodhp7o>.

subject: This year doesn’t need more pressure
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here.

​A small update to start the year (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/wvuvqz4n36ughk8z6r7b7hnq2okxxs8h6v89o/vqh3hrho886er7bg/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
)​

We’re starting this 2026 with clarity.

No rush. No pressure.
Just the choice to move forward, one step at a time.

If you know your job search needs direction this year but you’re
not sure where to begin, I’ve opened my Job Hunting Accelerator
at 50% off for the next 24 hours.

Use code December50 (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/wvuvqz4n36ughk8z6r7b7hnq2okxxs8h6v89o/vqh3hrho886er7bg/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
) at checkout.

This is only open today.

Whatever this year brings, I’m rooting for you.

-->Join the Accelerator: Get 50% Off (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/wvuvqz4n36ughk8z6r7b7hnq2okxxs8h6v89o/vqh3hrho886er7bg/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
)
Join the Accelerator: Get 50% Off (
https://link.yudij.com/newsletteremail )
​
Before anything else, I want to say this.

I’m glad you’re here.

Still curious. Still trying. Still reading on January 1st.
That already tells me something about you.

This time of year is full of loud promises.
Big declarations. Big goals. Big energy.

But most meaningful change doesn’t start loudly.
It starts quietly, with a small decision to keep going.

I want this note to be that kind of beginning.

If you’re an international student or job seeker in the US (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/wvuvqz4n36ughk8z6r7b7hnq2okxxs8h6v89o/l2hehmhlwwp67mf6/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g_dj04Rzl4ZjcxVm9Ncw==
), this year doesn’t just represent “new goals.”

It represents:
new chances
new timelines
new expectations
new pressure

And yet, also new learning.

Every conversation you had last year.
Every rejection that taught you what doesn’t work.
Every small win you didn’t celebrate enough.

None of that disappears because the calendar changed.

You’re not starting from zero.
You’re starting from experience.

One thing I’ve learned from working with students over the years
is this:

The people who do well aren’t the most confident on January 1st.
They’re the ones who stay consistent after January 15th.

They don’t try to overhaul everything.
They don’t chase motivation.
They build a rhythm they can return to even on heavy days.

That’s what I hope for you this year.
Not intensity. Not perfection.

Just steadiness.

If you’re job hunting, here’s a gentle way to think about the
months ahead.

Focus less on “breaking through”
and more on “showing up well.”

Clear roles.
Intentional applications.
Thoughtful conversations.
Enough rest to keep going.

Progress often looks boring while it’s happening.
That doesn’t make it meaningless.

This year doesn’t need a dramatic version of you.

It needs the same you,
a little clearer,
a little more patient,
a little more honest about what you want.

If you keep showing up with that energy, things move.
Sometimes slowly.
But they move.

I’m looking forward to walking this year with you.

Happy New Year.
The quiet, steady kind.

- Yudi J

email_text:
Email_newsletter_1748020047518-04 (https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWc8Fm2_TV2JV-tryp6BW-kXW4TqGZG5HNv_LN67vcnv3gP0xW6N1vHY6lZ3mfW3HlcxC7s0_F4W9khqb_1DXMmyW8wRJ6j1mTl8fW3_Jwwx6BsFRjW3CRMJJ45zgs9W10C86m8fM7vrW7-XxHD6YkW3LW4tQCW-4QJsmRW7pw7vM7WQDkpW76xK3Y2txlTTW1SRhBz57XZj-W5PWL3J101wRzW4yBTGy4pcGTMW1XpwLs7FzxtNW4zX1rn22YwkrW1dbgX61gYpXsW45gHdL5mrHs2W833Z-x7_VTWvW1kpSmX5GQBcNVfX_3h74TxLgW6PyWww8_VXPcW5Cd_rq5R42_Pf4m-mxx04 )

The HireReady Newsletter Vol. 75: Infiltrating a Company by Solving Their Secret Problem

Forget the front door. The best opportunities aren't posted on job boards; they're hidden in plain sight, disguised as problems no one has had the courage or creativity to solve. This issue is about becoming the answer to a question a company doesn't even know it's asking. Stop applying for jobs and start creating them.

🛠️ The job search hacks 🛠️

🔍 Reverse-Engineer Their "Future You" with Jobscan's Resume Scanner 🔍

Everyone uses resume scanners (https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWc8Fm2_TV2JV-tryp6BW-kXW4TqGZG5HNv_LN67vcnP3gP0xW7lCdLW6lZ3mKW99_K8N93Hr21W8PvQLW2_HyT6W4l0mR36C5d8SW9kSQHw8QMqGBW1_HW-F8wKH5dVvdLj52q-X-vW2ptqMW5TkHkcW9hj_mn5w5LHnW6p84SS2zjM6hW33wqQ36jNKwbW4Jwzrv44v1K6W9lkDSD7F17vpMLBW7Vq-k5BW5yTTJW4nwwCPW8vwskX7YTVTYW101RW13kpgp3W5F7tv78yCpFDW68j6Qf4JmfWVW2XbGpZ8DbHNpW2T9Jd05cBCG3W5wvtM-29zNcxW590HMF4MbsygW7jcFcV3pQd5kW4drX7V5jyf_Wf5Zx8h604 ) to match their resume to a current job description. That's playing checkers. I want you to play chess.

Instead of optimizing for the job they have open today, you're going to optimize for the job they'll need to create for you tomorrow. You'll do this by finding a job description for a role one or two levels above the one you're targeting at the same company or a direct competitor. This is the "future you" role. By scanning your resume against this more senior role, Jobscan (https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWc8Fm2_TV2JV-tryp6BW-kXW4TqGZG5HNv_LN67vcnP3gP0xW7lCdLW6lZ3mKW99_K8N93Hr21W8PvQLW2_HyT6W4l0mR36C5d8SW9kSQHw8QMqGBW1_HW-F8wKH5dVvdLj52q-X-vW2ptqMW5TkHkcW9hj_mn5w5LHnW6p84SS2zjM6hW33wqQ36jNKwbW4Jwzrv44v1K6W9lkDSD7F17vpMLBW7Vq-k5BW5yTTJW4nwwCPW8vwskX7YTVTYW101RW13kpgp3W5F7tv78yCpFDW68j6Qf4JmfWVW2XbGpZ8DbHNpW2T9Jd05cBCG3W5wvtM-29zNcxW590HMF4MbsygW7jcFcV3pQd5kW4drX7V5jyf_Wf5Zx8h604 ) will reveal the precise skills and keywords you need to develop to become their ideal candidate for a promotion they haven't even thought of yet.

Next Steps:

- Find a job description for a role that is one or two promotions ahead of where you are now in your target company or a close competitor.
- Use Jobscan's Resume Scanner (https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWc8Fm2_TV2JV-tryp6BW-kXW4TqGZG5HNv_LN67vcnP3gP0xW7lCdLW6lZ3mKW99_K8N93Hr21W8PvQLW2_HyT6W4l0mR36C5d8SW9kSQHw8QMqGBW1_HW-F8wKH5dVvdLj52q-X-vW2ptqMW5TkHkcW9hj_mn5w5LHnW6p84SS2zjM6hW33wqQ36jNKwbW4Jwzrv44v1K6W9lkDSD7F17vpMLBW7Vq-k5BW5yTTJW4nwwCPW8vwskX7YTVTYW101RW13kpgp3W5F7tv78yCpFDW68j6Qf4JmfWVW2XbGpZ8DbHNpW2T9Jd05cBCG3W5wvtM-29zNcxW590HMF4MbsygW7jcFcV3pQd5kW4drX7V5jyf_Wf5Zx8h604 ) to compare your current resume against this "future you" job description.
- Analyze the "missing skills" report not as a list of what you lack, but as your personal roadmap for professional development over the next six months.
- Begin strategically acquiring these skills and weaving them into your current projects and your LinkedIn profile.

Free Tool: Discover Future-Proof Skills
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWc8Fm2_TV2JV-tryp6BW-kXW4TqGZG5HNv_LN67vcnP3gP0xW7lCdLW6lZ3mKW99_K8N93Hr21W8PvQLW2_HyT6W4l0mR36C5d8SW9kSQHw8QMqGBW1_HW-F8wKH5dVvdLj52q-X-vW2ptqMW5TkHkcW9hj_mn5w5LHnW6p84SS2zjM6hW33wqQ36jNKwbW4Jwzrv44v1K6W9lkDSD7F17vpMLBW7Vq-k5BW5yTTJW4nwwCPW8vwskX7YTVTYW101RW13kpgp3W5F7tv78yCpFDW68j6Qf4JmfWVW2XbGpZ8DbHNpW2T9Jd05cBCG3W5wvtM-29zNcxW590HMF4MbsygW7jcFcV3pQd5kW4drX7V5jyf_Wf5Zx8h604 )

💡 Become the Solution to Their Most Expensive Problem 💡

Companies are always trying to either make more money or save more money. Your mission is to figure out which expensive problem your target company is bleeding money on and present yourself as the solution. This isn't about finding a job opening; it's about finding a costly inefficiency.

Pour through their quarterly investor reports, read industry news about the challenges they're facing, and even look at customer complaints on social media. Is their customer churn too high? Are they spending a fortune on a particular software that isn't delivering? Find that financial pain point and build a simple, one-page proposal on how your skills could alleviate it.

Next Steps:

- Choose a target company and research their biggest financial and operational challenges.
- Identify a specific, high-cost problem that you have the skills to address.
- Draft a concise, one-page proposal that outlines the problem and your proposed solution, focusing on the potential cost savings or revenue generation.
- Identify the head of the department most affected by this problem and send them your proposal directly.

Guide: How to Show Your Value
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWc8Fm2_TV2JV-tryp6BW-kXW4TqGZG5HNv_LN67vcp43gP0xW7Y8-PT6lZ3krW5-vCPX4FFjRfW4GsLfC3yNNBtW2zQfgr8KYbZCW2F289V2H3fkxW615DQt2jfqkyVZ3kTB4LXwVLMhzRyDhd2r9W6pMC3G4Bl05-W6Cj5_D5VMZWfW9bJxxy8tjJRFVGxg5x4-ZCfjW7hR3MR4DBJD5W7fwpSp4QGpGKN74w9LgPKMWRW42DSJR2-Myx2VQw7cf45t3P8N19KSN_8SSC-W6XcHfK38_hVxW35mqFQ10wWmBW6gQ8Xw2n49PsW4dnQK45h2L9HVWBY8q1bMd_NVplT2j3GJWrzW6khSLH7kK4LmW4tzjlW136716W7XBcY01C_d8Ff8-PZP804 )

🧠 The "Helpful Expert" LinkedIn Strategy 🧠

Stop networking and start helping. Instead of asking for a job, your goal is to become the most helpful person in your target company's professional network.

Identify key decision-makers and employees at the company on LinkedIn. Don't just connect and pitch; observe what they're posting about, what problems they're discussing, and what questions they're asking. Then, add real value to their conversations. Share a relevant article, offer a thoughtful perspective, or even create a short video explaining a solution to a problem they've mentioned. You're not a job seeker; you're a trusted expert who is already contributing to their success.

Next Steps:

- Identify 10-15 key people at your target company on LinkedIn.
- Follow their activity for a week and take notes on the challenges and topics they are discussing.
- Find or create a piece of content that directly addresses one of these challenges.
- Share this content in a comment on their post or in a brief, helpful direct message, with no expectation of anything in return.

Guide: How to Optimize Your LinkedIn Profile
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWc8Fm2_TV2JV-tryp6BW-kXW4TqGZG5HNv_LN67vcp43gP0xW7Y8-PT6lZ3n6W6K099g5ZJ_P-W6_6VW76XhZj2VZ27vM1bNnvQW6C8ZVC3-H8t-N86_gybmfSX-W75QMnP6NLbNtW25gzqp8YQcDwW1x9k9p4B4gZVW6Tk_j05bSSnvW8ftzR93tFFsqW39pNx41jz60jW7-4hVB8jmYHwW7f3g4M8H0rn4W1mhfnS2sv3DnW5LcKSh443sLzW5FR2XD9b333DN9kd_lPt2N-tW6m5sFl90st1DW4R7TT58CNx3QVlqs2-3F9-ljW6qSnYG5ccDTnW4V2hb4783q86VBkxPZ5mS5VsW5D0Gst8FVx3qN6CVCcMCJkYqW3WhdfQ3GgGmSf2G_6wg04 )

📝 Conduct a "Pre-Interview" Project 📝

Why wait for them to give you a hypothetical interview question when you can solve a real problem for them right now? Identify a small but visible issue with your target company's product, service, or marketing. It could be a confusing part of their website, a gap in their social media content, or a customer service issue you've personally experienced. Create a small project that solves this problem. This could be a redesigned webpage mockup, a sample social media campaign, or a detailed customer feedback report with actionable suggestions. Send it to the relevant department head with a simple note: "I'm a huge fan of what you do, and I had a few ideas on how to make it even better."

Next Steps:

- Identify a small, specific, and solvable problem at your target company.
- Create a tangible solution. This could be a design, a written document, a short video, or a slide deck.
- Identify the person who is most likely responsible for the area you've addressed.
- Email them your project with a concise and enthusiastic message.

👥 The "Informational Interview" Reframe 👥

Everyone asks for an "informational interview" to learn about the company. Instead, you're going to request a "problem-solving session." When you reach out to someone at your target company, your ask will be different. "I've been following [Company Name]'s work in [specific area] and I have a few ideas that might help solve [a specific, well-researched problem]. Could I borrow 15 minutes of your time to walk you through them?"

This reframes you from a job seeker asking for a favor to a peer offering valuable insight. Even if your ideas aren't perfect, you've demonstrated initiative, creativity, and a genuine interest in helping them succeed.

Next Steps:

- Deeply research a challenge or opportunity at your target company.
- Develop 2-3 high-level, innovative ideas to address it.
- Identify the right person to share these ideas with.
- Send a concise email requesting a brief "problem-solving session" to share your thoughts.

Guide: Prepare to Impress
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWc8Fm2_TV2JV-tryp6BW-kXW4TqGZG5HNv_LN67vcp43gP0xW7Y8-PT6lZ3lhW8q-PCD66ncfpW1Wmkyl3L8nh3N6TK76_qBFlXW5DvVSj67J8hpW7432102tH4NyW49bf475B2WqmW74_Zt72cYnKCW9kGSy46_MQ24W8LNX1N3bxmkQW8M61yN9lRgrkW5yx7x-28F76qW45HlpC7BzM3DW3dsybs2_KBDBW43CLv-56VYs7VC2lPw3Hzh8VW99rJmd8zD8zSW6Q4V2V6fSzcKW8D42zT54Lq7cW6MFHcz3hmdWxW87jJnn4QshMCW3sR3rs7w2vnsW776fb75VlSjWW82V7nL3Qs8f0W1vtXLT4lV3TRVRpWgZ26kgzBW8gjyXd11lJv1f6-2cPM04 )

🎯 A Smarter Job Search Strategy 🎯

If you’re still submitting the same resume to every job, you’re not alone, but it’s harder to stand out in today’s market. Career coach Elvi Caperonis recommends applying with intention, not urgency:

1️⃣ Tailor your resume to every role

Small adjustments in keywords, accomplishments, and phrasing can dramatically increase your match rate, and your chances of being seen.

2️⃣ Leverage LinkedIn to build genuine connections

Hiring is still human. Networking, thoughtful comments, and value-driven posts help you get on the radar before you even apply.

3️⃣ Know your strengths and use AI to find roles that truly fit

AI tools can help uncover hidden opportunities, improve keyword alignment, and identify roles that match your skills, not just your job title.

But the most important ingredient? A resilient mindset. “You may face rejections, but don’t take them personally… We are going to overcome them.”

Read More from Elvi
(https://info.jobscan.co/e3t/Ctc/S+113/cvzDf04/VWc8Fm2_TV2JV-tryp6BW-kXW4TqGZG5HNv_LN67vcnP3prCCW7lCdLW6lZ3nTW8b8Zp78TNK-sW9dmFrv5bpX0RW9fz1s534DRBkW1B9WJl6GWGVvW8XCKM44mz7NnW818TSb1blTpLMTNL3m1QRClW2X0y0L2jmzJmW2wVyKn75G_WHV1pLsY6gm8QqN2n_qjSmhNR0V8HQxd45Mt5wN6fmGNMtN0GpW8bkj2p1BpxBXN4-cPBmlftZSN3SMLbhGvVpRN5jQfWlpG7Z4W4w0Hqb6N5XKxW7Q7P2c6xTDWRW6Zh61k1jq3MkW8d0Bmd5RH913W25BKqw7p54rMW2y3mf06mjG0pW7M7Pl73479KLf5CrT-804 )

The job search isn't about fitting in; it's about standing out in a way that makes you indispensable. Don't for permission to be great. Go out there this week, find a problem that no one else is brave enough to tackle, and become the solution they can't afford to ignore.

To your successful search,

 4th January 2026 | 10:00 AM

 Work on real-world nation-building case studies

  Register Now

subject: Wishing you and your loved ones a very Happy New Year on behalf of the SSTC family.
email_text: Dear Nandini Singh,   As we step into 2026, a year filled with new hopes, renewed aspirations, and meaningful possibilities, Shri Shankaracharya Technical Campus extends its warmest New Year wishes to our cherished alumni across the world. You are the living legacy of our institution. Every achievement you accomplish, every challenge you overcome, and every path you carve forward reflects the values, learning, and spirit nurtured on our campus. Your journeys continue to inspire our students and strengthen the reputation of our alma mater. Though time and distance may take you far, your bond with Shri Shankaracharya Technical Campus remains timeless. We deeply value your association and take pride in the collective memories, experiences, and successes that unite us as one community. May the year ahead bring you growth, fulfillment, good health, and continued success—both personally and professionally. We look forward to strengthening our connection and celebrating many more milestones together. With Best Regards,Shri Shankaracharya Technical CampusJunwani, Bhilai- 490020 IMPORTANT: Please do not share this mail with others, as it gives direct access to your account You received this email because you are a member of Shri Shankaracharya Technical Campus Alumni Relations - Alumni Portal. subject: 5 trends you should watch for in 2026
email_text:
AI in Action

By IBM


By IBM

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/5-trends-you-should-watch-2026-ibm-kdeae?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3B%2FSNGcPRTQXWkAcVzHLr0Yw%3D%3D&midToken=AQEIM4HzHiyK2w&midSig=0RgVWLD7Vyrc41&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-ghmp60~mjubl2t8~3w-null-null&eid=ghmp60-mjubl2t8-3w&otpToken=MWIwYzE5ZTAxNTJmY2FjN2IyMjQwNGVkNDAxOWU1YjQ4YmM4ZDI0MDllYWU4NjYxNzljNDA3NmI0YjU5NWZmYWY3ZDFkZjgyNGNmNmU0ZDYwNGY4ZjZiZmY1ZjYxZTMxNmQyOTQ1YTJhNGFmZWVkYjMyZWQzNCwxLDE%3D
  
In a world moving at the speed of AI—and soon, quantum—stability is no longer the goal. The most forward-thinking leaders aren’t searching for solid ground; they’re looking for the cracks where new opportunities will emerge. Our latest research highlights five data-driven trends that will define business leadership in 2026. Check them out below ⤵ 1️⃣ Uncertainty will be your greatest asset—if you embrace it In a volatile environment, rapid response is non-negotiable. In fact, 90% of executives say they’ll lose their edge if their organization can’t operate in real time. What to do → Rewire the organization for rapid response. 2️⃣ Employees will want more AI—not less There’s a perception gap at the heart of AI transformation. It's not just about automation—it’s about unlocking human potential. 61% of employees say AI makes their job less mundane and more strategic. Across all age groups, workers are increasingly open to deeper AI integration by their employers. What to do → Create a roadmap for roles that don’t yet exist. 👉 How will age groups respond to AI adoption in 2026? See the breakdown ⤵ 3️⃣ Customers will hold your AI accountable Customer trust is the ultimate currency: 95% of executives say consumer trust in their AI will define the success of new products and services. Not influence—define. What to do → Make customers lab partners, not lab rats. Two-thirds of consumers would switch brands if a company intentionally concealed AI’s involvement in their experience. 4️⃣ Global AI resilience will require a local safety net AI resilience hinges on uninterrupted access. That means AI sovereignty—an organization’s ability to control and govern its AI systems, data, and infrastructure at all times—has become mission-critical. What to do → Solidify your AI safeguards. 5️⃣ Quantum advantage will demand strength in numbers Quantum computing has moved well-beyond wishful thinking: Recent research indicates that quantum advantage is likely to emerge by the end of 2026. What to do → Prepare for the next big breakthrough. Looking Ahead In 2026, the secret to capturing fleeting opportunities won’t be perfect planning—it will be developing an appetite for ambiguity. Forward-thinking organizations are already preparing for this next chapter. How will you stay ahead? 👉 Check out the full IBV report
Keep reading on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/5-trends-you-should-watch-2026-ibm-kdeae?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3B%2FSNGcPRTQXWkAcVzHLr0Yw%3D%3D&midToken=AQEIM4HzHiyK2w&midSig=0RgVWLD7Vyrc41&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-ghmp60~mjubl2t8~3w-null-null&eid=ghmp60-mjubl2t8-3w&otpToken=MWIwYzE5ZTAxNTJmY2FjN2IyMjQwNGVkNDAxOWU1YjQ4YmM4ZDI0MDllYWU4NjYxNzljNDA3NmI0YjU5NWZmYWY3ZDFkZjgyNGNmNmU0ZDYwNGY4ZjZiZmY1ZjYxZTMxNmQyOTQ1YTJhNGFmZWVkYjMyZWQzNCwxLDE%3D

subject: In case you missed it: The US isn’t dead but it’s not what you think

Yudi here.

Year - end note from me to you

As this year wraps up, I’ve been thinking a lot about this
community.

The messages, replies, DMs, and quiet "this helped me" notes mean
more than you know. This newsletter exists because of you, and I
genuinely want to give back before we step into a new year.

So for a limited time, I’m opening my Job Hunting Accelerator at
50% off.

If you know you need to change something but aren’t sure what to
do next, this is meant to help you take the first solid step.

Use code December50 (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/mvugrxw8m6f5hqwxl5zcmhrk64lqqs3h26gnr/z2hghnhe0094v7up/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=

I’m rooting for you.

)
Join the Accelerator: Get 50% Off (
https://link.yudij.com/newsletteremail )
​
"USA is literally dead."

That’s the sentence I kept hearing during my recent India meetups
across four cities.
And honestly, I get why students feel this way.

The uncertainty.
Immigration noise.
Layoffs everywhere.
Stories of people struggling even after doing everything right.

After talking to hundreds of students face-to-face & in my
accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/mvugrxw8m6f5hqwxl5zcmhrk64lqqs3h26gnr/z2hghnhe0094v7up/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), I realized something important.
People thinking about the US today fall into three very different
groups.

Group 1:​
They are clear. They have admits. They know why they are going.
Nothing shakes them.
If you are in this bucket, you are probably fine.

Group 2:​
They have already decided not to go.
They are tired of the drama and want to revisit the US later,
maybe 2028.
That choice is valid too.

Group 3:​
This is the group I worry about the most.

Ambitious.
Confused.
Smart.
Stuck between comfort in India and possibility in the US.

Some have work experience and a decent salary but wonder if they
are settling too early.
Others graduated recently, could not land a job, and see a US
master’s as their last hope.

If that’s you, this newsletter is for you.

A story you need to hear
------------------------

Let me tell you about someone I know. Let’s call him Vikram.

Middle-class background. Big dreams.
He believed hard work alone would figure everything out.

So he packed his bags and landed in the US with faith and
ambition.

What he did not expect was the reality.

Gatekeepers everywhere.
A brutal job market competing with laid-off engineers from Meta,
Amazon, Google.
No placements. No straight path.
Just networking, asking for chances, and constant uncertainty
around visas.

He eventually made it.
But the cost was emotional, financial, and mental.

And here’s the truth.
Most people land in the US without knowing what they are walking
into.

I don’t want that for you.

What I’ve observed after 100+ podcasts and 15 universities
----------------------------------------------------------

Some students landed jobs within 3- 4 months, even in the worst
job market.
Others are still struggling after a full year of OPT.

Luck matters. Timing matters.
But there are clear patterns.

Here are the three things students who made it had in common.

1. Career clarity (not vague dreams)
------------------------------------

"Tech."
"Management."
"AI something."

That’s not clarity. That’s confusion.

The students who succeeded knew:

* The exact role they were targeting
* What to type on LinkedIn
* The top companies hiring for that role
* Where alumni from their university worked
* And they started networking even before landing

If you don’t have this clarity, waiting is not failure. It’s
strategy.

2. Relentless focus
-------------------

These students were boring in the best way.

No constant partying.
No unnecessary drama.
They chose roommates carefully and protected their time.

They came for one reason and stayed loyal to it.

3. Realistic financial planning
-------------------------------

Many students are told, "On-campus jobs will cover everything."

That’s not always true.

On-campus jobs are limited. Competitive. Exhausting.

The smart students:

* Were strategic about on-campus work
* Avoided draining 20-hour labor jobs
* Preserved energy for internships, co-ops, and full-time roles

They understood why they were working, not just how much.

So, should you go to the US?
----------------------------

Ask yourself honestly:

* Do you have relevant or transferable work experience?
* Are you going to a university with real hiring outcomes?
* Are you okay with uncertainty and discomfort?
* If you lack experience, are you willing to go all-in from day
one?

If the answer feels fuzzy, pause.

Do not spend 50 lakhs to "find clarity."
Find clarity first. Then spend the money.

That’s how you get real ROI.

I’ve shared all of this in detail in my recent YouTube video.
It’s a longer, honest conversation, and I think it will help you
think clearly instead of emotionally.

https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/mvugrxw8m6f5hqwxl5zcmhrk64lqqs3h26gnr/x0hph6hexx8r6lf5/aHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dS5iZS92YmFCQVZLS3Fhdz9zaT0tVTdTTHc1eXBtel81Nkx2
)​

-Yudi J

subject: Amazon's year in AI: The innovations transforming how we work, shop, and live
email_text:
Amazon News

By Amazon


By Amazon

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/amazons-year-ai-innovations-transforming-how-we-work-shop-live-kh7sc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BFSgxm%2BEGTEmI4Xg9XVbNQQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQEIM4HzHiyK2w&midSig=3jxE0r4Pf5pI41&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-ghmp60~mjswcxtw~wy-null-null&eid=ghmp60-mjswcxtw-wy&otpToken=MWIwYzE5ZTAxNTJmY2FjN2IyMjQwNGVkNDAxOWU2YjU4N2NkZDk0MTljYWM4ZTYxNzljNDA3NmI0YjU5NWZmYWY3ZDFkZjgzNDBlOWQwZGU3YmFlZWU5ZjdmZjYyZjhmNjMxN2RiYmYwZmEyZDNlZTBmOWFkOSwxLDE%3D
  
Get the latest from Amazon anytime by visiting About Amazon. As 2025 comes to a close, thank you for being part of the Amazon News community. Your engagement helps us share the stories behind the technologies and teams shaping Amazon’s future. This edition looks back at the AI breakthroughs that defined the year—from agentic systems and frontier models to smarter shopping, robotics, and global investments—and how they’re reshaping everyday experiences for customers, businesses, and communities heading into 2026. Amazon's AI innovations defining tomorrow For customers and businesses AI innovations transforming how people shop, sell, and experience Amazon How Amazon is using generative and agentic AI to transform the shopping experience Amazon is using generative and agentic AI—anchored by Rufus, our next-generation AI assistant for shopping, alongside tools like Buy for Me and AI-powered search and discovery—to help customers find, discover, and evaluate products from its broad selection more quickly and naturally, reducing the time and effort required to make confident purchase decisions. Read more → Scott Karpen, producer for 'Prime Vision with Next Gen Stats' Amazon's custom AI-powered Prime Insights take fans inside the game Prime Video introduced Prime Insights, a suite of AI-powered visual features including Defensive Alerts, Pressure Alert, Coverage ID, Prime Target, and Pocket Health that use real-time player tracking data and proprietary machine learning models to predict plays and highlight key moments across sports like Thursday Night Football, NASCAR, and the NBA. See inside → Amazon introduces agentic AI across the seller experience, transforming how sellers manage their businesses Powered by Amazon Bedrock, Seller Assistant evolves into an always-on, agentic AI-powered partner that helps sellers launch, manage, and grow their business, handling everything from routine operations to complex business strategy while keeping sellers in control. Read more → Amazon unveils the next generation of AI-powered Echo devices, purpose-built for Alexa+ Amazon announced four new Echo devices built from the ground up for Alexa+, powered by custom-designed silicon, Omnisense sensor fusion, and next-generation AI processing. Together, these advances enable more personalized, proactive, and deeply conversational Alexa+ experiences that fade into the background of everyday life, making the new Echo lineup the best way for customers to experience Alexa+ out of the box. Read more → Amazon SVP of Devices and Services Panos Panay on the future of AI devices: For developers and enterprise AI tools and infrastructure powering the next generation of innovation AWS unveils frontier agents, a new class of AI agents that work as an extension of your software development team The first three frontier agents—Kiro autonomous agent, AWS Security Agent, and AWS DevOps Agent—are autonomous, scalable, and work independently for hours or days without constant intervention, revolutionizing how teams build, secure, and operate software by functioning as extensions of development teams rather than just task assistants. Read more → Meet Amazon Quick Suite: The agentic AI application reshaping how work gets done QuickSuite answers questions and gets detailed answers, conducts deep dive research, analyzes and visualizes data, and creates automations for workflows across all your information with enterprise-grade security and privacy standards, transforming tasks that used to take days into minutes while connecting to over 50 built-in applications and 1,000+ apps through integrations. Read more → Inside an AWS Trainium chip. A city in the palm of your hand: Exploring the intricate world of an Amazon Web Services Trainium chip Tour the micro metropolis where calculations run 24/7 and data commutes at light speed—AWS Trainium is a custom-designed AI chip that completes trillions of calculations a second, with 64 interconnected chips working in concert to power one of the world's most powerful computers for training AI. See inside → Amazon introduces new frontier Nova models, a pioneering Nova Forge service for organizations to build their own models, and Nova Act for building agents Nova 2 models deliver industry-leading price-performance across reasoning, multimodal processing, conversational AI, code generation, and agentic tasks, while Nova Forge pioneers "open training" that empowers organizations to build their own optimized model variants by blending proprietary data with Nova's frontier capabilities, and Nova Act achieves breakthrough 90% reliability for browser-based UI automation workflows. Read more → AWS CEO Matt Garman on Nova Forge: 🧠 Pop quiz ✨ How many customers have used Amazon's AI shopping assistant Rufus this year? A. Over 25 million B. Over 100 million C. Over 250 million Find out the answer here. For global growth & empowerment Investments and innovations expanding AI access and impact worldwide AWS and OpenAI announce multi-year strategic partnership AWS will provide OpenAI with immediate access to world-class infrastructure including Amazon EC2 UltraServers featuring hundreds of thousands of NVIDIA GPUs, representing a $38 billion commitment that empowers OpenAI to rapidly scale their advanced AI workloads with the performance, scale, and security of AWS. Read more → Amazon announces $35 billion investment in India by 2030 to advance AI innovation, create jobs Amazon will focus on AI-driven digitization, export growth, and job creation, aiming to bring AI benefits to 15 million small businesses, quadruple cumulative ecommerce exports to $80 billion, and support 3.8 million jobs by 2030. One of many global AI investment commitments Amazon has made this year. Read more → Blue Jay, a robotics system that picks and stows in Amazon fulfillment centers. Amazon's newest AI and robotics systems are empowering employees and speeding up delivery Amazon introduces Blue Jay (a next-generation robotics system), Project Eluna (an agentic AI model), and smart delivery glasses that transform how fulfillment centers operate and how drivers deliver packages, reducing repetitive tasks while enabling faster, safer deliveries. Read more → Future Ready 2030: Amazon expands skills training goal, invests $2.5 billion to prepare 50 million people for the future of work Building on Amazon's success in upskilling over 700,000 employees globally—exceeding its original 2019 goal by more than seven times—this expanded commitment connects education directly to real employment outcomes across every stage of the career journey, from K-12 students to current employees and the broader workforce.
