subject: The US isn’t dead but it’s not what you think
email_text:
Hi Nandini,
Yudi here.

I have something special for you.

Year - end note from me to you

As this year wraps up, I’ve been thinking a lot about this
community.

The messages, replies, DMs, and quiet "this helped me" notes mean
more than you know. This newsletter exists because of you, and I
genuinely want to give back before we step into a new year.

So for a limited time, I’m opening my Job Hunting Accelerator at
50% off.

If you know you need to change something but aren’t sure what to
do next, this is meant to help you take the first solid step.

Use code December50 (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/n4uq7w9026hvhxelep0a6h68xz5ggflhew90n/kkhmh6hn4696qdhl/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
) at checkout to get 50% off.
I’m rooting for you.

-->Join the Accelerator: Get 50% Off (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/n4uq7w9026hvhxelep0a6h68xz5ggflhew90n/kkhmh6hn4696qdhl/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
)
Join the Accelerator: Get 50% Off (
https://link.yudij.com/newsletteremail )
​
"USA is literally dead."

That’s the sentence I kept hearing during my recent India meetups
across four cities.
And honestly, I get why students feel this way.

The uncertainty.
Immigration noise.
Layoffs everywhere.
Stories of people struggling even after doing everything right.

After talking to hundreds of students face-to-face & in my
accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/n4uq7w9026hvhxelep0a6h68xz5ggflhew90n/kkhmh6hn4696qdhl/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
), I realized something important.
People thinking about the US today fall into three very different
groups.

Group 1:​
They are clear. They have admits. They know why they are going.
Nothing shakes them.
If you are in this bucket, you are probably fine.

Group 2:​
They have already decided not to go.
They are tired of the drama and want to revisit the US later,
maybe 2028.
That choice is valid too.

Group 3:​
This is the group I worry about the most.

Ambitious.
Confused.
Smart.
Stuck between comfort in India and possibility in the US.

Some have work experience and a decent salary but wonder if they
are settling too early.
Others graduated recently, could not land a job, and see a US
master’s as their last hope.

If that’s you, this newsletter is for you.

A story you need to hear
------------------------

Let me tell you about someone I know. Let’s call him Vikram.

Middle-class background. Big dreams.
He believed hard work alone would figure everything out.

So he packed his bags and landed in the US with faith and
ambition.

What he did not expect was the reality.

Gatekeepers everywhere.
A brutal job market competing with laid-off engineers from Meta,
Amazon, Google.
No placements. No straight path.
Just networking, asking for chances, and constant uncertainty
around visas.

He eventually made it.
But the cost was emotional, financial, and mental.

And here’s the truth.
Most people land in the US without knowing what they are walking
into.

I don’t want that for you.

What I’ve observed after 100+ podcasts and 15 universities
----------------------------------------------------------

Some students landed jobs within 3- 4 months, even in the worst
job market.
Others are still struggling after a full year of OPT.

Luck matters. Timing matters.
But there are clear patterns.

Here are the three things students who made it had in common.

1. Career clarity (not vague dreams)
------------------------------------

"Tech."
"Management."
"AI something."

That’s not clarity. That’s confusion.

The students who succeeded knew:

* The exact role they were targeting
* What to type on LinkedIn
* The top companies hiring for that role
* Where alumni from their university worked
* And they started networking even before landing

If you don’t have this clarity, waiting is not failure. It’s
strategy.

2. Relentless focus
-------------------

These students were boring in the best way.

No constant partying.
No unnecessary drama.
They chose roommates carefully and protected their time.

They came for one reason and stayed loyal to it.

3. Realistic financial planning
-------------------------------

Many students are told, "On-campus jobs will cover everything."

That’s not always true.

On-campus jobs are limited. Competitive. Exhausting.

The smart students:

* Were strategic about on-campus work
* Avoided draining 20-hour labor jobs
* Preserved energy for internships, co-ops, and full-time roles

They understood why they were working, not just how much.

So, should you go to the US?
----------------------------

Ask yourself honestly:

* Do you have relevant or transferable work experience?
* Are you going to a university with real hiring outcomes?
* Are you okay with uncertainty and discomfort?
* If you lack experience, are you willing to go all-in from day
one?

If the answer feels fuzzy, pause.

Do not spend 50 lakhs to "find clarity."
Find clarity first. Then spend the money.

That’s how you get real ROI.

I’ve shared all of this in detail in my recent YouTube video.
It’s a longer, honest conversation, and I think it will help you
think clearly instead of emotionally.

-Yudi J

​
One pattern I keep noticing after talking to hundreds of
international students and job seekers is this:

Most people struggling in the U.S. job market are not lazy.
They’re not under-skilled.
They’re not careless.

They’re just playing the game without fully understanding how it
works.

And the U.S. job market is very much a game of rules.

When students land in the U.S., the focus usually goes here:

* getting good grades
* doing more projects
* adding certifications
* "working harder" than everyone else

All of that feels productive.
But it doesn’t always translate into interviews or offers.

I’ve seen people with average resumes land roles quickly.
And others with strong profiles struggle for months.

The difference is rarely effort.
It’s timing, positioning, and how early they start.

A pattern I see again and again
-------------------------------

Some job seekers prepare before they land.
Others wait until things "settle down."

The first group usually:

* has a resume ready
* knows which roles they’re targeting
* understands what companies actually look for
* starts applying immediately

The second group:

* spends the first 1–2 months adjusting
* figures things out slowly
* applies late
* feels behind before they even start

Both groups may work equally hard.
But their outcomes look very different.

That early preparation creates momentum.
And momentum changes everything.

Another pattern that shows up often
-----------------------------------

Some job seekers wait to feel "ready."
Others move even when things feel uncomfortable.

The ones who move early:

* apply even when their resume isn’t perfect
* talk to people instead of waiting for job boards
* show up at events, offices, or meetups
* learn on the job instead of waiting to know everything

They don’t wait for permission.
They put themselves in rooms where opportunities exist.

Their resumes don’t magically improve overnight.
But their exposure does.

And exposure leads to interviews.

The uncomfortable truth
-----------------------

In the U.S. job market:

* effort is invisible
* intentions don’t matter
* preparation beats talent
* speed beats perfection

Recruiters don’t see how stressed you are.
They only see what you show up with when the opportunity appears.

That’s why people who prepare early often look "lucky" later.

If you’re job hunting right now
-------------------------------

Instead of asking:
"Am I good enough?"

Try asking:

* Am I applying early or late?
* Do I clearly know which role I’m targeting?
* Does my resume show impact or just activity?
* Am I talking to people or only clicking apply?
* If an interview comes tomorrow, am I ready?

These questions move the needle more than one more project.

If you’re already working in the U.S.
-------------------------------------

This still applies.

Layoffs, visa pressure, and team instability are realities.
A big company name alone won’t protect you.

What helps is:

* owning problems that matter
* understanding how your work ties to revenue or risk
* keeping your network warm even when things feel stable
* preparing before you need to job hunt again

The people who recover fastest from layoffs usually started
preparing long before the layoff.

One last thing-

The U.S. job market doesn’t reward effort.
It rewards timing, positioning, and consistency.

Once you understand that, the anxiety reduces.
Because now you’re playing the right game.

And if you ever feel like you’re doing a lot but going nowhere,
that’s usually not a motivation issue.
It’s a structure issue.

That’s exactly what we work on inside the Job Hunting Accelerator
(
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/n4uq7w9026hvhxe7q2db6h68xz5ggflhew90n/8ghqhohoko27gnuk/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
).
No hype. No shortcuts. Just systems that work for international
students.

See you next week,
​– Yudi

Celebrating 10 Years of Parivartan - A Decade of Impact 

Dear Customer, 

In the last financial year, we marked a special milestone - 10 years of HDFC Bank Parivartan, our CSR programme. What began as a commitment to give back to the communities we serve has grown into a structured programme that has touched over 10.56 crore lives till date, across 28 States and 8 Union Territories of India. 

Over the past decade, Parivartan has delivered measurable impact across six key focus areas, in which it operates. I’d like to share some of the highlights of the cumulative impact created by the Bank’s CSR programme, up to March 2025. 
	
	
	
	
	

Rural Development 	Promotion of 

Education 
	Skill Training 

& Livelihood Enhancement 
	
 Impacted  10,000+ villages  and  15 lakh  households through development initiatives. 	Established  2,600+  SMART schools and  930+  community libraries. 	Trained  7 lakh+  individuals and supported  10,700+  Self-Help Groups (SHGs). 	
	
Healthcare 

& Hygiene 
	Financial Literacy 

& Inclusion 
	Natural Resource Management 	
Constructed  24,000+  household toilets, enabled waste management solutions across   550+  local bodies. 	Reached over 23+ lakh  people through secure banking campaigns such as Vigil Aunty. 	Created  14,500+  water conservation structures, installed  69,000+  solar lights. 	
	
	
	
	

We are proud to share our progress with our stakeholders through HDFC Bank’s first standalone CSR Report, 2025 , which captures the scale and stories behind a decade of Parivartan.

Thank you for being a valued part of our journey of change. 

Wishing you and your loved ones a joyous Festive Season. 

Warm regards,

From Salary Ceiling to Unlimited Growth: Why Smart People Choose Entrepreneurship
email_text:
Founder Diaries By Tushar

By Tushar Sahu


By Tushar Sahu

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/from-salary-ceiling-unlimited-growth-why-smart-people-tushar-sahu-aqlwc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BN0VcsktoQWeEyyWfUIP%2BWg%3D%3D&midToken=AQEIM4HzHiyK2w&midSig=2k4lqffPOnlc41&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-ghmp60~mjoafgjd~ks-null-null&eid=ghmp60-mjoafgjd-ks&otpToken=MWIwYzE5ZTAxNTJmY2FjN2IyMjQwNGVkNDAxOGVmYjc4NmNmZDg0MTlhYTg4YjYxNzljNDA3NmI0YjU5NWZmYWY3ZDFkZjk5NjliOWVkZmE3Mjg2ZDA4NDk3MWEwMjg4ZThhMWU1NWYxOTkxNjEzYWE0ZTVhZiwxLDE%3D
  
The Path Less Traveled That Leads to Extraordinary Growth In a world where most pursue the safety of a 9-to-5, entrepreneurship stands as a beacon for those craving autonomy, impact, and exponential growth. Whether you're a student, professional, or someone at a crossroads, here's why building your own venture might be your calling. 1. FREEDOM TO DEFINE YOUR OWN RULES As an entrepreneur, you're not bound by corporate hierarchies or someone else's vision. You set the roadmap. You decide what gets built, how it's built, and who you work with. This autonomy is liberating—and it's what drives relentless execution. 2. UNLIMITED INCOME POTENTIAL Your salary cap is only your ambition. In employment, there's a ceiling. As an entrepreneur? Your income scales with your business's growth. I've witnessed businesses that started with zero capital generating 6-7 figures in months. That's the power of compounding effort and smart systems. 3. SKILL ACCELERATION LIKE NOTHING ELSE Nothing teaches you faster than real money on the line. You'll learn marketing, sales, finance, operations, and product development—not from textbooks, but through execution. Every mistake becomes a lesson. Every win builds confidence. 4. IMPACT THAT COMPOUNDS Your job serves your employer's vision. Your business serves your vision and impacts customers directly. Building something meaningful—whether it's a SaaS product, lead generation platform, or content brand—creates ripple effects far beyond your own income. 5. BUILD VALUABLE CONNECTIONS Entrepreneurs surround themselves with other builders. You'll form deeper relationships with mentors, peers, and investors. These networks often become more valuable than the business itself. 6. TAX EFFICIENCY & WEALTH BUILDING Businesses have legitimate tax advantages individuals don't have. Expense deductions, equity appreciation, and asset building are game-changers for long-term wealth creation. 7. YOU OWN THE UPSIDE In employment, shareholders own your value creation. As an entrepreneur, every dollar of growth belongs to you (and your team). That equity compounds into generational wealth. THE REAL TRUTH Entrepreneurship isn't for everyone—and that's okay. It requires relentless discipline, tolerance for risk, and acceptance of uncertainty. You'll work harder than ever before. You'll face rejection, failure, and doubt. But if you have something to build, a problem to solve, or a vision that won't leave you alone... then entrepreneurship isn't a choice. It's a necessity. The question isn't "Should I become an entrepreneur?" The question is: "Can I afford NOT to?" Start today. Start small. Start now.

support, Splunk execs launch automated SRE, and 5 trending companies hiring

Quick Hits



Brit bound: Uber, Lyft <https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cy8jmx1dl9ro> pond 
skip.



Chip stack: Nvidia buys Groq for $20B 
<https://www.cnbc.com/2025/12/24/nvidia-buying-ai-chip-startup-groq-for-about-20-billion-biggest-deal.html>
.



Dance’s over: TikTok avoids U.S. ban 
<https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cgexp1q8wn1o>.



Data deal: Alphabet acquires Intersect 
<https://www.cnbc.com/2025/12/22/alphabet-to-acquire-intersect.html>.



Funding vibes: VCs (heart) Sweden’s Lovable 
<https://vestbee.com/insights/articles/lovable-raises-330-m>.



Name change: Gmail allows address updates 
<https://www.cnbc.com/2025/12/26/google-gmail-change-email-address-without-new-account-india-hindi-support.html>
.



Research rush: SF’s Edison 
<https://www.linkedin.com/news/story/edison-scientific-raises-70m-to-accelerate-research-with-ai-8093538/>
 shares VC spoils.



Trucks in space?: SpaceX buys up Cybertrucks 
<https://futurism.com/advanced-transport/spacex-buying-unfathomable-number-cybertrucks>
.



Gov’t venture: China backs VC funds 
<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-12-26/china-starts-state-backed-venture-funds-to-support-tech-startups>
.



‘World model’: Yann LeCun 
<https://techcrunch.com/2025/12/19/yann-lecun-confirms-his-new-world-model-startup-reportedly-seeks-5b-valuation/>
 seeks post-LLM AI.




Founder Tip: Every engineer graduating now should join a startup first.

Kartik Hegde <https://www.linkedin.com/in/kartikhegde/>, CEO of ChipStack 
<https://www.chipstack.ai/>, flips the usual advice. Some treat startups as the 
risky move you make after you've "figured it out" at a big company.

Early career is when you can afford to fail. Startups give you direct impact 
and no buffer between you and the problem. The corporate path teaches you to 
optimize inside someone else's system. Startups teach you whether you can build 
one.

It's easier to transition from a startup to a corporate setting than the 
reverse. Big companies will hire someone who's learned to operate without 
structure. Startups want people who've already done it.

Watch the full 'Why Work Here' interview here. <https://youtu.be/c_B5m6Vn9g4>

They're hiring! See 8 open roles here.  
<https://wellfound.com/company/chipstack>




 <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3i8RPaNrTRU>

Startups Hiring Now  <https://www.linkedin.com/in/glojdallas/>



Realtor.com

Realtor.com offers a comprehensive list of for-sale properties and online 
consumer tools

See 66 open roles. <https://wellfound.com/company/realtor-com>



IXL Learning

K-12 Personalized learning platform
See 57 open roles. <https://wellfound.com/company/ixl-learning>



Karbon HQ

Collaborative work management software for accounting firms

See 20 open roles. <https://wellfound.com/company/karbonhq>



Entrupy

Instantly authenticate high-value physical goods

See 22 open roles. <https://wellfound.com/company/entrupy>




Job Search Tip: What AI can't replace in your career.

Jassi Starleaf <https://www.linkedin.com/in/jassistarleaf/>, Senior Technical 
Recruiter atQuillbot <https://quillbot.com/>, answers "Will AI replace 
entry-level jobs?"

 AI won't replace your social and emotional intelligence. It won't replace 
complex problem solving, critical thinking, or creativity. What it will expose 
is who's been coasting on repetitive tasks.

The play isn't to avoid AI; it's to show you can work with it. Use it to 
brainstorm, then lead with judgment. Mention AI in your project examples, but 
highlight the human decisions that mattered.

 Watch the full 'Ask a Recruiter' session here. <https://youtu.be/WeInmjYoC1c>

Quillbot is hiring! See their 16 open roles.  
<https://wellfound.com/company/quillbot>



✨ Bonus: Join our Wellfound Discord community <https://discord.gg/YDNFFzKv7j>. 






 <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Bqnj1c5CqYg>





What We're Reading



Generalists shine in AI era

Generalists 
<https://venturebeat.com/ai/hiring-specialists-made-sense-before-ai-now-generalists-win>
 are in demand. AI hasn’t been around long—so recruiters seek out speed, 
adaptability, and the ability to work with little direction.



YC founders vibe with Claude

Anthropic’s Claude is trending among YC founders thanks in part to the rise of 
vibe-coding platforms 
<https://www.inc.com/ben-sherry/the-ai-models-that-top-startups-are-suddenly-choosing-over-chatgpt/91281830>
 like Replit and Lovable, according to one YC exec.



Study: AI users seek emotional support

One in three adults uses AI for emotional support 
<https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cd6xl3ql3v0o> or social connection, 
according to U.K. government research. One in 25 turn to tech for support every 
day.



Splunk execs automate SRE

San Francisco’s Resolve AI 
<https://techcrunch.com/2025/12/19/ex-splunk-execs-startup-resolve-ai-hits-1-billion-valuation-with-series-a/>
 raised at a near-unicorn valuation promising to automate site reliability 
engineering (SRE). The founders sold their first company to Splunk.

subject: 2025 year in review: 8 areas with research breakthroughs
email_text:
The Monthly AI Recap

By Google


By Google

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/2025-year-review-8-areas-research-breakthroughs-google-ctpke?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3B2IPB71pnSfG73D5n6qZ4Lw%3D%3D&midToken=AQEIM4HzHiyK2w&midSig=3KE8QThCH3kc41&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-ghmp60~mjnb8qtq~5a-null-null&eid=ghmp60-mjnb8qtq-5a&otpToken=MWIwYzE5ZTAxNTJmY2FjN2IyMjQwNGVkNDAxOGUwYmM4ZmNkZDc0MzljYWU4YjYxNzljNDA3NmI0YjU5NWZmYWY3ZDFkZmEyNDBlY2VjZmUwN2ZhYzhlNGMzYTYwMmJmNjIxN2VlYTI1ZTUzYzU2ZTMzZmJmNCwxLDE%3D
  
This article was written by Jeff Dean, Chief Scientist, Demis Hassabis, CEO Google DeepMind and James Manyika, SVP Research, Labs, Technology & Society. 2025 has been a year of extraordinary progress in research. With artificial intelligence, we can see its trajectory shifting from a tool to a utility: from something people use to something they can put to work. If 2024 was about laying the multimodal foundations for this era, 2025 was the year AI began to really think, act and explore the world alongside us. With quantum computing, we made progress towards real-world applications. And across the board, we helped turn research into reality, with more capable and useful products and tools making a positive impact on people's lives today. Here’s a look back at some of the breakthroughs, products and scientific milestones that defined the work of Google, Google DeepMind and Google Research in a year of relentless progress. Delivering breakthroughs on world-class models This year, we significantly advanced our model capabilities with breakthroughs on reasoning, multimodal understanding, model efficiency, and generative capabilities, beginning with the release of Gemini 2.5 in March and culminating in the November launch of Gemini 3 and the December launch of Gemini 3 Flash. Built on a foundation of state-of-the-art reasoning, Gemini 3 Pro is our most powerful model to date, designed to help you bring any idea to life. It topped the LMArena Leaderboard and redefined multimodal reasoning with breakthrough scores on benchmarks like Humanity’s Last Exam — a fiendishly hard test for AI models to see if AI can truly think and reason like humans — and GPQA Diamond. It also set a new standard for frontier models in mathematics, achieving a new state-of-the-art of 23.4% on MathArena Apex. We followed shortly with Gemini 3 Flash, which combines Gemini 3's Pro-grade reasoning with Flash-level latency, efficiency and cost, making it the most performant model for its size. Gemini 3 Flash's quality surpasses our previous Gemini 2.5 Pro-scale model's capabilities at a fraction of the price and substantially better latency, continuing our Gemini-era trend of 'the next generation's Flash model is better than the previous generation's Pro model'. Learn more about our progress on our world-class AI models this year: Gemini 3 Flash: frontier intelligence built for speed (Dec 2025) A new era of intelligence with Gemini 3 (Nov 2025) Introducing Nano Banana Pro (Nov 2025) Introducing Veo 3.1 and new creative capabilities in the Gemini API (Nov 2025) Gemini 2.5: Our most intelligent AI model (March 2025) Gemini 3 Flash price & benchmark table. We’re committed to making useful AI technology accessible, with state-of-the-art open models. We built our Gemma family of models to be lightweight and open for public use; this year we were able to introduce multimodal capabilities, significantly increase the context window, expand multilingual capabilities, and improve efficiency and performance. Learn more about this year’s advances in Gemma models: Introducing Gemma 3: The most capable model you can run on a single GPU or TPU (March 2025) Introducing Gemma 3 270M: The compact model for hyper-efficient AI (Aug 2025) Innovating and transforming our products with AI Throughout 2025, we continued to advance the trajectory of AI from tool to utility, transforming our portfolio of products with new, powerful agentic capabilities. We reimagined software development by moving beyond tools that assist coding to introducing powerful, agentic systems that collaborate with developers. Key advances, such as the impressive coding capabilities in Gemini 3 and the launch of Google Antigravity, mark a new era in AI-assisted software development. Learn more about this year’s advances building developer tools: Start building with Gemini 3 (Nov 2025) Introducing Google Antigravity, a New Era in AI-Assisted Software Development (Nov 2025) This evolution was also clear across our core products, from AI-enabled features on the Pixel 10 and updates to AI Mode in Search, to AI-first innovations like the Gemini app and NotebookLM, which gained advanced features like Deep Research. Learn more about how we’ve transformed our products with AI: 9 ways AI makes Pixel 10 our most helpful phone yet (Aug 2025) Expanding AI Overviews and introducing AI Mode (March 2025) Gemini 3 brings upgraded smarts and new capabilities to the Gemini app (Nov 2025) NotebookLM adds Deep Research and support for more source types (Nov 2025) Empowering creativity and co-creating with AI 2025 was a transformative year for generative media, giving people new and unprecedented capabilities to realize their creative ambitions. Generative media models and tools for video, images, audio and worlds became more effective and broadly used, with breakouts Nano Banana and Nano Banana Pro offering unprecedented capabilities for native image generation and editing. We worked with people in creative industries to develop tools like Flow and Music AI Sandbox, making them more helpful for creative workflows, and we expanded creative possibilities for people with new, AI-powered experiences in the Google Arts & Culture lab, major upgrades to image editing within the Gemini app, and the introduction of powerful new generative media models like Veo 3.1, Imagen 4 and Flow. Learn more about how we’re building AI to enhance creativity: Art, science, travel: 3 new AI-powered experiences this holiday season (Nov 2025) Introducing Veo 3.1 and advanced capabilities in Flow (Oct 2025) Nano Banana: Image editing in Gemini just got a major upgrade (Aug 2025) Veo 3, Imagen 4, and Flow: Fuel your creativity with new generative media models and tools (May 2025) Music AI Sandbox, now with new features and broader access (April 2025) As research breakthroughs continue to expand AI’s capabilities, Google Labs is where we share AI experiments as we develop them — hearing from users and evolving as we learn. Some of this year’s most engaging experiments from Labs: Pomelli, an AI experiment for on-brand marketing content; Stitch, which introduced a way to turn prompt and image inputs into complex UI designs and frontend code in minutes; Jules, an asynchronous coding agent that acts as a collaborative partner for developers; and Google Beam, a 3D video communications platform that used AI to advance the possibilities of remote presence. Learn more about how we’re experimenting in Labs: Create on-brand marketing content for your business with Pomelli (Oct 2025) Google Beam: Our AI-first 3D video communication platform (May 2025) From idea to app: Introducing Stitch, a new way to design UIs (May 2025) Build with Jules, your asynchronous coding agent (May 2025) Advancing science and mathematics 2025 was also a banner year for scientific advances with AI, marked by breakthroughs in life sciences, health, natural sciences, and mathematics. In the space of a year, we made progress in building AI resources and tools that empower researchers and help them understand, identify, and develop treatments in healthcare. In genomics, where we’ve been applying advanced technology to research for 10 years, we moved beyond sequencing, using AI to interpret the most complex data. We also marked the 5-year anniversary of AlphaFold, the Nobel-winning AI system that solved the 50-year-old protein folding problem. AlphaFold has been used by over 3 million researchers in more than 190 countries, including over 1 million users in low- and middle-income countries. Learn more about how we’re using AI to advance life sciences and health: AlphaFold: Five years of impact (Nov 2025) Using AI to identify genetic variants in tumors with DeepSomatic (Oct 2025) AI as a research partner: Advancing theoretical computer science with AlphaEvolve (Sept 2025) AlphaGenome: AI for better understanding the genome (June 2025) Accelerating scientific breakthroughs with an AI co-scientist (Feb 2025) Gemini’s advanced thinking capabilities, including Deep Think, also enabled historic progress in mathematics and coding. Deep Think was able to solve problems that require deep abstract reasoning — achieving gold medal-standard in two international contests. Learn more about how we’re advancing natural sciences and mathematics: Gemini achieves gold-medal level at the International Collegiate Programming Contest World Finals (Sept 2025) Advanced version of Gemini with Deep Think officially achieves gold-medal standard at the International Mathematical Olympiad (July 2025) Shaping innovations in computing and the physical world We’re also leading major discoveries and shaping the future of science in areas like quantum computing, energy and moonshots. Research in this area drew new levels of public attention, with progress towards real-world applications of quantum computing as demonstrated by Quantum Echoes and, notably, Googler Michel Devoret becoming a 2025 Physics Nobel Laureate along with former Googler John Martinis and UC Berkeley’s John Clarke, for their foundational 1980s quantum research. Learn more about our work on space infrastructure and quantum computing: Project Suncatcher: Exploring a space-based, scalable AI infrastructure system design (Nov 2025) Googler Michel Devoret awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics (Oct 2025) Our Quantum Echoes algorithm is a big step toward real-world applications for quantum computing (Oct 2025) In 2025, we continued to advance the core infrastructure that powers our AI, focusing on breakthroughs in hardware design and improving energy efficiency. This included the introduction of Ironwood, a new TPU built for the age of inference, which was designed using a method called AlphaChip, alongside a commitment to measuring the environmental impact of our technology. Learn more about how we’re using AI to develop chips, infrastructure and improve energy efficiency: 3 things to know about Ironwood, our latest TPU (Nov 2025) How much energy does Google’s AI use? We did the math (Aug 2025) Ironwood: The first Google TPU for the age of inference (April 2025) Our work in robotics and visual understanding brought AI agents into both the physical and virtual worlds, with advancements like the foundational Gemini Robotics models, the more sophisticated Gemini Robotics 1.5, and the introduction of Genie 3 as a new frontier for general-purpose world models. Learn more about our work with world models and robotics: Gemini Robotics 1.5 brings AI agents into the physical world (Sept 2025) Genie 3: A new frontier for world models (Aug 2025) Gemini Robotics brings AI into the physical world (March 2025) Tackling global challenges and opportunities at scale Our work throughout 2025 demonstrates how AI-enabled scientific progress is being directly applied to address the world's most critical and pervasive challenges. By leveraging state-of-the-art foundational models and agentic reasoning, we are significantly increasing our understanding of the planet and its systems, while also delivering impactful solutions in areas vital to human flourishing, including climate resilience, public health and education. For example, we are using state-of-the-art foundational models and agentic reasoning to help increase our understanding of the planet, helping enable work that is making a difference in people’s lives now from weather predictions to urban planning to public health. For example, our flood forecasting information now covers more than two billion people in 150 countries for severe riverine floods. And our most advanced and efficient forecasting model, WeatherNext 2 can generate forecasts 8x faster and with resolution up to 1-hour. Using this technology, we’ve supported weather agencies in making decisions based on a range of scenarios through our experimental cyclone predictions. Learn more about our work in weather, mapping and wildfires: WeatherNext 2: Our most advanced weather forecasting model (Nov 2025) New updates and more access to Google Earth AI (Oct 2025) Google Earth AI: Our state-of-the-art geospatial AI models (July 2025) AlphaEarth Foundations helps map our planet in unprecedented detail (July 2025) How we're supporting better tropical cyclone prediction with AI (June 2025) Inside the launch of FireSat, a system to find wildfires earlier (March 2025) We are working with partners to apply AI-enabled scientific progress closer to patients, opening up new avenues for disease management and therapeutic discovery. Learn more about our health-related work: Cell2Sentence-Scale 27B: How a Gemma model helped discover a new potential cancer therapy pathway (Oct 2025) From diagnosis to treatment: Advancing AMIE for longitudinal disease management (March 2025) AI is proving to be a powerful tool in education, enabling new forms of understanding and expanding curiosity through initiatives like LearnLM and Guided Learning in Gemini. We brought Gemini’s most powerful translation capabilities to Google Translate, enabling much smarter, more natural and accurate translations and piloting new speech to speech translation capabilities. Learn more about how we’re using AI to enable learning: Bringing state-of-the-art Gemini translation capabilities to Google Translate (Dec 2025) Guided Learning in Gemini: From answers to understanding (Aug 2025) How generative AI expands curiosity and understanding with LearnLM (May 2025) Prioritizing responsibility and safety We couple our research breakthroughs with rigorous and forward-looking work on responsibility and safety. As our models grow more capable, we’re continuing to advance and evolve our tools, resources and safety frameworks to anticipate and mitigate risk. Gemini 3 demonstrated this approach in action: it's our most secure model yet, and has undergone the most comprehensive set of safety evaluations of any Google AI model to date. And we’re looking further ahead, exploring a responsible path to AGI, prioritizing readiness, proactive risk assessment, and collaboration with the wider AI community. Learn more about our responsibility and safety work: You can now verify Google AI-generated videos in the Gemini app (Dec 2025) How we’re bringing AI image verification to the Gemini app (Nov 2025) Strengthening our Frontier Safety Framework (September 2025) Taking a responsible path to AGI (April 2025) Evaluating potential cybersecurity threats of advanced AI (April 2025) Leading frontier collaborations with industry, academia and civil society Advancing the frontier of AI responsibly demands collaboration across all parts of society. In 2025, we worked with leading AI labs to help to form the Agentic AI Foundation and support open standards to ensure a responsible and interoperable future for agentic AI. In education, we’ve partnered with school districts like Miami Dade County and education groups like Raspberry Pi to equip students with AI skills. Our research partnerships with universities like UC Berkeley, Yale, the University of Chicago and many more have been instrumental to some of this year’s most exciting frontier research, and we’re working with the US Department of Energy’s 17 national laboratories to transform how scientific research is conducted. And we’re working with filmmakers and other creative visionaries to put the best AI tools in their hands and explore storytelling in the age of AI. Learn more about our work on frontier collaboration: Google DeepMind supports U.S. Department of Energy on Genesis: a national mission to accelerate innovation and scientific discovery (Dec 2025) Formation of the Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF), Anchored by New Project Contributions Including Model Context Protocol (MCP), goose and AGENTS.md (Dec 2025) Announcing Model Context Protocol (MCP) support for Google services (Dec 2025) Our latest commitments in AI and learning (Nov 2025) Partnering to power Miami’s AI-ready future (Oct 2025) AI on Screen premiere: “Sweetwater” short film explores new AI narratives (Sept 2025) Behind “ANCESTRA”: combining Veo with live-action filmmaking (Jun 2025) How Indian music legend Shankar Mahadevan experiments with Music AI Sandbox (April 2025) Looking ahead As we look towards 2026, we’re looking forward to continuing to advance the frontier, safely and responsibly, for the benefit of humanity.

​

I’ve personally used Crobo for over a year. What I like is simple
- $0 transfer fees, transparent exchange rates & honestly some of
the best conversion rates I’ve seen.

​

If you already need to make a transfer, this is a nice bonus on
top.

-->Give it a try -> Crobo Money (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9q3z7oulhvzgdx55snh7lwrq/6qheh8hl0pdv6kuo/aHR0cHM6Ly9jcm9iby5vbmVsaW5rLm1lL1JOQmovWVVESV9OWV9OTA==
)
Give it a try -> Crobo Money (
https://crobo.onelink.me/RNBj/YUDI_NY_NL )
​
One pattern I keep noticing after talking to hundreds of
international students and job seekers is this:

Most people struggling in the U.S. job market are not lazy.
They’re not under-skilled.
They’re not careless.

They’re just playing the game without fully understanding how it
works.

And the U.S. job market is very much a game of rules.

When students land in the U.S., the focus usually goes here:

* getting good grades
* doing more projects
* adding certifications
* "working harder" than everyone else

All of that feels productive.
But it doesn’t always translate into interviews or offers.

I’ve seen people with average resumes land roles quickly.
And others with strong profiles struggle for months.

The difference is rarely effort.
It’s timing, positioning, and how early they start.

A pattern I see again and again
-------------------------------

Some job seekers prepare before they land.
Others wait until things "settle down."

The first group usually:

* has a resume ready
* knows which roles they’re targeting
* understands what companies actually look for
* starts applying immediately

The second group:

* spends the first 1–2 months adjusting
* figures things out slowly
* applies late
* feels behind before they even start

Both groups may work equally hard.
But their outcomes look very different.

That early preparation creates momentum.
And momentum changes everything.

Another pattern that shows up often
-----------------------------------

Some job seekers wait to feel "ready."
Others move even when things feel uncomfortable.

The ones who move early:

* apply even when their resume isn’t perfect
* talk to people instead of waiting for job boards
* show up at events, offices, or meetups
* learn on the job instead of waiting to know everything

They don’t wait for permission.
They put themselves in rooms where opportunities exist.

Their resumes don’t magically improve overnight.
But their exposure does.

And exposure leads to interviews.

The uncomfortable truth
-----------------------

In the U.S. job market:

* effort is invisible
* intentions don’t matter
* preparation beats talent
* speed beats perfection

Recruiters don’t see how stressed you are.
They only see what you show up with when the opportunity appears.

That’s why people who prepare early often look "lucky" later.

If you’re job hunting right now
-------------------------------

Instead of asking:
"Am I good enough?"

Try asking:

* Am I applying early or late?
* Do I clearly know which role I’m targeting?
* Does my resume show impact or just activity?
* Am I talking to people or only clicking apply?
* If an interview comes tomorrow, am I ready?

These questions move the needle more than one more project.

If you’re already working in the U.S.
-------------------------------------

This still applies.

Layoffs, visa pressure, and team instability are realities.
A big company name alone won’t protect you.

What helps is:

* owning problems that matter
* understanding how your work ties to revenue or risk
* keeping your network warm even when things feel stable
* preparing before you need to job hunt again

The people who recover fastest from layoffs usually started
preparing long before the layoff.

One last thing-

The U.S. job market doesn’t reward effort.
It rewards timing, positioning, and consistency.

Once you understand that, the anxiety reduces.
Because now you’re playing the right game.

And if you ever feel like you’re doing a lot but going nowhere,
that’s usually not a motivation issue.
It’s a structure issue.

That’s exactly what we work on inside the Job Hunting Accelerator
(
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/0vue26dk3gh9h9q3z7oulhvzgdx55snh7lwrq/kkhmh6hn483g25cl/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
).
No hype. No shortcuts. Just systems that work for international
students.

See you next week,
​– Yudi

subject: In case you missed: She landed a Tesla co-op in worst job market
email_text:
Hi Nandini,

Yudi here,

Before I start, I have to share something with you.

​Crobo’s Christmas Offer (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/5quzwk28xph7hnldedeu6h9286v44bnhrvn8m/n2hohvhveo6nkwu6/aHR0cHM6Ly9jcm9iby5vbmVsaW5rLm1lL1JOQmovWVVESV9OWV9OTA==
)🎄​

If you’re planning to send money to India in the next few weeks,
this is worth knowing.

​

Crobo Money is running a Christmas campaign right now.
​

If you transfer $1,000 using my link by Jan 15 (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/5quzwk28xph7hnldedeu6h9286v44bnhrvn8m/n2hohvhveo6nkwu6/aHR0cHM6Ly9jcm9iby5vbmVsaW5rLm1lL1JOQmovWVVESV9OWV9OTA==
), you’ll get a $35 gift card.

​

I’ve personally used Crobo for over a year. What I like is simple
- $0 transfer fees, transparent exchange rates & honestly some of
the best conversion rates I’ve seen.

​

If you already need to make a transfer, this is a nice bonus on
top.

-->Give it a try -> Crobo Money (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/5quzwk28xph7hnldedeu6h9286v44bnhrvn8m/n2hohvhveo6nkwu6/aHR0cHM6Ly9jcm9iby5vbmVsaW5rLm1lL1JOQmovWVVESV9OWV9OTA==
)
Give it a try -> Crobo Money (
https://crobo.onelink.me/RNBj/YUDI_NY_NL )
​
I just wrapped a podcast that honestly left me sitting quietly in
the studio for a few minutes.

You can watch it here → Link (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/5quzwk28xph7hnldedeu6h9286v44bnhrvn8m/48hvhehmk260n5fx/aHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dS5iZS9ILUJKM08xLVhOYw==
)​

Because listening to Riya felt like watching the hidden journey
of thousands of international students.

The part no consultant back home prepares you for.
The part your parents never hear about because you don’t want to
worry them.

And the part you quietly fight alone.

When she said,
​"I didn’t feel enough… I felt guilty I was spending my father’s
money."​
It shook me.

She came to the U.S. confident, ambitious, thinking she would
figure things out quickly.
Within weeks, reality hit her hard.
Rejections.
Silent applications.
Resume tears.
Friends getting interviews, she didn’t.
And that terrible mix of self-doubt and pressure that only
international students truly understand.

But here’s what made her story powerful.
She didn’t stay in that place.

The turning point
-----------------

At one point, she said she was applying everywhere with one
generic resume.
No strategy. No alignment. Just panic.

Then something snapped.
Her batchmates - the right ones - sat her down and told her the
truth:

"You’re applying wrong. Fix your resume. Apply within 24 hours.
Match the keywords.
Stop scrolling Instagram on the bus. Start scrolling LinkedIn."

It stung, but it changed everything.

And then something even bigger happened.
She went for a resume review with Purdue MEM’s career advisor,
Katie… and she broke down crying.
Because she finally saw how far behind she felt.
Katie didn’t judge her. She hugged her. She walked her through
what to fix.
She reminded her she belonged here.

That moment gave her the confidence to rebuild.

This is what makes Purdue MEM (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/5quzwk28xph7hnldedeu6h9286v44bnhrvn8m/wnh2hghq8oxr3kc7/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9wdXJkdWVtZW0=
)special - not just the coursework, but the humans behind it,
guiding you through the toughest phase of your career.

You can explore Purdue MEM program here (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/5quzwk28xph7hnldedeu6h9286v44bnhrvn8m/wnh2hghq8oxr3kc7/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9wdXJkdWVtZW0=
).

From chaos to clarity
---------------------

Once she learned how to structure her resume, research roles,
target companies that sponsor, and ask for referrals properly…
everything shifted.

She wasn’t doing "more."
She was finally doing what mattered.

That’s when the interview calls started coming - TikTok,
automotive companies, and eventually Tesla.​
Not because of luck, but because she learned how to think
strategically:

• match your resume to the job
• apply early
• use keywords
• ask seniors for feedback
• understand what companies actually need
• stay honest in interviews
• follow up properly with thank you emails and answers you missed

This is the exact transformation most international students in
my Accelerator (
https://604b57c3.click.convertkit-mail2.com/5quzwk28xph7hnldedeu6h9286v44bnhrvn8m/reh8hohmn5wqe7c2/aHR0cHM6Ly9saW5rLnl1ZGlqLmNvbS9uZXdzbGV0dGVyZW1haWw=
) go through - from panic mode to structured strategy.

Because behind every internship or offer is a journey your
LinkedIn feed never shows.
The tears. The family pressure.
The moments you question your worth.
The loneliness of being in a foreign country with no one to tell
you what’s right or wrong.

And yet… the students who make it are the ones who learn to
rebuild themselves like Riya did.
One strategy at a time.
One conversation at a time.
One tough day at a time.

Her story isn’t just inspiring.
It’s a mirror for so many of you.

If you’re going through something similar, I hope you listen to
this conversation.

It’ll give you perspective, strategy, and maybe a bit of hope
that you didn’t know you needed.

​Watch the full episode here. (

 Step onto your own virtual stage and host your first meeting
 

 Attending meetings lets you connect with others seamlessly. Do more with greater control – here’s how to utilize meetings to access your full potential.

 

https://click.e.zoom.us/?qs=35a6c0657aad576228d4fe95b04529acef8ddc2e288b2b4b0ef90d81399c2163af6ceed3562370c0a041737e83f5ab868c51ad2a053f605d79e1b0e18b920f18 
Host a meeting 


  
 

https://click.e.zoom.us/?qs=35a6c0657aad576228d4fe95b04529acef8ddc2e288b2b4b0ef90d81399c2163af6ceed3562370c0a041737e83f5ab868c51ad2a053f605d79e1b0e18b920f18 


 Using Zoom for personal reasons?
 

 Hosting a Zoom meeting isn't just for business—it's a fantastic way to bring your community together, no matter where they are. 



Imagine being able to host virtual gatherings for game nights, birthday celebrations, or catching up with friends over coffee. With Zoom, you can create memorable moments and stay connected like never before.

 

  
 

https://click.e.zoom.us/?qs=35a6c0657aad576228d4fe95b04529acef8ddc2e288b2b4b0ef90d81399c2163af6ceed3562370c0a041737e83f5ab868c51ad2a053f605d79e1b0e18b920f18 


 Using Zoom for business?
 

 Zoom has you covered! In the professional world, hosting meetings is essential for effective communication and collaboration. 



Whether it’s team meetings, client presentations, or remote workshops, being a meeting host gives you the control and flexibility to drive productive discussions and achieve your goals. Plus, it’s a great opportunity to showcase your leadership and communication skills.


 


  
 

 Discover the power of hosting meetings:
 

Greater Control
Set agendas, manage permissions, and steer meetings.

 

Enhanced Collaboration
Host brainstorming sessions, share screens, and collaborate in real-time.

 

Improved Communication
Ensure everyone has a chance to contribute and be heard.
 

Look Your Best
Built-in features help your audio and video shine with backgrounds, filters, and even touch up your appearance.
